Perfect for dinner, these stuffed peppers are a testament to how versatile and fulfilling vegan cuisine can be, making them a favorite across various cultures and dietary preferences.
author: Vegor
ingredients: 4 large bell peppers, halved and deseeded
1 can black beans, rinsed and drained
steps: Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C).
In a mixing bowl, combine quinoa, black beans, diced tomatoes, cumin, paprika, garlic powder, onion powder, salt, pepper, olive oil, cilantro, and lime juice.
Stuff each bell pepper half with the quinoa mixture.
Place stuffed peppers in a baking dish, cover with foil, and bake for 25 minutes.
Remove foil and bake for an additional 10 minutes, or until peppers are tender.

title: Hearty Vegan Okra and Tomato Stew
description: This Hearty Vegan Okra and Tomato Stew brings the rich flavors and textures of traditional Southern cooking to a health-conscious audience. This stew, vibrant with the freshness of okra and the tanginess of tomatoes, offers a comforting and satisfying meal, suitable for any occasion. The blend of aromatic spices and fresh vegetables will fill your kitchen with an irresistible aroma, inviting everyone to the table. Designed to be both nourishing and flavorful, this stew delivers on all fronts, providing a hearty, vegan option that pays homage to its Southern origins. The simplicity of ingredients, combined with the depth of flavor achieved, makes this dish a must-try for anyone looking to incorporate more plant-based meals into their diet.
author: Vegor
steps: Heat oil in a large pot over medium heat.
Add onion and garlic, cook until softened.
Stir in okra, tomatoes, smoked paprika, and cayenne pepper. Season with salt and pepper.
Pour in vegetable broth and bring to a simmer.
Cook until okra is tender, about 25-30 minutes.

title: Creamy Vegan Polenta
description: Our Creamy Vegan Polenta recipe is a comforting and versatile dish, perfect as a base for various toppings or as a cozy accompaniment to your main courses. This dish combines the simplicity of traditional polenta with the richness of vegan ingredients, offering a dairy-free alternative that doesn’t compromise on creaminess or flavor. It’s made with cornmeal, vegetable broth for depth of flavor, and nutritional yeast and vegan butter for that irresistible creamy texture and umami taste. Served hot, this polenta becomes the ultimate comfort food, while leftovers can be chilled and then grilled or fried for a delightful texture contrast. Whether you’re vegan or simply looking to incorporate more plant-based options into your diet, this Creamy Vegan Polenta is sure to satisfy.
author: Vegor
steps: Bring the vegetable broth to a boil in a large pot.
Gradually whisk in the cornmeal.
Reduce the heat to low and cook, stirring frequently, until the mixture thickens and the cornmeal is tender, about 25-30 minutes.
Stir in the nutritional yeast and vegan butter until well combined. Season with salt to taste.
Serve hot.

title: Vegan Gumbo
description: This Classic Vegan Gumbo recipe is a delightful twist on the traditional Louisiana dish. Meticulously crafted to cater to a vegan diet without compromising on the authentic flavors and essence, this gumbo is packed with a variety of vegetables, vegan sausages, and a perfect blend of spices, simmered in a rich and thick roux-based broth. The recipe ensures that the richness and depth of flavor are maintained, making it a soul-warming dish for any occasion. It’s a testament to how versatile vegan cuisine can be, seamlessly adapting to cultural classics. Complete with okra, bell peppers, onions, and a selection of herbs and spices, this gumbo is a celebration of flavor and texture that promises to delight both vegans and non-vegans alike.
author: Vegor
steps: In a large pot, heat the vegetable oil over medium heat. Add the flour to make a roux, stirring constantly, until it reaches a dark brown color.
Add the chopped onions, bell pepper, celery, and garlic to the roux. Cook until vegetables are soft.
Add the sliced vegan sausages, vegetable broth, diced tomatoes, okra, bay leaves, thyme, cayenne pepper, smoked paprika, salt, and pepper. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 1 hour.
Serve the gumbo hot over cooked rice.

title: Vegan Coleslaw
description: This Classic Vegan Coleslaw retains the nostalgic textures and flavors of the beloved traditional side dish, reimagined for a plant-based audience. By substituting dairy ingredients with vegan alternatives, this recipe offers a fresh and creamy taste that can be enjoyed by everyone. The slaw is a crunchy, tangy, and slightly sweet medley of shredded cabbage and carrots, all tossed in a smooth, homemade vegan mayonnaise-based dressing. Perfect for picnics, BBQs, or as a refreshing side for numerous dishes, this vegan coleslaw promises to be a versatile staple in your culinary repertoire. Its simplicity in preparation without compromising on taste makes it a must-try recipe for both vegans and non-vegans alike.
author: Vegor
steps: In a large bowl, combine the shredded green and purple cabbage with the shredded carrots.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the vegan mayzzarella, apple cider vinegar, Dijon mustard, maple syrup, salt, and pepper until smooth.
Pour the dressing over the cabbage and carrot mixture and toss well to coat.
Refrigerate the coleslaw for at least one hour before serving to allow the flavors to meld together.
Serve chilled as a refreshing side dish.

title: Vegan Tostones
description: Classic Vegan Tostones are a cherished recipe originating from the heart of the Caribbean and Latin American kitchens, where green plantains are the star of the show. This recipe transforms the starchy fruit into crispy, golden delights, making it a perfect vegan snack or appetizer. The process begins with slicing the green plantains, then frying them lightly, flattening, and frying again until they reach perfection. Served with a side of vegan garlic sauce or a sprinkle of sea salt, these tostones offer a crunchy outside with a tender inside. This dish embraces the simplicity and flavors of its origin, providing a delightful experience for those seeking to explore vegan variants of traditional Caribbean cuisine.
author: Vegor
ingredients: 2 cups of vegetable oil for frying
steps: Peel the green plantains and cut them into 1-inch thick slices.
Heat the oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Fry the plantain slices for about 3 minutes on each side, or until they are light golden but not brown.
Remove the plantains from the oil and flatten them using a tostonera or the bottom of a glass jar.
Return the flattened plantains to the hot oil and fry again for about 2 minutes on each side, or until they are crispy and golden brown.
Drain the tostones on paper towels and season with salt while still hot.

title: Tropical Coconut Ceviche
description: Tropical Coconut Ceviche offers a sublime blend of fresh, zesty, and creamy flavors, making it a quintessential dish for those who adore the vibrant tastes of the tropics. This vegan version eschews traditional seafood ingredients, utilizing instead the meat of young coconuts to mimic the delicate texture of fish, while a medley of bell peppers, red onions, and cilantro adds crunch and color. The lime juice not only infuses the dish with its characteristic tang but also acts as the marinade that ‘cooks’ the coconut. Avocado adds a creamy dimension, rounding out the dish’s complex flavor profile. Ideal as a refreshing starter or a light main, this ceviche embraces the essence of summer dining. With no cooking required, it’s a breeze to prepare, making it perfect for impromptu gatherings or a quick, healthful meal.
author: Vegor
steps: In a large bowl, combine the coconut meat, red onion, bell peppers, and cilantro.
Add the lime juice, salt, and chili flakes if using, and mix well to ensure the coconut meat is well coated.
Let the mixture marinate for at least 10 minutes to allow the flavors to meld.
Just before serving, gently fold in the diced avocado.
Serve chilled, garnished with additional cilantro if desired.

title: Vegan Tomato and Basil Bruschetta
description: This Classic Vegan Tomato and Basil Bruschetta embodies the essence of Italian cuisine, focusing on simplicity, freshness, and quality ingredients. This recipe is a testament to how a few components, when combined with skill and passion, can create a dish that’s both unassuming and profoundly flavorful. At the heart of this appetizer is the ripe tomato, its juicy sweetness complemented by the aromatic basil and the robustness of garlic, all sitting atop a toasted slice of crusty bread. The quality of the olive oil adds a velvety texture and a hint of fruitiness, rounding off the flavors magnificently. This bruschetta is not only a delightful starter to any meal but also serves as a bright, refreshing snack or side dish, versatile in its simplicity yet unmatched in its ability to impress.
author: Vegor
ingredients: 1 loaf crusty bread, sliced and toasted
steps: 1. In a mixing bowl, combine the chopped tomatoes, basil, garlic, olive oil, and balsamic vinegar. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
2. Let the mixture sit for 5-10 minutes to allow flavors to meld.
3. Spoon the tomato mixture generously onto each slice of toasted bread.
4. Serve immediately and enjoy the freshness of this classic Italian appetizer.

title: Vegan Spanakopita
description: This Vegan Spanakopita is a cruelty-free take on the classic Greek pastry. Packed with a flavorful spinach and tofu feta mixture, nestled between layers of crispy, golden phyllo dough, it promises a delightful eating experience. This recipe reinvents traditional spanakopita using plant-based ingredients without compromising taste or texture. Whether you are hosting a dinner party or looking for a comforting meal, this vegan version of the beloved spinach pie will satisfy your cravings while adhering to a vegan lifestyle. Carefully selected ingredients ensure that every bite is as authentic as its traditional counterpart, making it a must-try for vegans and non-vegans alike.
author: Vegor
steps: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C).
Heat a skillet over medium heat, add olive oil and sauté onion and garlic until soft.
Add spinach and cook until wilted. Remove from heat.
In a bowl, combine crumbled tofu, nutritional yeast, lemon juice, salt, pepper, nutmeg, and dill. Add the cooked spinach mixture and mix well.
Unroll phyllo dough and cover with a damp cloth to prevent drying out. Take one sheet, brush with olive oil, and place in the baking dish. Repeat with half the phyllo sheets.
Bake for 40-45 minutes, or until golden and crispy.
Let cool for a few minutes before serving.

title: Vegan Schnitzel
description: This Classic Vegan Schnitzel recipe captures the essence and nostalgia of traditional schnitzels while using entirely plant-based ingredients. The schnitzels are made from a satisfying blend of chickpeas and vital wheat gluten, delivering a texture that’s both tender and chewy, reminiscent of the classic dish. Seasonings like nutritional yeast, garlic powder, and smoked paprika give these vegan schnitzels a depth of flavor that is incredibly satisfying. Breaded with a mix of breadcrumbs and spices, and fried to golden perfection, this dish offers a delightful crunch with every bite. Serving this alongside a fresh lemon wedge, creamy mashed potatoes, or your choice of vegan gravy can elevate this dish to a new level of comfort food. Whether you’re a long-time vegan or just exploring meat-free options, this schnitzel recipe promises a delicious and satisfying meal experience.
author: Vegor
steps: 1. In a food processor, blend chickpeas until smooth.
2. In a large bowl, combine chickpea paste, vital wheat gluten, nutritional yeast, garlic powder, smoked paprika, salt, and pepper. Mix well.
3. Gradually add vegetable broth and soy sauce to the mixture, kneading until a dough forms.
4. Divide the dough into 4 equal portions. Flatten each portion into a schnitzel shape.
5. Prepare two shallow dishes: one with plant-based milk and another with breadcrumbs.
7. Heat oil in a frying pan over medium heat. Fry schnitzels for about 5 minutes on each side, until golden brown.
8. Serve hot with lemon wedges on the side.

title: Vegan Ravioli
description: This recipe for Classic Vegan Ravioli is a testament to the beauty and depth of Italian cuisine, adapted for a modern, plant-based lifestyle. Crafting this dish involves preparing a tender, homemade pasta dough, then stuffing it with a rich, flavorful filling of herbed tofu ricotta. The ravioli are then cooked to perfection and can be served with your favorite vegan sauce, offering a sumptuous, hearty meal that’s sure to delight. Whether you’re a seasoned vegan or simply looking to expand your culinary repertoire, this recipe is an outstanding choice, providing a delightful exploration into the versatility of vegan cooking.
author: Vegor
ingredients: Your choice of vegan sauce for serving
steps: Mix the flour, water, olive oil, and turmeric in a bowl to form a dough. Knead until smooth, then let it rest for 30 minutes.
For the filling, blend tofu, nutritional yeast, lemon juice, basil, oregano, salt, and pepper until smooth.
Roll out the dough on a floured surface. Cut circles, place a teaspoon of filling on each, fold, and press edges to seal.
Boil ravioli in salted water for about 4 minutes or until they float. Serve with your preferred vegan sauce.

title: Vegan Lamingtons
description: The beloved Australian dessert, Lamingtons, gets a delightful vegan makeover in this recipe. These Classic Vegan Lamingtons preserve the cherished qualities of the traditional treat—a soft, spongey cake dipped in a rich chocolate glaze and rolled in desiccated coconut—while ensuring every component is entirely plant-based. Crafted with care, these lamingtons offer a moist, delicate crumb, thanks to the innovative use of vegan alternatives for common dairy ingredients. Each bite envelops the taste buds in a luxurious blend of chocolate and coconut, making for a dessert that’s as satisfying as it is compassionate. Ideal for gatherings, celebrations, or as a comforting treat, these lamingtons aim to please a diverse range of palates, vegan and otherwise.
author: Vegor
ingredients: For the chocolate glaze: 2 cups powdered sugar, sifted
steps: 1. Preheat oven to 350°F (180°C) and line a square baking pan with parchment paper.
2. In a large bowl, whisk together the flour, sugar, baking soda, and salt.
3. In another bowl, mix the non-dairy milk, vegetable oil, apple cider vinegar, and vanilla.
4. Combine wet and dry ingredients until smooth.
5. Pour batter into prepared pan and bake for 25-30 minutes or until a toothpick inserted comes out clean.
7. For the glaze, whisk together powdered sugar, cocoa powder, water, and vanilla in a bowl until smooth.
8. Dip each cake square into the chocolate glaze, ensuring all sides are covered, then roll in desiccated coconut.
9. Place on a wire rack to set before serving.

title: Vegan Königsberger Klopse
description: The Classic Vegan Königsberger Klopse is a delightful plant-based reinterpretation of the traditional German dish, typically made with meatballs in a white sauce with capers. This vegan version swaps out the meat for a blend of mushrooms, chickpeas, and breadcrumbs, creating a rich and satisfying texture. The sauce is a creamy concoction of vegetable broth, non-dairy milk, and capers, offering the perfect balance of tanginess and smoothness. This dish is not only a nod to German culinary tradition but also caters to modern dietary preferences, making it a fantastic option for a family dinner or a special occasion.
author: Vegor
steps: Mash chickpeas in a large bowl until mostly smooth.
Mix in mushrooms, breadcrumbs, soy sauce, smoked paprika, salt, and pepper. Form into meatballs.
Heat olive oil in a pan over medium heat and cook meatballs until browned on all sides. Remove and set aside.
In the same pan, add onion and cook until soft. Stir in flour to make a roux.
Gradually whisk in vegetable broth, then non-dairy milk to form a smooth sauce.
Serve garnished with fresh parsley.

title: Vegan Injera
description: Injera, a traditional Ethiopian flatbread, is essential to Ethiopian cuisine and is now embraced by the vegan community worldwide. This Classic Vegan Injera adapts the ancient recipe by ensuring all ingredients are plant-based, maintaining the dish’s integrity and cultural significance. The recipe captures the unique, spongy texture and slight tanginess of injera, which is the result of fermentation. Preparing this dish involves mixing teff flour, a gluten-free ancient grain native to Ethiopia, with water and allowing the mixture to ferment. The fermentation process, which takes about 48 hours, is crucial for developing the bread’s characteristic flavor and texture. Once fermented, the batter is cooked like a pancake, but only on one side, allowing bubbles to form and create its distinctive porous appearance. This vegan version of injera remains true to its traditional roots while offering a delicious and inclusive option for those following a plant-based diet.
author: Vegor
steps: In a large bowl, mix teff flour with water until smooth. Cover with a kitchen towel and let it ferment in a warm place for 48 hours.
After fermentation, stir in the salt. Heat a non-stick skillet over medium heat.
Pour 1/2 cup of batter into the skillet, tilting to spread the batter into a thin, even layer.
Cook until bubbles form and the edges lift from the pan (do not flip), about 2-3 minutes.
Transfer to a plate and repeat with the remaining batter.

title: Vegan Dal Tadka
description: This Classic Vegan Dal Tadka recipe is an authentic Indian dish that promises a symphony of flavors with every spoonful. Prepared with an assortment of nutritious lentils and seasoned with a fragrant mix of spices, this comforting dish is both nourishing and satisfying. A traditional tadka, or tempering technique, infuses the dal with a rich aroma and a deep layer of flavor, making it a beloved staple in Indian cuisine. Its simplicity, combined with the profound depth of taste, allows it to stand out. Perfect for any day of the week, this recipe brings the essence of Indian culinary traditions right into your kitchen.
author: Vegor
ingredients: 1 cup mixed lentils (such as toor dal, masoor dal, and yellow moong dal), rinsed
steps: In a large pot, combine lentils, water, turmeric, and salt. Bring to a boil, then simmer until lentils are soft, about 20-30 minutes.
In a separate pan, heat oil over medium heat. Add cumin seeds and allow them to splutter.
Add onions and cook until golden. Stir in ginger garlic paste, green chilies, and tomatoes. Cook until tomatoes are soft.
Add coriander powder, garam masala, and red chili powder to the tomato mixture. Cook for another 2 minutes.
Pour the tomato mixture into the cooked lentils. Stir well to combine. Cook for another 5-10 minutes.

title: Vegan Choripán
description: The Classic Vegan Choripán takes the traditional South American street food experience and reimagines it for the plant-based diet. This recipe brilliantly captures the essence of its meat-based counterpart, utilizing vegan sausages to recreate the characteristic smoky flavor and satisfying texture significant to Choripán. Complemented by a homemade chimichurri sauce that bursts with freshness, tang, and a hint of spice, this sandwich is nestled within a crusty bread roll, evoking the perfect blend of textures and flavors in every bite. The dish is not only a nod to its Argentinian roots but also a testament to the versatility and richness of vegan cuisine. It’s a meal that promises to bring the vibrant streets of South America into your kitchen, delivering an unforgettable culinary experience that’s both nourishing and delightfully flavorful.
author: Vegor
ingredients: 1 long baguette or 4 crusty bread rolls
steps: Preheat grill or pan over medium heat.
Cook vegan sausages according to package instructions until browned and heated through.
While sausages are cooking, prepare the chimichurri sauce by combining parsley, garlic, olive oil, vinegar, chili flakes, salt, and pepper in a bowl. Stir well and set aside.
Slice the baguette or bread rolls in half and lightly toast them.
Place a cooked sausage in each bread roll, generously top with chimichurri sauce, and serve immediately.

title: Vegan Bouillon
description: This Classic Vegan Bouillon recipe offers a versatile and health-conscious alternative to traditional meat-based broths. Crafted with a blend of aromatic vegetables, herbs, and spices, this bouillon serves as an excellent base for soups, stews, and sauces, enriching any dish with its robust flavor profile. Designed to meet the dietary needs of vegans and those looking to reduce their meat intake, this recipe emphasizes sustainability and nutrition without compromising on taste. The preparation process is straightforward, allowing both novice and experienced cooks to easily incorporate this essential component into their culinary repertoire. By choosing high-quality, organic ingredients, you ensure that the bouillon not only enhances the taste of your meals but also contributes to a healthy, ethical lifestyle.
author: Vegor
steps: Heat olive oil in a large pot over medium heat. Add carrots, celery, onion, and garlic. Sauté until vegetables are softened, about 5-7 minutes.
Add water, bay leaves, thyme, turmeric, and peppercorns. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer, uncovered, for 45 minutes.
Strain the broth through a fine sieve, pressing on the solids to extract as much liquid as possible. Season with salt to taste.
Let the bouillon cool to room temperature, then transfer to storage containers. It can be refrigerated for up to 1 week or frozen for up to 3 months for future use.

title: Vegan Berliner
description: This Classic Vegan Berliner is a quintessential recipe that captures the essence of traditional German pastry making while adhering to plant-based principles. Crafted with meticulous attention to detail, this vegan version retains all the pillowy softness and delectable sweetness of its conventional counterpart. The dough is enriched with vegan milk and butter, offering a tender crumb, while a generous filling of jam provides a burst of fruitiness in every bite. Coated in a delicate layer of sugar, each Berliner is not just a treat but an experience, echoing the flavors of Germany’s storied bakeries. This culinary creation is perfect for those seeking to indulge in a classic dessert without the use of animal products, ensuring that everyone can partake in the joy of a well-made Berliner.
author: Vegor
steps: 1. In a large mixing bowl, combine the warmed plant-based milk, sugar, and yeast. Allow to sit for 5 minutes until frothy.
2. Add the flour, melted vegan butter, and salt to the yeast mixture. Mix until a soft dough forms.

3. Knead the dough on a lightly floured surface for about 8 minutes, or until smooth and elastic. Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover, and let rise in a warm place for 1 hour, or until doubled in size.

4. Punch down the risen dough and turn it out onto a lightly floured surface. Divide the dough into 8 equal pieces and shape each into a ball. Flatten slightly and spoon a heaping teaspoon of jam into the center of each. Fold the dough over the filling and seal the edges. Form back into a ball shape, ensuring the jam is completely enclosed.
5. Place the filled dough balls on a baking sheet, cover lightly with a towel, and let rise for another 30 minutes.
7. While still warm, roll the fried Berliners in granulated sugar to coat. Let cool slightly before serving.

title: Vegan Bolón de Verde
description: Classic Vegan Bolón de Verde transforms the traditional Ecuadorian dish into a delightful plant-based feast. Originally known for its rich flavors centered around green plantains and cheese or meat, this vegan version maintains the authentic taste profile using a mix of mushrooms and spices to mimic the savory depth. The dish serves not only as a testament to the versatility of vegan cooking but also pays homage to its South American roots, offering a beautiful blend of cultural tradition and modern dietary preferences. Perfect for breakfast, brunch, or even as a unique side dish, this recipe combines mashed green plantains with a sautéed mushroom mixture, culminating in a crispy exterior with a soft, flavorful interior. Enjoy a journey through Ecuador’s culinary landscape, all while adhering to a vegan lifestyle.
author: Vegor
ingredients: 4 green plantains, peeled and cut into chunks
Salt and black pepper to taste
steps: Boil plantain chunks until tender, about 15 minutes. Drain and mash until smooth.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium heat. Add mushrooms, onion, garlic, coriander, and smoked paprika. Cook until mushrooms are soft and browned.
Fold the mushroom mixture into the mashed plantains until well combined. Season with salt and pepper.
Form the mixture into balls or patties. In the same skillet, brown the bolones on each side until crispy.
Serve hot, garnished with fresh cilantro.

title: Vegan Bee Sting Cake
description: The Vegan Bee Sting Cake is a delightful reimagining of the traditional German dessert, Bienenstich, which combines a rich, yeast-based cake with a creamy custard filling and a crunchy, caramelized almond topping. This vegan version maintains the beloved flavors and textures of the original while employing plant-based ingredients to ensure that everyone can enjoy this exquisite dessert. By skillfully replacing dairy and eggs with vegan alternatives, this recipe achieves a light and spongy cake, complemented by a silky almond milk custard and finished with a sweet and nutty topping. The contrast between the soft cake, the creamy filling, and the textured almond crust makes this vegan version a delightful experience, satisfying both traditionalists and vegans alike.
author: Vegor
steps: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C).
In a large bowl, mix flour, sugar, yeast, and salt.
Warm the almond milk and vegan butter until melted, then add to the dry ingredients. Mix until a soft dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes, then let it rise for 1 hour.
Roll out the dough and fit it into a greased cake pan.
Spread custard over the dough.
Mix sliced almonds with maple syrup and sprinkle over the custard.
Bake for 30 minutes or until the top is golden brown.
Allow to cool before serving.

title: Vegan Apfelstrudel
description: The Classic Vegan Apfelstrudel is a testament to the timeless appeal of traditional desserts, reimagined with a compassionate twist. This recipe pays homage to the beloved Austrian pastry, ensuring that its vegan version loses none of the original’s charm or flavor. Crafted with precision and care, the strudel features thinly rolled dough encasing a succulent filling of spiced apples, raisins, and cinnamon, all sweetened to perfection. The dough, made from simple ingredients, is both tender and flaky, offering a delightful contrast to the warm, spiced fruit within. Baked to golden perfection, this dessert is finished with a dusting of powdered sugar, creating a visually stunning and delicious treat. The Classic Vegan Apfelstrudel not only caters to vegan dietary needs but also invites all dessert lovers to partake in its comforting embrace, making it a versatile choice for various occasions.
author: Vegor
ingredients: 4 large apples, peeled and thinly sliced
steps: In a mixing bowl, combine flour, water, and oil to form a dough. Knead until smooth, then let rest for 30 minutes.
Roll the dough on a floured surface into a thin sheet.
Preheat the oven to 375°F (190°C).
Mix apples with sugar, raisins, cinnamon, and walnuts.
Spread the apple mixture over the dough, leaving a small margin at the edges.
Bake for 45 minutes, or until golden.
Dust with powdered sugar before serving.

title: Vegan Ajiaco
description: This Classic Vegan Ajiaco offers a tantalizing twist on the traditional Colombian stew, substituting meat with hearty plant-based ingredients to create a comforting and nutritious meal. By incorporating a blend of potatoes, guascas (a unique Colombian herb), corn, and a rich, flavorful broth, this vegan version maintains the soul-warming essence of the original dish. The stew is thickened naturally through the breakdown of the potatoes, resulting in a creamy consistency without the need for dairy. Served with capers and avocado, this dish not only pays homage to its Colombian roots but also provides a satisfying, wholesome experience for those following a plant-based diet. The Classic Vegan Ajiaco is a testament to the versatility of vegan cooking, proving that traditional recipes can be adapted to meet modern dietary preferences while retaining their cultural significance and taste.
author: Vegor
ingredients: 2 ears of corn, cut into thirds
steps: In a large pot, combine the sliced potatoes, yellow potatoes, guascas, and vegetable broth. Bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to medium-low and simmer until the potatoes are tender, about 30 minutes.
Add the corn pieces to the pot and continue to simmer until the corn is cooked through, about 15 to 20 minutes.
Adjust seasoning with salt and pepper.
Serve hot, garnished with capers and slices of avocado.

title: Egyptian Koshari
description: Classic Egyptian Koshari combines lentils, rice, and pasta under a spicy, tomato-based sauce and caramelized onions, offering a comforting bowl of wholesome goodness. This vegan recipe brings together a delightful mix of textures and flavors that are sure to satisfy. Originating from Egypt, Koshari is often considered the national dish, enjoyed by many for its satisfying complex carbohydrates and protein-packed lentils, making it a nutritious option for anyone. The process involves separately cooking the lentils, rice, and pasta, then layering them before topping with a rich, garlicky tomato sauce and golden brown onions. Serving this dish is a celebration of Egyptian street food culture, offering a hearty meal that delights with every spoonful.
author: Vegor
steps: 1. Rinse the lentils and rice separately. Cook the lentils in boiling water until tender but not mushy, about 20 minutes. Drain and set aside.
2. In another pot, cook the rice according to package instructions until it’s fluffy. Set aside.
3. Cook the pasta in boiling salted water until al dente. Drain and rinse under cold water to stop the cooking process. Set aside.

4. In a large skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add the sliced onions and cook, stirring occasionally, until they are caramelized and deeply browned, about 15-20 minutes. Remove half of the onions and set aside for garnishing.
5. To the same skillet with half the onions, add the garlic, tomato puree, cumin, and cayenne pepper. Cook for another 5-7 minutes until the sauce thickens.
7. Garnish with the caramelized onions kept aside and serve immediately.

title: Vegan Aloo Gobi
description: A delightful and aromatic concoction of potatoes (aloo) and cauliflower (gobi), this Classic Vegan Aloo Gobi recipe is a testament to the simplicity and elegance of Indian cuisine. Each vegetable is tenderly cooked to preserve its distinct taste, while being perfectly seasoned with a blend of aromatic spices that bring warmth and depth to the dish. This recipe ensures that the traditional textures and flavors are fully embraced in a vegan-friendly manner, without compromising on the richness that characterizes this beloved dish. Ideal for those seeking to explore the essence of Indian cooking or simply enjoy a wholesome, plant-based meal, this version of Aloo Gobi promises to be a heartwarming addition to any dinner table.
author: Vegor
steps: Heat oil in a large pan and add cumin seeds. Once they start to splutter, add onions and sauté until golden brown.
Add ginger-garlic paste and sauté for a minute before adding turmeric, coriander, and red chili powders, along with salt. Stir well.
Add potatoes to the pan, mix well with the spices, and cook covered on medium heat for about 10 minutes.
Incorporate cauliflower florets and tomatoes, mixing gently to ensure the vegetables are coated with spices. Cover and cook on a low flame until the vegetables are tender.
Garnish with fresh cilantro before serving with warm flatbreads or rice.

title: Vegan Chocolate Curd
description: Experience the lusciousness of silky-smooth Vegan Chocolate Curd, a delightful concoction that marries the richness of chocolate with the simplicity of vegan ingredients. Crafted for those who yearn for a guilt-free indulgence, this curd uses a blend of coconut milk, cocoa powder, and vegan sweeteners to achieve its velvety texture and decadent taste. Perfect as a spread, filling, or a standalone dessert, this recipe caters to a variety of dietary needs without compromising on flavor. Whether you’re a committed vegan or simply exploring alternative dessert options, this chocolate curd promises a satisfying experience.
author: Vegor
steps: In a saucepan, whisk together the coconut milk, cocoa powder, and cornstarch until smooth.
Stir in the maple syrup, vanilla extract, and salt.
Cook over medium heat, stirring constantly, until the mixture thickens and begins to bubble.
Remove from heat and transfer to a bowl to cool.
Once cooled, cover and refrigerate until set, about 2 hours.

title: Hearty Chickpea Spinach Curry
description: This Hearty Chickpea Spinach Curry encapsulates the rich and vibrant flavors of Indian cuisine, bringing a delectable vegan option to your dinner table. Crafted with an array of spices, tender chickpeas, and nutrient-rich spinach, this dish is not just a treat to the taste buds but also to the body. The creamy coconut base intertwined with the aromatic spices renders a perfect balance of flavors, making it a go-to comfort food while adhering to vegan dietary preferences. This curry is ideally served over a bed of steaming basmati rice or with warm naan bread for an immersive culinary experience. Whether you are a vegan or just looking to incorporate more plant-based meals into your diet, this chickpea spinach curry promises satisfaction in every bite.
author: Vegor
steps: 1. Heat the olive oil in a large saucepan over medium heat.
2. Add the onion, garlic, and ginger to the pan and sauté until the onions are translucent.
3. Stir in the cumin, coriander, turmeric, and cayenne pepper, cooking for about 1 minute until fragrant.
4. Add the chickpeas and tomato to the pan, stirring well to combine.
5. Pour in the coconut milk and bring the mixture to a simmer. Simmer for 10 minutes.
7. Season with salt to taste and serve over cooked basmati rice.

title: Vegan Cheesecake
description: This Classic Vegan Cheesecake is a sublime treat for all dessert lovers, boasting a velvety texture and a rich, creamy flavor that defies its dairy-free nature. Expertly blending the natural richness of cashews with the zest of lemon for a subtle tang, this cheesecake offers a sumptuous taste experience that’s both indulgent and refreshing. Set atop a crumbly, homemade graham cracker base, and finished with a luscious fruit compote, it’s a testament to how plant-based ingredients can create a dessert as delicious, if not more so, than its traditional counterpart. Perfect for any occasion, from casual gatherings to festive celebrations, it ensures that everyone can enjoy a slice of sweet bliss without compromise.
author: Vegor
steps: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C).
Combine graham cracker crumbs with melted coconut oil, press into the bottom of a springform pan and bake for 10 minutes. Let cool.
Blend soaked cashews, maple syrup, lemon juice, vanilla, and salt until smooth and creamy.
Pour the filling over the crust and smooth the top with a spatula.
Bake for 50 minutes, or until the cheesecake is set but still slightly jiggly in the center.
Top with mixed berries before serving.

title: Chickpea Salad Sandwich
description: The Classic Chickpea Salad Sandwich recipe strikes the perfect balance between healthfulness and taste, offering an excellent vegan alternative to traditional chicken salad sandwiches. It begins with a base of mashed chickpeas, to which a symphony of flavors and textures are added: crunchy celery, tangy pickles, and spring onions, all bound together with a creamy, dairy-free dressing seasoned to perfection. This mixture is then generously layered between slices of your favorite bread, accompanied by crisp lettuce and juicy tomato slices, creating a sandwich that’s both satisfying and wholesome. Not only is this recipe quick and easy, making it ideal for busy weekdays, but it’s also versatile enough to be customized with additional ingredients such as sunflower seeds for extra crunch or a dash of chili flakes for some heat. With its rich nutritional profile and delightful flavors, the Classic Chickpea Salad Sandwich is set to become a staple in your vegan recipe collection.
author: Vegor
steps: Mash the chickpeas in a bowl using a fork or a potato masher.
Add the chopped celery, diced pickles, vegan mayonnaise, Dijon mustard, garlic powder, salt, and pepper to the mashed chickpeas. Mix well to combine.
Taste the chickpea mixture and adjust the seasonings, if necessary.
Assemble the sandwiches by spreading the chickpea salad on two slices of bread, then topping with lettuce and tomato slices. Cover with the remaining slices of bread.
Cut the sandwiches in half and serve immediately.

title: Ultimate Vegan Blueberry Cheesecake
description: This Ultimate Vegan Blueberry Cheesecake reinvents the classic dessert into a dairy-free dream. It starts with a crunchy base made from almonds and dates, offering a perfect contrast to the creamy filling. The filling, a blend of cashews, coconut cream, and maple syrup, is whipped until smooth and then layered with a vibrant, homemade blueberry sauce for a pop of fresh, fruity flavor. Setting in the fridge, this cheesecake becomes delightfully firm, capturing the essence of its traditional counterpart but without any dairy. Finished with a topping of fresh blueberries, it’s a visually stunning dessert that’s as satisfying to look at as it is to eat. Ideal for a sophisticated dinner party or a casual family gathering, it’s a guilt-free indulgence that will leave everyone asking for seconds.
author: Vegor
steps: Line a springform pan with parchment paper.
Process almonds and dates in a food processor until a sticky dough forms. Press into the bottom of the prepared pan.
Blend soaked cashews, coconut cream, maple syrup, vanilla extract, and sea salt in a blender until smooth. Pour half over the crust.
Simmer 1 cup blueberries, lemon juice, and 1 tablespoon water until berries burst. Mix in chia seeds. Cool slightly and pour over the cheesecake layer.
Top with the remaining cashew mixture. Chill for 4 hours.

title: Vegan Black Forest Cake
description: This Vegan Black Forest Cake is a decadent, luscious dessert that brings a plant-based twist to the classic German delicacy. With layers of moist chocolate cake, sweet cherries, and fluffy coconut whipped cream, this cake is a symphony of textures and flavors. Each component is crafted to ensure the cake remains true to its traditional roots while being entirely vegan. Ideal for celebrations, this cake will impress vegans and non-vegans alike with its rich taste and elegant presentation. Embrace the challenge and create a masterpiece that’s not only a treat for the taste buds but also a feast for the eyes.
author: Vegor
steps: Prepare the chocolate cake according to package instructions using vegan substitutes as needed.
Let the cakes cool completely before slicing them horizontally to create layers.
Simmer cherries in a mixture of their juice, sugar, and cornstarch until thickened. Let cool.
Whip the vegan cream with vanilla until stiff peaks form.
Assemble the cake by layering cake, cherries, and whipped cream. Repeat.

title: Aloo Gobi Masala
description: This Vegan Aloo Gobi Masala is a cornerstone of Indian cuisine, beloved for its aromatic blend of spices, tender potatoes, and hearty cauliflower. The dish begins with a base of cumin seeds sputtered in hot oil, into which onions, garlic, and ginger are sautéed until golden. Tomatoes meld with this flavorful base, followed by a carefully measured mix of turmeric, coriander, garam masala, and red chili powder, creating a richly spiced sauce. Florets of cauliflower and chunks of potato are simmered until perfectly tender, absorbing the vibrant flavors. Finished with a sprinkle of fresh cilantro and a squeeze of lemon juice for brightness, this Aloo Gobi Masala is a warming, nutritious meal that pays homage to its Indian roots.
author: Vegor
steps: Heat oil in a large pan over medium heat and add the cumin seeds.
Once the seeds start to splutter, add the onions, garlic, and ginger, sautéing until golden.
Mix in the tomato and cook until soft.
Stir in the turmeric, coriander, garam masala, red chili powder, and salt, cooking for a minute.
Add the cauliflower and potatoes, mixing to coat them with the spices.
Garnish with cilantro and serve hot with lemon wedges.

title: Vegan Hummus and Veggie Sandwich
description: Crafted for the ultimate quick and nutritious meal, the Classic Vegan Hummus and Veggie Sandwich combines the creamy, rich flavors of homemade hummus with the crisp freshness of garden vegetables, all nestled between slices of artisan bread. This sandwich offers not only a burst of flavors but also a variety of textures, from the smooth hummus to the crunchy vegetables. Ideal for a hearty lunch, it ensures a fulfilling meal without the need for cooking, making it perfect for busy days. Originating from a blend of Mediterranean and Middle Eastern influences, this sandwich pays homage to the traditional use of hummus while presenting it in an accessible, modern format. The inclusion of diverse vegetables not only adds to the nutritional value but also allows for customization based on personal preference, ensuring each sandwich is unique.
author: Vegor
steps: Spread a generous amount of hummus on two slices of bread.
Layer the cucumber, carrot, red bell pepper, avocado, and spinach leaves on top of the hummus.
Season with salt and pepper according to taste.
Top with the remaining slices of bread, press down gently, and serve.

title: Ultimate Vegan Peanut Butter Banana Smoothie
description: This Ultimate Vegan Peanut Butter Banana Smoothie is the perfect nourishing start to your day or a delicious pick-me-up during a busy afternoon. Crafted with creamy bananas, indulgent peanut butter, and a dash of cinnamon for depth, this smoothie is not only satisfying but also packs a nutritional punch. Its velvety texture and the harmonious balance of sweet and nutty flavors make it a favorite among both adults and children. Ideal for vegans and those following a dairy-free lifestyle, this recipe promises to deliver taste and energy without compromising on health. Whether you’re looking for a quick breakfast, a post-workout rejuvenator, or a sweet treat that doesn’t guilt, this smoothie is versatile enough to meet all those needs with ease and elegance.
author: Vegor
steps: Peel and slice the bananas.
Combine bananas, peanut butter, cinnamon, almond milk, and ice cubes (if using) in a blender.
Blend on high until smooth and creamy.
Pour into glasses and sprinkle with chia seeds if desired. Serve immediately.

title: Mediterranean Veggie Wrap
description: Inspired by the vibrant flavors and wholesome ingredients of the Mediterranean diet, this Mediterranean Veggie Wrap is not only a testament to the health benefits of plant-based eating but also a celebration of flavor and texture. Packed with crisp vegetables, creamy hummus, and tangy olives wrapped in a soft, whole grain tortilla, this recipe is designed for anyone seeking a quick, nutritious, and delicious meal option. The combination of ingredients not only provides a variety of essential nutrients and fiber but also ensures each bite is bursting with flavor. Perfect for a quick lunch, a light dinner, or a healthy snack, this wrap is sure to satisfy both vegans and non-vegans alike.
author: Vegor
steps: Spread hummus evenly over the center of each tortilla.
Top with mixed greens, cucumbers, red onions, cherry tomatoes, olives, and parsley.
Fold the bottom edge of the tortilla up over the filling, then fold in the sides and roll tightly.
Serve immediately or wrap in foil to enjoy on the go.

title: Vegan Burrito Bowl
description: This Classic Vegan Burrito Bowl captures the essence of Mexican cuisine while offering a hearty, nourishing meal suitable for any time of the day. It begins with a base of fluffy, seasoned rice, layered with black beans simmered in a rich blend of spices, and is topped with a vibrant mix of grilled vegetables, including bell peppers and onions. The dish is then finished with a generous drizzle of creamy avocado sauce, adding a refreshing contrast to the warm, spicy flavors of the bowl. Not only is this recipe a testament to the versatility of vegan cooking, but it also offers a perfect balance of proteins, fibers, and fats, making it a well-rounded meal. Whether you’re a long-time vegan or just looking to incorporate more plant-based meals into your diet, this burrito bowl promises to satisfy your cravings without compromise.
author: Vegor
ingredients: 1 can black beans, drained and rinsed
steps: Cook rice with water as per package instructions.
In a pan, heat beans with cumin, chili powder, garlic powder, onion powder, and salt.
Grill bell pepper and onion with olive oil until soft.
Blend avocado, cilantro, lime juice, and garlic for the sauce.
Assemble the bowl with rice, beans, vegetables, and drizzle with avocado sauce.

title: Vegan Tofu Scramble
description: The Classic Vegan Tofu Scramble recipe stands as a quintessential breakfast delight for those seeking a plant-based alternative to traditional scrambled eggs. This dish masterfully combines the rich, savory palette of spices with the natural, subtle flavors of tofu, creating a texture and taste that appeals to vegans and non-vegans alike. Turmeric gives the scramble its characteristic yellow hue, mimicking the appearance of eggs, while nutritional yeast adds a depth of umami and a cheese-like flavor, enhancing its overall gastronomic appeal. Bell peppers and onions introduce a satisfying crunch and sweetness, respectively, rounding off the dish’s complex flavor profile. This recipe not only caters to the nutritional needs of a vegan diet but also serves as an excellent introduction to tofu for those curious about plant-based proteins.
author: Vegor
steps: Press tofu to remove excess water and crumble into a bowl.
Add nutritional yeast, turmeric, paprika, and black salt to the crumbled tofu. Mix well.
Heat olive oil in a pan over medium heat. Add onion and bell pepper, sautéing until soft.
Add tofu mixture to the pan. Cook, stirring frequently, for about 5-7 minutes or until heated through.
Season with salt and pepper to taste. Serve warm.

title: Moroccan Spiced Rice with Vegetables
description: This Moroccan Spiced Rice with Vegetables recipe brings the vibrant flavors of Morocco to your table with ease and sophistication. Each spoonful is a journey through the rich tapestry of Moroccan cuisine, characterized by its bold spices and hearty vegetable mix. This dish not only promises a delicious vegan meal but also a healthful option that doesn’t compromise on taste. It is meticulously crafted with a blend of aromatic spices, such as cumin, coriander, and cinnamon, which perfectly complement the assortment of vegetables. The rice acts as the perfect canvas to marry the flavors together for a truly memorable dining experience. Suitable for any occasion, this dish is designed to impress with its bold flavors and colorful presentation, making it a perfect choice for both family dinners and special gatherings. It pays homage to the traditional Moroccan culinary arts while offering a modern, health-conscious twist.
author: Vegor
steps: Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add the onion and garlic, and sauté until softened.
Stir in the cumin, coriander, cinnamon, and ginger, cooking for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the basmati rice, stirring to coat the grains with the spice mixture. Pour in the vegetable broth and bring to a boil. Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 10 minutes.

Add the carrots, zucchini, peas, and raisins to the skillet. Season with salt and pepper, then continue to cook, covered, for another 20 minutes, or until the rice is tender and the liquid is absorbed.
Fluff the rice with a fork, adjust seasoning if necessary, and garnish with fresh cilantro before serving.

title: Vegan Pasta Primavera
description: This Classic Vegan Pasta Primavera is a tribute to the freshness and bounty of spring vegetables, encompassing the essence of plant-based simplicity without sacrificing flavor. Carefully crafted with a selection of crisp, vibrant vegetables such as bell peppers, broccoli, and asparagus, this dish is sautéed to perfection and then tossed with freshly cooked pasta. The inclusion of aromatic herbs and a light, zesty lemon sauce elevates the natural flavors of the vegetables, ensuring a balanced and deeply satisfying dining experience. Whether you’re a long-time vegan or simply exploring plant-based dining, this Pasta Primavera promises a delightful and nutritious meal that honors the season’s freshest produce.
author: Vegor
steps: Cook pasta according to package instructions. Drain and set aside.
In a large pan, heat olive oil over medium heat. Add garlic and sauté for 1 minute.
Add bell peppers, broccoli, asparagus, carrot, peas, and zucchini to the pan. Sauté for 5-7 minutes until vegetables are tender but still crisp.
Season with salt, pepper, and Italian herbs. Add cooked pasta to the pan.
Toss everything together with lemon juice and zest. Cook for an additional 2 minutes.

title: Vegan Stuffed Grape Leaves
description: Our Classic Vegan Stuffed Grape Leaves are a meticulous combination of tender grape leaves wrapped around a flavorful blend of rice, fresh herbs, and spices. This dish, rooted in Mediterranean tradition, offers a plant-based twist to an age-old recipe, ensuring that everyone can enjoy its rich, savory notes without compromise. Each grape leaf is carefully rolled to encase the aromatic filling, offering a delightful texture contrast that is both satisfying and light. Cooked slowly to perfection, these stuffed grape leaves emerge from the kitchen fragrant, with a tender bite, ready to be savored as an appetizer, side, or light meal. Whether served on their own, with a side of tangy lemon sauce, or alongside other Mediterranean favorites, they promise a culinary journey that respects tradition while embracing modern dietary preferences.
author: Vegor
ingredients: 1 jar grape leaves in brine (about 50 leaves)
Salt and black pepper to taste
steps: Rinse the grape leaves in cold water and set aside.
In a bowl, mix cooked rice with olive oil, onions, dill, parsley, dried mint, and half the lemon juice. Season with salt and pepper.
Take a grape leaf, place it shiny side down, and add a small amount of the rice mixture at the base. Fold in the sides and roll tightly.
Place the stuffed leaves seam side down in a pot.
Once all are in the pot, pour over the remaining lemon juice and enough water to cover. Place a plate on top to hold them down.
Allow to cool in the pot before serving.
Serve with extra lemon slices.

title: Moroccan Lentil Salad
description: This Moroccan Lentil Salad embodies the vibrant flavors and rich textures of Moroccan cuisine. Prepared with meticulously cooked lentils, fresh vegetables, and a harmonious blend of spices, this salad offers a fulfilling and nutritious meal option. The inclusion of herbs like cilantro and mint, coupled with the tanginess of lemon juice, provides a refreshing undertone to the dish. Topped with crunchy nuts for an added texture, this salad not only caters to the palate but also to the body’s nutritional needs, offering a high fiber, protein-packed vegan delight. Each ingredient is carefully selected to complement each other, ensuring a balance of flavors that is both invigorating and satisfying.
author: Vegor
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
1/4 cup chopped nuts (almonds or walnuts, optional)
steps: Rinse the lentils and cook in boiling water until tender but firm, about 20-30 minutes. Drain and let cool.
In a large bowl, combine the cooked lentils, diced tomato, cucumber, red onion, cilantro, and mint.
In a small bowl, whisk together olive oil, lemon juice, minced garlic, cumin, coriander, cayenne pepper, salt, and pepper.
Pour the dressing over the lentil mixture and toss to coat evenly.
Garnish with chopped nuts if using, and serve chilled or at room temperature.

title: Moroccan Mint Tea with Dates
description: Moroccan Mint Tea with Dates is a traditional and ceremonial beverage cherished across Morocco and beyond. This vegan recipe captures the essence of hospitality and warmth, quintessential to Moroccan culture. It combines the refreshing zest of mint, sweetness from dates, and the subtlety of green tea, creating a harmonious blend that is both invigorating and soothing. The process involves brewing loose-leaf green tea with generous handfuls of fresh mint, and the natural sweetness is enhanced with dates, serving not just as a sweetener but also as a delightful snack. This recipe is a testament to simple ingredients yielding exquisite flavors, making it perfect for any time of the day.
author: Vegor
steps: Rinse the green tea leaves with cold water and drain.
Place the green tea in a teapot, add fresh mint leaves and pour in boiling water.
Let the tea steep for 5-7 minutes, depending on desired strength.
Serve the tea in glasses, adding a date or two to each glass for natural sweetness.
Optionally, sweeten further to taste with your preferred sweetener.

title: Artichoke and Pea Tagine
description: Embark on a culinary journey with this Artichoke and Pea Tagine, a quintessential dish that encapsulates the essence of North African cuisine. This vegan masterpiece combines the tender, earthy artichokes and sweet green peas in a beautifully spiced tomato-based sauce, slow-cooked to perfection. Infused with a rich palette of spices, including cumin, coriander, and a hint of cinnamon, each bite offers a symphony of flavors. Served atop fluffy couscous or with a side of warm, crusty bread, this tagine promises not only a nourishing meal but an unforgettable dining experience.
author: Vegor
steps: Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add garlic and onion, cooking until softened.
Stir in cumin, coriander, and cinnamon and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add diced tomatoes and vegetable broth; bring to a simmer.
Incorporate artichoke hearts and peas into the skillet, cover, and cook on low heat for 30-35 minutes, until vegetables are tender.
Season with salt and pepper. Garnish with fresh cilantro before serving.

title: Mediterranean Vegan Stuffed Bell Peppers with Couscous
description: This delectable recipe for Mediterranean Vegan Stuffed Bell Peppers with Couscous captures the essence of the Mediterranean diet, focusing on plant-based ingredients, whole grains, and a bounty of flavors. These peppers are filled with a savory mixture of couscous, cherry tomatoes, olives, and aromatic herbs, presenting a symphony of flavors in every bite. Baked to perfection, the peppers become tender, while the couscous mixture remains wonderfully moist yet fluffy. Ideal for a nutritious dinner, this dish is not only pleasing to the palate but also to the eye, making it a perfect centerpiece for any vegan feast.
author: Vegor
ingredients: 4 large bell peppers, halved and seeded
steps: Preheat the oven to 375°F (190°C).
In a medium saucepan, bring the vegetable broth to a boil. Stir in the couscous, cover, and remove from heat. Let it stand for 5 minutes, then fluff with a fork.
In a large bowl, combine the cooked couscous, cherry tomatoes, kalamata olives, red onion, garlic, parsley, olive oil, and lemon zest. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Stuff the halved bell peppers with the couscous mixture, packing them tightly.
Place the stuffed peppers in a baking dish, cover with foil, and bake for 25 minutes. Remove the foil and bake for an additional 10 minutes, or until the peppers are tender.

title: Beetroot and Orange Salad
description: This Beetroot and Orange Salad is a refreshing blend of the sweet, earthy flavor of beetroot and the tangy zest of orange, making it the perfect dish for any season. Balanced with the crunch of walnuts and the creamy texture of avocado slices, this salad offers a satisfying mix of textures and flavors. Dressed in a simple yet flavorful vinaigrette that brings out the natural sweetness of the ingredients, this dish is not only delicious but also visually stunning, with its vibrant colors adding a pop to any dining table. Whether served as a starter, side, or light main, this salad promises to deliver both nutrition and taste, making it a must-try for anyone looking for a wholesome and easy-to-prepare vegan option.
author: Vegor
steps: Wash and peel the beetroots, then thinly slice or grate them.
Peel the oranges and cut into segments, capturing any juice.
Slice the avocado.
In a large bowl, combine beetroot, orange segments, and avocado slices.
In a small bowl, whisk together olive oil, balsamic vinegar, mustard, and any captured orange juice. Season with salt and pepper.
Sprinkle with chopped walnuts before serving.

title: Couscous with Seven Vegetables
description: Couscous with Seven Vegetables is an emblematic dish of Moroccan cuisine, reflecting the rich flavors and vibrant colors of the region. This meticulously crafted vegan version keeps the essence of the traditional recipe while incorporating a delightful array of vegetables such as zucchini, carrots, butternut squash, turnips, bell peppers, tomatoes, and chickpeas. Gently simmered with a blend of aromatic spices like cumin, coriander, and cinnamon, this dish offers a fulfilling and nutritious meal, capturing the essence of Moroccan hospitality and culinary tradition. Perfect for those seeking to experience the authentic taste of North Africa’s vegan cuisine, it is designed to satisfy your palate and nourish your soul.
author: Vegor
steps: 1. Prepare the couscous according to package instructions. Set aside.
2. In a large pot, heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add the diced vegetables and cook until they start to soften, about 10 minutes.
3. Add the spices, salt, and pepper, stirring well to combine.
4. Pour in the vegetable broth and bring the mixture to a boil. Lower the heat, cover, and simmer for about 40 minutes or until the vegetables are tender.
5. Serve the vegetable stew over the fluffy couscous.

title: Moroccan Chickpea and Spinach Soup
description: This Moroccan Chickpea and Spinach Soup is a hearty, nourishing, and flavorsome dish that combines the robust flavors of Morocco with the healthful benefits of chickpeas and spinach. Laden with spices such as cumin, coriander, and turmeric, this soup offers a vibrant tapestry of flavors, enriched with the umami depth of tomato paste and the zesty brightness of lemon juice. A touch of harissa adds a gentle heat, marrying well with the creamy chickpeas and tender spinach. This soup is not only a comforting bowl of warmth but also a tribute to the simplicity of Moroccan cuisine, where each ingredient plays a pivotal role in creating a harmonious and balanced dish. Suitable for a cold winter’s evening or as a nourishing meal year-round, this vegan recipe promises to delight the senses and nourish the body.
author: Vegor
ingredients: 1 can (14 oz) chickpeas, rinsed and drained
steps: Heat olive oil in a large pot over medium heat. Add onion and garlic, cooking until soft.
Stir in cumin, coriander, turmeric, and cinnamon, cooking for 1 minute.
Add tomato paste and harissa, stirring to combine.
Pour in vegetable broth, chickpeas, and diced tomatoes. Bring to a simmer and cook for 20 minutes.
Stir in spinach and lemon juice, cooking until spinach is wilted. Season with salt and pepper.

title: Moroccan Spiced Quinoa
description: This Moroccan Spiced Quinoa recipe is a splendid fusion of nutty quinoa grains with the rich, warm flavors of Moroccan cuisine. It’s meticulously crafted to bring a vegan delight that resonates with the essence of Moroccan spices such as cumin, coriander, and cinnamon, all of which are known for their health benefits and flavor profiles. The inclusion of ingredients like raisins and almonds adds a delightful contrast of textures and sweetness, making this dish not only a pleasure for the palate but also a nutritional powerhouse. Designed for those who appreciate the finesse of spice-infused dishes, this recipe promises to be a wholesome addition to your culinary repertoire, embodying the essence of Moroccan hospitality and warmth.
author: Vegor
steps: In a medium saucepan, combine the rinsed quinoa and water. Bring to a boil, then cover, reduce heat, and simmer for 15 minutes or until water is absorbed.
While the quinoa is cooking, heat olive oil in a separate pan over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and minced garlic, sautéing until translucent.
Stir in the cumin, coriander, and cinnamon, and cook for another minute until fragrant.
Add the cooked quinoa to the spice mixture. Include the raisins and chopped almonds, and stir well to combine. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve the quinoa garnished with fresh cilantro.

title: Spinach and Preserved Lemon Tagine
description: This delightful Spinach and Preserved Lemon Tagine embraces the heart of Moroccan cuisine with its aromatic blend of spices, tangy preserved lemons, and the earthy tones of fresh spinach. A vegan treasure, this tagine is slow-cooked to perfection, allowing each ingredient to release its full spectrum of flavors. Not only does it offer a deeply satisfying taste, but it also provides a nutritious meal, rich in vitamins and minerals. The preserved lemons impart a unique citrusy flavor that’s both bold and refreshing, marrying beautifully with the subtle warmth of spices like cumin and coriander. This dish is a testament to the versatility and richness of vegan cooking, showcasing how simple ingredients can be transformed into a celebration of flavors.
author: Vegor
steps: Heat olive oil in a tagine or large pot over medium heat.
Add onion and garlic, cooking until soft.
Stir in cumin, coriander, and cayenne pepper, cooking for a minute until fragrant.
Add the spinach in batches, allowing each batch to wilt down.
Stir in preserved lemon, salt, and pepper.
Cover and cook on low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Garnish with chopped cilantro before serving.

title: Moroccan Roasted Cauliflower
description: This Moroccan Roasted Cauliflower recipe embodies the vibrant culinary culture of Morocco, using a blend of aromatic spices to transform the humble cauliflower into a delicious, fragrant, and visually stunning dish. The combination of cumin, coriander, turmeric, and paprika lends a warm, earthy flavor that is perfectly balanced by the subtle sweetness of cinnamon. This dish is not only a testament to the rich flavors of Moroccan cuisine but is also a healthy, vegan option that is both satisfying and nourishing. Beyond its flavor, this recipe is a visual delight, with the golden hue of turmeric and the vibrant red of paprika making the cauliflower not just a dish, but a centerpiece.
author: Vegor
steps: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C).
Cut the cauliflower into florets and place in a large bowl.
Add olive oil and all the spices to the bowl. Toss until the cauliflower is well-coated.
Spread the cauliflower in a single layer on a baking sheet.
Roast for 25 minutes or until tender and golden.

title: Vegan Rfissa Bil Khodra
description: Vegan Rfissa Bil Khodra is a delightful twist on the traditional Moroccan dish, Rfissa. Traditionally prepared with chicken and lentils, this vegan version replaces animal products with a colorful medley of vegetables, while still preserving the essence and flavors of the original dish. The dish is built around a base of msimen, Moroccan flatbread, which is then topped with a richly spiced vegetable stew. The spices, notably fenugreek seeds, saffron, and ras el hanout, infuse the vegetables with depth and warmth, creating a hearty and comforting meal. The inclusion of lentils ensures the dish remains a good source of protein, making it not only a feast for the senses but also nourishing for the body. Perfect for a family dinner or a special occasion, Vegan Rfissa Bil Khodra invites you to explore the richness of Moroccan cuisine while adhering to a plant-based diet.
author: Vegor
ingredients: 2 cups of mixed vegetables (carrots, zucchini, bell peppers)
1 bunch of cilantro, chopped for garnish
steps: Prepare the vegetables by cutting them into bite-sized pieces.
In a large pot, heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add the mixed vegetables and sauté until they start to soften.
Add the lentils to the pot along with ras el hanout, turmeric, fenugreek seeds, and saffron. Stir well to coat everything in the spices.
Pour in the vegetable broth and bring the mixture to a boil. Reduce the heat and simmer until the lentils are tender and the vegetables are cooked through, about 40 minutes.
Taste and adjust seasoning with salt and pepper.
To serve, place pieces of msimen in each bowl and ladle the vegetable and lentil stew over the bread. Garnish with chopped cilantro. Serve immediately.

title: Sweet Potato and Date Tagine
description: This Sweet Potato and Date Tagine recipe is a splendid example of the rich and vibrant culinary traditions of North Africa. A dish that is both nourishing and aromatic, it combines the natural sweetness of dates and sweet potatoes with the intricate flavors of Moroccan spices. The slow cooking method allows each ingredient to impart its unique flavor, creating a harmonious blend that is characteristic of a traditional tagine. As a vegan dish, it is meticulously crafted to ensure that no flavor is compromised, providing a hearty and satisfying meal that is perfect for any occasion. This recipe is not only a celebration of vegan cuisine but also an homage to the diverse and historical culinary practices of the region.
author: Vegor
ingredients: 4 medium sweet potatoes, peeled and cut into chunks
1 can (14 oz) chickpeas, rinsed and drained
steps: Heat the olive oil in a large pot over medium heat. Add the onion and garlic, and cook until softened.
Stir in the cinnamon, cumin, and ginger, cooking for another minute until fragrant.
Add the sweet potatoes, dates, diced tomatoes, chickpeas, and vegetable broth to the pot. Season with salt and pepper.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat to low and simmer, covered, for about 60 minutes, or until the sweet potatoes are tender.
Garnish with fresh cilantro before serving.

title: Vegan Salade de Carottes à la Chermoula
description: This Vegan Salade de Carottes à la Chermoula is a vibrant and flavorful North African-inspired dish that brings together the sweet, earthy tones of carrots with the bold, aromatic spices of chermoula. Chermoula, a key component of this salad, is a marinade traditionally used in Algerian, Libyan, Moroccan, and Tunisian cuisines, known for its rich blend of herbs, garlic, lemon, and spices. In this vegan version, the salad is made with freshly grated carrots, which provide a crunchy texture and natural sweetness, perfectly complementing the spicy, herbaceous sauce. The combination of fresh cilantro, parsley, cumin, paprika, and a hint of chili, blended with the tanginess of lemon juice and olive oil, creates a dressing that’s both invigorating and deeply satisfying. This salad is not only a feast for the taste buds but also provides a generous dose of nutrients, making it a wholesome choice for any meal.
author: Vegor
steps: In a large mixing bowl, whisk together olive oil, lemon juice, garlic, cumin, papariak, chili powder, salt, and pepper until well combined.
Stir in the grated carrots, fresh cilantro, and parsley until everything is evenly coated with the dressing.
Adjust the seasoning to taste, then refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to allow the flavors to meld together.
Serve the salad chilled, garnished with additional herbs if desired.

title: Vegan Moroccan Khobz
description: The Classic Vegan Moroccan Khobz is a traditional bread that is deeply embedded in the Moroccan culinary heritage. This vegan version of the iconic Khobz retains all the authentic flavors and textures that have made this bread a staple in Moroccan homes for generations. Made with simple, plant-based ingredients, this bread is crusty on the outside and soft on the inside, with a delightful chewiness that comes from the thorough kneading process. The bread is subtly flavored with the warmth of nigella and sesame seeds, which are often sprinkled on top before baking, adding a lovely texture and depth of flavor. Perfect for scooping up rich stews or enjoying on its own, this bread is a versatile accompaniment to any meal.
author: Vegor
steps: In a large bowl, combine both flours, salt, and sugar.
Make a well in the center and add the olive oil and dry yeast. Gradually pour in warm water and mix until a dough forms.
Knead the dough on a floured surface for about 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a greased bowl, covering it with a damp cloth. Let it rise in a warm place for about 1-2 hours or until doubled in size.
Punch down the dough and form into two round loaves. Sprinkle the tops with nigella and sesame seeds.
Allow the bread to cool before serving.

title: Mediterranean Orange and Olive Salad
description: This delightful Mediterranean Orange and Olive Salad combines the sweet juiciness of oranges with the briny tang of olives to create a refreshing dish that is as colorful as it is flavorful. Perfect for a light lunch or a vibrant side dish, this salad is a celebration of simple ingredients coming together in harmony. The addition of fresh herbs, like parsley or mint, brings a burst of freshness, while a drizzle of olive oil and a sprinkle of black pepper add depth and complexity to the flavors. Each bite is a testament to the Mediterranean’s rich culinary traditions, offering a balance of sweetness, tanginess, and savory notes that is both invigorating and satisfying.
author: Vegor
ingredients: 4 large oranges, peeled and sliced into rounds
Salt and black pepper to taste
steps: In a large bowl, combine the orange slices and green olives.
Sprinkle with chopped parsley.
Drizzle with olive oil and season with salt and pepper to taste.
Gently toss everything together to combine.
Serve immediately or chill in the fridge for 30 minutes before serving for enhanced flavors.

title: Moroccan Chickpea Stew
description: This Classic Moroccan Chickpea Stew combines the rich flavors of chickpeas, tomatoes, and a myriad of spices to create a hearty and comforting dish. The beauty of this stew lies in its simplicity and the depth of flavors achieved through the careful layering of spices such as cumin, coriander, and cinnamon. This vegan masterpiece is not only nurturing for the body but also a feast for the senses, inviting you on a culinary journey to the heart of Moroccan cuisine. Perfect for any day of the year, this stew is a celebration of plant-based ingredients, showcasing the possibilities of vegan cooking in creating deeply satisfying and nutritious meals. Whether you’re a long-time vegan or simply exploring meatless options, this Moroccan Chickpea Stew will undoubtedly become a favored recipe in your culinary repertoire.
author: Vegor
ingredients: 2 cans (15 oz each) chickpeas, rinsed and drained
Salt and black pepper to taste
steps: Heat olive oil in a large pot over medium heat.
Add the onion and garlic, cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Stir in the cumin, coriander, cinnamon, and ginger, cook for 1 minute more.
Add the tomatoes (with juices), chickpeas, and vegetable broth. Bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to simmer and cook uncovered for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Serve with lemon wedges on the side.

title: Vegan Couscous Tfaya
description: Vegan Couscous Tfaya is a Moroccan-inspired dish that beautifully pairs the nuttiness of couscous with the sweetness of caramelized onions and raisins. This delightful dish is often served during special occasions and is a testament to the rich and vibrant flavors of North African cuisine. It has been adapted to a vegan version by substituting traditional ingredients with plant-based alternatives, ensuring that the essence of the dish remains intact while making it accessible to those following a vegan lifestyle. The combination of spices, including cinnamon and ginger, with the added sweetness of raisins and the distinct taste of caramelized onions, offers a unique culinary experience. This dish not only provides a comforting and filling meal but also a nutritious one, as it is packed with fiber and essential nutrients. Vegan Couscous Tfaya is perfect for those looking to explore international flavors in their plant-based diet.
author: Vegor
steps: 1. In a medium saucepan, bring the vegetable broth to a boil. Add couscous, stir once, and cover. Remove from heat and let it stand for 5 minutes. Fluff with a fork.

2. While the couscous is cooking, heat olive oil in a skillet over medium heat. Add the sliced onions and a pinch of salt, cooking until caramelized, about 15-20 minutes. Stir in the raisins, cinnamon, and ginger, cooking for an additional 5 minutes.
3. Combine the couscous with the onion and raisin mixture. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
4. Serve the couscous tfaya garnished with toasted slivered almonds and fresh parsley.

title: Authentic Moroccan Garden Salad
description: This Authentic Moroccan Garden Salad stands as a quintessential dish showcasing the vibrant flavors and colorful bounty of Morocco’s local produce. This no-cook recipe is a testament to the simplicity and freshness inherent in Moroccan cuisine, where the quality of ingredients speaks for itself. A melange of ripe tomatoes, crisp cucumbers, silky green peppers, and piquant red onions are finely chopped to create a refreshing base. The salad is then elevated with a generous dressing of extra virgin olive oil, freshly squeezed lemon juice, and seasoned with a blend of salt, pepper, and traditional Moroccan spices, including cumin, which adds a warm, earthy undertone. This dish is not only a celebration of summer’s finest but also an embodiment of the Moroccan art of salad-making, meant to stimulate the appetite and prepare the palate for the courses to follow.
author: Vegor
steps: In a large mixing bowl, combine the tomatoes, cucumber, green pepper, and red onion.
In a small bowl, whisk together the olive oil, lemon juice, ground cumin, salt, and pepper.
Pour the dressing over the salad ingredients and mix well.
Chill in the refrigerator for at least 30 minutes before serving.
Serve chilled, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.

title: Vegan Moroccan Harcha
description: This Vegan Moroccan Harcha recipe presents a delightful vegan twist to the traditional Moroccan semolina bread, offering both a sumptuous texture and a delectable taste suitable for a variety of dining occasions. Harcha, a much-loved staple in Moroccan cuisine, typically relished at breakfast or tea time, is known for its crispy exterior and soft, buttery interior. This vegan version maintains the beloved textural contrast and flavor profile, utilizing plant-based ingredients to create a dairy-free alternative that does not compromise on the authentic Moroccan experience. The recipe involves a simple mix of fine semolina, baking powder, olive oil, and a touch of plant-based milk, resulting in a bread that’s both crispy and tender. Serve it warm with vegan spreads or as a side to your favorite meals for a touch of Moroccan delight in your vegan diet.
author: Vegor
steps: In a large bowl, mix semolina, baking powder, and salt.
Add olive oil and mix until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
Gradually add warm water and plant-based milk, and knead to form a soft dough.
Divide the dough into 8 equal portions and shape each into a round disc.
Cook the discs on a hot non-stick pan over medium heat until both sides are golden brown.

title: Moroccan Roasted Carrots
description: Embark on a culinary journey to North Africa with our Moroccan Roasted Carrots recipe. This dish sings with the vibrant flavors of Morocco, using a blend of exotic spices such as cumin, coriander, and smoked paprika to deliver a deeply aromatic and savory experience. Each carrot is meticulously roasted to perfection, ensuring a delightful contrast of a caramelized exterior with a tender, sweet interior. This vegan recipe is not only a testament to the richness of Moroccan cuisine but also a celebration of simple ingredients transformed into an exquisite side dish. Ideal for anyone looking to elevate their dining experience, it serves as a delectable introduction to the complexities of Moroccan flavors.
author: Vegor
ingredients: 1 pound carrots, peeled and sliced lengthwise
Freshly ground black pepper to taste
steps: Preheat the oven to 400°F (200°C).
In a large bowl, mix together olive oil, cumin, coriander, paprika, salt, and pepper.
Add the carrots to the bowl and toss until they are fully coated in the spice mixture.
Spread the carrots in a single layer on a baking sheet.
Roast in the preheated oven for 25 minutes, or until the carrots are tender and slightly caramelized.

title: Vegan Maakouda
description: Maakouda, a beloved street food and a staple in Moroccan cuisine, has made its way to the hearts of food aficionados worldwide. This vegan version retains the charm of the traditional Maakouda, with a blend of spices, herbs, and the key ingredient – potatoes, creating a fritter that is both delightfully crispy on the outside and tender on the inside. Perfect for those seeking the comfort of street food with a plant-based twist, these potato fritters are not only satisfying but also versatile, serving as an excellent appetizer, side dish, or snack. Accompanied by a tangy chermoula dip or a simple vegan yogurt sauce, they promise a harmonious blend of flavors. This recipe not only pays homage to its origin but also introduces a delightful vegan treat to kitchens around the globe.
author: Vegor
steps: Boil the potatoes until tender, then drain and mash.
Mix the mashed potatoes with olive oil, cumin, paprika, cilantro, parsley, garlic, salt, and pepper.
Form the mixture into small patties and lightly coat with flour.
Heat oil in a pan and fry the patties until golden brown on both sides.
Serve hot with your choice of vegan dip.

title: Moroccan Lentil Stew
description: This Moroccan Lentil Stew offers a hearty, plant-based dinner option that’s perfect for any night of the week. It melds the rich, aromatic flavors typical of North African cuisine—like cumin, coriander, and cinnamon—with the nutritional powerhouse of lentils and vegetables. Every spoonful brings a comforting warmth, making it a delightful dish during colder months, yet it’s light enough to enjoy year-round. Carrots, tomatoes, and spinach are simmered with lentils in a broth infused with garlic, ginger, and a hint of harissa for a little kick. The result is a colorful, inviting stew that’s not only satisfying but also packed with proteins, vitamins, and minerals. It’s a testament to the fact that vegan meals can be as nutritious as they are flavorful.
author: Vegor
