steps: Heat olive oil in a large pot over medium heat. Add onion and cook until soft, about 5 minutes.
Add garlic, ginger, cumin, coriander, cinnamon, and cayenne. Cook, stirring, for 1 minute.
Stir in lentils, broth, carrots, and tomatoes (with their juice). Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer, covered, until lentils are tender, about 35 minutes.
Add spinach and cook until wilted, about 5 minutes. Season with salt, pepper, and harissa (if using).
Serve hot, garnished with fresh cilantro.

title: Chermoula Marinated Vegetables Medley
description: This Chermoula Marinated Vegetables Medley offers an exquisite North African twist to your vegan culinary experience. Chermoula, a vibrant and herby marinade, is the star of this recipe, infusing a variety of fresh vegetables with an irresistibly tangy, slightly spicy, and deeply aromatic flavor profile. Perfectly suited for a wholesome dinner or a flavorful side dish, this recipe not only promises a palette of rich flavors but also ensures a nutrition-packed meal. The blend of fresh herbs, spices, and robust olive oil in the chermoula paste marries beautifully with the earthy tones of the roasted vegetables, providing a dish that is as nutritious as it is flavorful. Ideal for any season, this medley can be served hot or cold, making it a versatile addition to your vegan recipe repertoire.
author: Vegor
steps: In a large bowl, combine olive oil, cilantro, parsley, garlic, cumin, paprika, cayenne pepper, salt, and lemon juice to make the chermoula marinade.
Add sliced vegetables to the marinade and toss to coat evenly. Allow to marinate for at least 30 minutes.
Preheat the oven to 400°F (200°C).
Spread the marinated vegetables on a baking sheet in a single layer.
Roast in the preheated oven for 30 minutes, or until vegetables are tender and slightly charred.

title: Vegan Moroccan Briouats
description: Vegan Moroccan Briouats are a delightful twist on the traditional Briouat Bil Khodra, offering a mouthwatering experience that blends the essence of Moroccan cuisine with a vegan lifestyle. These delicate pastry triangles are stuffed with a savory mixture of spiced vegetables, including spinach, mushrooms, and bell peppers, all enhanced with the rich flavors of cumin, paprika, and fresh cilantro. The process of preparing these briouats involves gently folding the filling into thin phyllo dough sheets, then either baking or frying until golden and crispy. This vegan version is not only a tribute to the classic Moroccan appetizer but also a celebration of plant-based ingenuity, providing a perfect blend of crunchy textures and aromatic spices that encapsulate the vibrancy of Morocco’s culinary heritage.
author: Vegor
steps: Preheat your oven to 375°F (190°C).
In a large skillet over medium heat, sauté the spinach, mushrooms, and bell peppers with olive oil until soft.
Add cumin, paprika, salt, and pepper to the vegetable mixture and cook for another 2 minutes before removing from heat. Stir in fresh cilantro.

Carefully separate the phyllo sheets. Place a tablespoon of the vegetable filling on one corner of a phyllo sheet, and fold it diagonally to form a triangle. Continue folding it over itself until you reach the end of the sheet. Seal the edge with a little water or olive oil.
Place the folded briouats on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper. Brush them lightly with olive oil.
Serve hot as an appetizer or snack.

title: Vegan Msemmen
description: Vegan Msemmen, a traditional Moroccan square flatbread, offers a delightful journey into the heart of Moroccan cuisine with a vegan twist. This version retains the authentic textures and layers that Msemmen is known for, utilizing plant-based ingredients to cater to a vegan diet. The process involves creating a smooth, elastic dough, intricately folded with vegan butter and olive oil to achieve its characteristic flaky layers. Skilled cooking on a hot griddle then transforms the dough into golden, crispy-edged bread with a tender interior. Served as a versatile accompaniment, Vegan Msemmen can elevate any meal, from breakfast spreads with jams and spreads to savory dinners, complementing a variety of dishes with its rich, buttery flavor without the use of traditional animal products. This recipe not only pays homage to its Moroccan roots but also embraces modern dietary preferences, showcasing the adaptability of traditional foods to contemporary vegan lifestyles.
author: Vegor
ingredients: Approx. 1 cup water, as needed for dough
steps: Mix both flours, salt, and sugar in a large bowl. Gradically add water, mixing until a soft, elastic dough forms.
Knead the dough on a floured surface for 10-15 minutes until smooth.
Divide the dough into balls, cover, and let them rest for 30 minutes.
Roll each ball into a thin layer, brush with melted vegan butter mixed with olive oil, then fold into a square.
Heat a non-stick pan with a little olive oil over medium heat. Cook each Msemmen for 2-3 minutes per side until golden and crispy.

title: Vegan Batbout – Moroccan Flatbread
description: Vegan Batbout, a Moroccan flatbread, is a marvel of simplicity and taste, blending the traditions of Moroccan cuisine with vegan sensibilities. This recipe replaces traditional dairy and animal products with plant-based ingredients, offering a delightful experience that doesn’t compromise on flavor or texture. Batbout is distinct for its puffed-up middle, which is achieved through a unique cooking method involving a skillet or griddle. This version yields a soft, slightly chewy bread that’s perfect for scooping up sauces or enjoyed as is. Its versatility makes it a favorite for breakfast, lunch, or dinner, embodying the rich culinary heritage of Morocco while catering to a modern, vegan lifestyle. The process involves mixing, kneading, and allowing the dough to rise, followed by shaping and finally, cooking to achieve its signature puff and golden-brown exterior.
author: Vegor
steps: In a large bowl, combine the whole wheat flour, all-purpose flour, sugar, and salt.
In a separate bowl, dissolve the yeast in warm water and let it sit until frothy, about 5 minutes.
Add the yeast mixture and olive oil to the dry ingredients and mix until a sticky dough forms.
Knead the dough on a floured surface for about 10 minutes, until smooth.
Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover with a damp cloth, and let it rise in a warm place for about 1 hour, or until doubled in size.
Heat a skillet or griddle over medium heat. Cook each batbout for 2-3 minutes on each side, until it puffs up and gets golden brown spots.
Serve warm or at room temperature.

title: Vegan B’stilla Bil Khodra
description: This Vegan B’stilla Bil Khodra reimagines the classic Moroccan pie, traditionally filled with pigeon meat, into a lavish feast of vegetables and spices, wrapped in a crispy phyllo pastry crust. The filling combines a rich palette of flavors, featuring butternut squash, chickpeas, spinach, and aromatic spices such as cumin, cinnamon, and coriander, creating a symphony of taste that captures the essence of Moroccan cuisine with a plant-based twist. Served during special occasions or as a sumptuous weekend dinner, this dish is not only a testament to the versatility of vegan cooking but also a celebration of cultural fusion and culinary creativity. Each bite of this Vegan B’stilla offers a crisp exterior followed by a succulent, spiced interior, making it a memorable experience.
author: Vegor
steps: 1. Preheat the oven to 375°F (190°C).
2. In a large pan, heat the olive oil and sauté the onion and garlic until soft.
3. Add the butternut squash and cook until just tender.
4. Stir in the chickpeas, spinach, cumin, cinnamon, coriander, salt, and pepper, and cook for an additional 5 minutes.
5. Off the heat, stir in the parsley and cilantro.
7. Place the vegetable filling in the center and sprinkle with almond slices.
8. Fold the phyllo sheets over the filling, forming a parcel.
9. Place the parcel seam-side down on a baking sheet.
10. Bake for 25-30 minutes, until golden and crisp.

title: Zaalouk
description: Zaalouk is a delectable Moroccan salad that masterfully combines charred eggplants with ripe tomatoes, garlic, and a blend of aromatic spices to create a rich, smoky, and tangy dish. Originating from the vibrant culinary landscape of Morocco, this vegan delight is traditionally served as a side dish but can easily become the star of any meal. The process involves roasting or grilling the eggplants to perfection, ensuring they absorb the medley of flavors from the spices and vegetables they are cooked with. This dish not only offers a burst of Mediterranean flavors but also provides a satisfying and nutritious option for anyone looking to indulge in the richness of Moroccan cuisine without compromising on health.
author: Vegor
steps: Roast or grill the eggplants until the skin is charred and the inside is soft. Allow to cool, then peel and mash.
In a skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add the garlic and spices, cooking until fragrant.
Add the tomatoes to the skillet and simmer until they begin to break down.
Incorporate the mashed eggplant, lemon juice, cilantro, and parsley. Cook for another 10-15 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Adjust seasoning with salt. Serve warm or at room temperature.

title: Vegan Taktouka
description: Taktouka is a vibrant and flavorful Moroccan salad that epitomizes the art of blending roasted peppers and tomatoes with a medley of spices. This vegan rendition of the classic dish respects the traditional culinary practices while ensuring inclusivity for diverse dietary preferences. By roasting the peppers and tomatoes, their natural flavors intensify, yielding a rich and smoky undertone that is then elevated with the addition of garlic, cumin, and a hint of chili for a subtle heat. The salad is typically served either warm or cold, making it a versatile accompaniment to various meals or a delightful stand-alone dish. With each bite, it promises a journey to the heart of Moroccan cuisine, offering a taste that resonates with the soul of Mediterranean cooking.
author: Vegor
ingredients: 2 large bell peppers, roasted and peeled
4 large tomatoes, roasted, peeled, and chopped
steps: Roast the bell peppers and tomatoes until charred. Peel and chop them.
In a skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat and sauté the garlic until fragrant.
Add the roasted peppers, tomatoes, cumin, paprika, and chili powder. Cook for 10-15 minutes on medium-low heat, stirring occasionally.
Season with salt to taste. Garnish with fresh cilantro before serving.

title: Vegan Harira
description: Harira, a traditional Moroccan soup, is celebrated for its rich flavors and hearty ingredients. This vegan version preserves the soul-warming essence of the original recipe while ensuring it’s accessible to those following a plant-based diet. Boasting a blend of tomatoes, lentils, chickpeas, and aromatic spices such as cinnamon, turmeric, and ginger, this soup is a culinary journey to the heart of Moroccan cuisine. Integral to this recipe is the use of fresh herbs and a squeeze of lemon juice added just before serving, enhancing its vibrant taste. Perfect for any season, this version of Harira is not only satisfying but also nutritionally balanced, making it a wonderful addition to your recipe collection.
author: Vegor
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
steps: Heat the olive oil in a large pot over medium-high heat. Add onion and celery, cooking until softened.
Stir in garlic, cinnamon, turmeric, and ginger, cooking for another minute until fragrant.
Add lentils, chickpeas, tomatoes, and vegetable broth. Bring to a boil, then simmer uncovered for 30 minutes.
Stir in cilantro, parsley, and lemon juice. Season with salt and pepper. Serve hot.

title: Vegan Vegetable Tagine
description: The Classic Vegan Vegetable Tagine is a splendid stew that embodies the heart and soul of Moroccan cuisine with a plant-based twist. This recipe carefully balances the rich, earthy flavors of various vegetables and spices to create a harmonious and aromatic dish. The preparation involves a medley of zucchini, bell peppers, carrots, and tomatoes, simmered gently with a blend of traditional Moroccan spices such as cumin, coriander, cinnamon, and turmeric. The use of apricots and raisins adds a subtle sweetness that contrasts beautifully with the savory depth of the spices. Served atop fluffy couscous, this tagine is not only a feast for the taste buds but also a nutritionally balanced meal that captures the essence of vegan cooking. Every spoonful promises a journey through the vibrant souks of Morocco, making it a perfect dish for anyone looking to explore international flavors while adhering to a vegan diet.
author: Vegor
steps: Heat the olive oil in a large pot or tagine over medium heat. Add the onion and garlic, sautéing until soft.
Stir in the cumin, coriander, cinnamon, and turmeric, cooking for another minute until fragrant.
Add the carrots, zucchini, bell pepper, tomatoes, apricots, and raisins. Pour in the vegetable broth and season with salt and pepper.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for about 45 minutes, or until the vegetables are tender.
Serve the vegetable tagine over cooked couscous, garnished with fresh cilantro.

title: Vegan Saskatoon Berry Pie
description: This Vegan Saskatoon Berry Pie brings a slice of Canadian tradition to the vegan table. Laden with juicy Saskatoon berries, this pie doesn’t skimp on flavor, offering a symphony of sweet, tangy, and subtle almond-like tastes distinctive to these berries. Following a vegan diet or catering to dairy-free preferences does not mean having to skip on indulgent desserts, and this pie is a testament to that. Encased in a flaky, golden crust made without any animal products, the filling shines as the star, simmered to perfection, thickened with plant-based ingredients, and enhanced with a touch of lemon for balance. This pie not only satisfies sweet cravings but also serves as a delightful way to enjoy a piece of Canadian heritage in a plant-based format.
author: Vegor
ingredients: 2 1/2 cups all-purpose flour (plus extra for rolling)
steps: In a large bowl, combine flour and salt. Cut in the vegan butter until mixture resembles coarse crumbs. Gradually add water, stirring until mixture forms a ball. Wrap in plastic and refrigerate for 4 hours or overnight.
Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C).
On a lightly floured surface, roll out half of the dough to fit a 9-inch pie plate. Place crust in pie plate. Trim to 1/2 inch beyond the edge of the pie plate.
In a saucepan, combine Saskatoon berries, sugar, cornstarch, lemon juice, vanilla extract, and salt. Cook over medium heat until mixture thickens and boils. Boil and stir 1 minute.
Pour filling into crust. Roll out the remaining dough; make a lattice crust. Trim, seal, and flute edges.

title: Vegan Couscous Bidaoui
description: This Vegan Couscous Bidaoui offers a delightful medley of flavors and textures, bringing the essence of the Moroccan Bidaoui cuisine right to your table without any animal products. The dish is carefully crafted with a rich base of fluffy couscous, topped with a vibrant array of steamed vegetables and infused with a blend of aromatic spices that are sure to tantalize your taste buds. Each ingredient is chosen with care to ensure a harmonious balance of flavors, making this dish not merely a meal but an experience. This recipe not only adheres to vegan standards but also celebrates the beauty of Moroccan culinary traditions, offering a plant-based feast that is both nutritious and satisfying.
author: Vegor
steps: In a large pan, heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add the onion and garlic, and cook until softened.
Stir in the cumin, coriander, and cinnamon, cooking for a minute until fragrant.
Add the vegetable broth and bay leaf. Bring to a boil.
Stir in the couscous, then remove from heat. Cover and let stand for 5 minutes.
In a separate pan, steam the carrots, zucchini, and pumpkin until tender.
Season with salt and pepper to taste. Serve garnished with fresh cilantro.

title: Vegan Butter Tarts
description: Delve into the heartwarming comforts of a classic Canadian dessert, reimagined for those following a vegan lifestyle. Our Classic Vegan Butter Tarts offer a luscious, golden filling cradled within a tender, flaky crust, achieving the perfect balance between sweet and rich. Each bite is a nod to tradition, while celebrating modern dietary choices. This recipe promises to delight with its deeply caramelized undertones and subtle vanilla essence, ensuring each square is a luxurious treat. Ideal for family gatherings, holiday festivities, or as a thoughtful homemade gift, these squares bring a touch of nostalgia and warmth to any occasion. Embrace the indulgence without compromise and allow these vegan butter tart to become a cherished addition to your recipe collection.
author: Vegor
ingredients: 1/2 cup vegan butter, cold and cubed
steps: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C).
In a food processor, pulse together flour, vegan butter, sugar, and salt until crumbly. Press into the bottom of a greased 9×9 inch baking pan.
Bake crust for 15 minutes until lightly golden. Remove from oven.
In a saucepan over medium heat, combine brown sugar, maple syrup, and coconut cream. Bring to a simmer.
Whisk cornstarch with 3 tablespoons of water in a bowl. Stir into the saucepan. Cook until the mixture thickens.
Bake for 25 minutes, or until filling is set. Allow to cool before cutting.

title: Vegan Tarte au Sucre
description: Our Classic Vegan Tarte au Sucre brilliantly captures the spirit and sweetness of the traditional French-Canadian dessert, while catering to a plant-based lifestyle. This delightful tart combines the smooth, rich texture of a vegan brown sugar filling with a soft, homemade pastry crust. Each bite offers a harmonious blend of sweet and subtle notes, perfectly complemented by the light, flaky crust. To achieve the characteristic taste and texture, we utilize plant-based butter and milk alternatives, ensuring that the tart is entirely vegan without compromising on the taste or authenticity of this beloved dessert. This recipe is a tribute to the classic, beloved by both vegans and non-vegans alike, and is sure to be a centerpiece at any gathering.
author: Vegor
steps: In a large bowl, combine flour and salt. Cut in the plant-based butter until mixture resembles coarse crumbs. Gradually add cold water, stirring until dough forms a ball. Wrap in plastic and refrigerate for 30 minutes.
Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Roll out dough on a floured surface to fit a 9-inch tart pan. Place crust in the pan; trim excess dough along the edges.
In a saucepan, mix brown sugar, plant-based milk, and cornstarch. Cook over medium heat, stirring constantly until thickened. Remove from heat; stir in vanilla. Pour filling into crust.
Bake in preheated oven for 25 minutes, or until filling is set. Allow to cool before serving.

title: Vegan Oatcakes
description: These Classic Vegan Oatcakes are a testament to the timeless appeal of wholesome ingredients. Crafted from a simple mixture of oats, water, a pinch of salt, and a dash of oil for binding, they embody the very essence of rustic, plant-based baking. Not only do these oatcakes offer an accessible foray into vegan cuisine, but they also serve as a versatile canvas for a plethora of toppings, ranging from sweet jams to savory spreads. Originating from the British Isles, specifically Scotland, they carry with them a rich cultural heritage that champions simplicity and nutritional goodness. Suitable for breakfast, as a snack, or an accompaniment to soups and salads, these oatcakes are a celebration of the beauty found in minimalism and the undeniable satisfaction that comes from a dish well-made. Each batch yields a delicately crispy texture, inviting a moment of quiet pleasure in every bite.
author: Vegor
steps: Preheat your oven to 350°F (175°C).
In a large mixing bowl, combine the rolled oats, oat flour, and salt.
Add the olive oil and hot water to the dry ingredients, and stir until a firm dough forms.
Roll the dough out on a floured surface until it is about 1/4 inch thick. Cut the dough into circles using a cookie cutter.
Place the oatcakes on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Allow to cool on a wire rack before serving.

title: Vegan Sugar Pie
description: The Classic Vegan Sugar Pie melds the simplicity of its ingredients into a sumptuous dessert that speaks to the soul of comfort food. This recipe, deeply ingrained in tradition, has been ingeniously modified to cater to a vegan lifestyle, ensuring that no one misses out on the joy it brings. The base is a tender, flaky crust that encases a smooth, velvety filling, characterized by its rich caramel flavor derived from the careful caramelization of sugar and the subtle addition of dairy-free cream. This pie, often associated with holidays and family gatherings, transcends its humble origins to become a revered classic, offering a taste of nostalgia with every bite. Ideal for those seeking to indulge in a sweet, fulfilling dessert, this vegan version remains true to its roots while embracing modern dietary preferences.
author: Vegor
steps: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C).
In a saucepan, melt dairy-free butter over medium heat. Stir in brown sugar and flour until smooth.
Gradually whisk in almond milk and bring to a boil. Cook and stir until thickened, about 2 minutes.
Remove from heat and stir in vanilla extract.
Pour filling into the prepared pie crust.
Allow to cool before serving.

title: Vegan Canadian Pancakes with Maple Syrup
description: Indulge in the comforting sweetness of Vegan Canadian Pancakes with Maple Syrup, a delightful breakfast option that promises to start your day on a high note. This recipe transforms the traditional pancake experience into a vegan delight without compromising on taste or texture. Crafted with a blend of simple, plant-based ingredients, these pancakes rise to fluffy perfection, ready to soak up the rich, authentic Canadian maple syrup. Serving as a testament to the versatility of vegan cuisine, this dish not only caters to those following a plant-based lifestyle but also appeals to anyone seeking a delicious, healthier alternative to a classic breakfast staple. Accompanied by the natural sweetness and depth of flavor from pure maple syrup, each bite offers a harmonious balance of fluffy tenderness and sweet indulgence, making it an irresistible choice for any morning or brunch gathering.
author: Vegor
ingredients: Pure Canadian maple syrup for serving
steps: In a large bowl, whisk together flour, sugar, baking powder, and salt.
Add almond milk, vegetable oil, and vanilla extract to the dry ingredients and stir until just combined.
Heat a non-stick pan over medium heat and add a small amount of vegan butter to coat the pan.
Pour 1/4 cup of batter for each pancake onto the hot pan. Cook until bubbles appear on the surface, then flip and cook until golden brown on both sides.
Serve hot with a generous drizzle of pure Canadian maple syrup.

title: Vegan Canadian Maple Fudge
description: Indulge in the essence of Canada with our Vegan Canadian Maple Fudge, a delectable treat that perfectly balances the rich flavors of maple syrup with the creamy, soft texture that fudge lovers adore. Crafted with meticulous care, this recipe uses high-quality, plant-based ingredients to ensure a luxurious taste experience without the use of animal products. Ideal for those following a vegan lifestyle or anyone looking to enjoy a classic dessert with a compassionate twist, this fudge offers a guilt-free indulgence. With its smooth finish and melt-in-your-mouth consistency, our Vegan Canadian Maple Fudge is the perfect ending to any meal or a delightful snack to enjoy any time of the day.
author: Vegor
steps: Line an 8-inch square pan with parchment paper.
In a heavy-bottomed saucepan, combine coconut cream, maple syrup, and coconut oil. Cook over medium heat, stirring constantly.
Bring to a boil and cook until the mixture reaches the soft ball stage (235-240°F on a candy thermometer).
Remove from heat and add vanilla extract and salt.
Beat the mixture with a wooden spoon until thick and creamy.
Cut into squares and serve.

title: Vegan Rappie Pie
description: The Vegan Rappie Pie is a delightful and hearty dish that pays homage to the traditional Acadian meal while ensuring it is entirely plant-based. This recipe swaps out the usual meat for a savory blend of mushrooms and vegetables, maintaining the essence of the original dish. Rappie pie, historically rooted in the Acadian culture found in parts of Canada, is renowned for its unique texture and deeply comforting qualities. Our vegan version keeps faith with these core characteristics, employing grated potatoes to create the dense, gratifying base that defines rappie pie. This dish is meticulously prepared, ensuring that the layers of flavor from the vegetables and seasonings meld harmoniously, encapsulated within the tender but firm potato-based crust. It’s an homage to tradition with a modern, ethical twist, ensuring that both newcomers and long-time aficionados of Acadian cuisine can find joy and satisfaction in each bite.
author: Vegor
steps: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C).
Place grated potatoes in a clean towel and squeeze out as much liquid as possible.
In a large bowl, mix the squeezed potatoes with 2 cups of vegetable broth.
Press this mixture into the bottom and sides of a greased baking dish to form a crust.
Bake for 60 minutes, or until it starts to crisp.
Add mushrooms, carrots, and peas, cooking until they are soft.
Stir in the soy sauce, thyme, salt, and pepper.
Spread this vegetable mixture over the baked crust.
Pour the remaining 2 cups of vegetable broth over the filling.

title: Vegan Montreal-Style Bagels
description: Vegan Montreal-Style Bagels reinvent the classic Montreal bagel into a plant-based delight. This recipe maintains the distinctive sweet and dense qualities of its traditional counterpart, while offering a vegan alternative that doesn’t compromise on texture or taste. The bagels are hand-rolled, boiled in honey-sweetened water—which can be substituted with maple syrup for a vegan option—and then baked to golden perfection. Each bagel is generously coated with sesame seeds, creating a delightful crunch that complements the slightly chewy interior. This version ensures that vegans and those with dairy intolerances can enjoy a beloved staple of Montreal’s culinary scene, without sacrificing authenticity or flavor.
author: Vegor
steps: Dissolve the sugar in warm water and stir in the yeast. Let it stand until frothy, about 10 minutes.
In a large mixing bowl, combine the flour, salt, 1/2 cup maple syrup, and vegetable oil. Add the yeast mixture and stir until a dough forms.

Knead the dough on a floured surface until smooth and elastic, about 10 minutes. Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover with a damp cloth, and let it rise in a warm place until doubled in size, about 1 hour.
Punch down the dough and divide it into 8 pieces. Roll each piece into a rope, then shape into a bagel. Let the shaped bagels rest for 30 minutes.
Preheat the oven to 425°F (220°C). Bring a large pot of water to a boil and add 1/4 cup maple syrup. Boil the bagels, a few at a time, for about 2 minutes per side.

title: Creamy Vegan Wild Rice Soup
description: This Creamy Vegan Wild Rice Soup combines the rich flavors and textures of wild rice, vegetables, and herbs to create a comforting and nutritious meal. The soup is thickened with coconut milk, giving it a creamy texture without the use of dairy, making it perfect for those following a vegan diet. The use of aromatic vegetables and spices like garlic, onions, carrots, and thyme adds depth and complexity to the flavor profile. Wild rice, the star ingredient, not only contributes its unique, nutty flavor but also provides essential nutrients and a hearty texture. This soup is ideal for chilly evenings or as a comforting meal year-round, offering a blend of healthful ingredients and satisfying taste.
author: Vegor
steps: In a large pot, heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add the onion, carrots, and celery, and sauté until the vegetables are tender.
Add the garlic and thyme, cooking for another minute until fragrant.
Stir in the wild rice, vegetable broth, and bring to a boil. Reduce heat to a simmer, cover, and cook for 35-45 minutes, or until the rice is tender.
Stir in the coconut milk, and season with salt and pepper. Heat through.
Serve hot, garnished with chopped parsley.

title: Vegan Caesar Cocktail
description: Indulge in a classic cocktail reimagined for the vegan palate with the Vegan Caesar Cocktail. This innovative drink maintains the bold and savory elements of the traditional Caesar but is crafted with plant-based ingredients to suit vegan dietary preferences. The recipe intelligently substitutes anchovy-packed Worcestershire sauce with a vegan alternative, and uses a blend of spices, tomato juice, and a dash of hot sauce to achieve the signature Caesar taste. Served with an optional celery salt-rimmed glass and garnished with fresh celery, this Vegan Caesar Cocktail is a perfect blend of flavor and tradition, making it a beloved choice for social gatherings, brunches, or a relaxing day at home.
author: Vegor
ingredients: Pinch of celery salt (plus extra for rimming the glass if desired)
steps: In a shaker, combine vegan Worcestershire sauce, tomato juice, lemon juice, hot sauce, ground pepper, and celery salt with ice cubes.
Shake well until the mixture is well chilled.
Rim a glass with celery salt if desired, then strain the cocktail into the glass.
Garnish with a celery stick and serve immediately.

title: Vegan Maple Syrup Pie
description: This Classic Vegan Maple Syrup Pie features a rich, gooey, sweet filling made entirely from plant-based ingredients, cradled in a flaky, buttery crust that’s sure to melt in your mouth. A standout dessert that hails from the culinary traditions of Canada, this pie embraces the natural sweetness of maple syrup, combined thoughtfully with the creamy texture of blended cashews and the robustness of coconut oil. It serves as a heartwarming finale to any meal or a splendid treat with afternoon tea. This recipe is notable not only for its delightful taste but also for being accessible to those adhering to a vegan lifestyle, ensuring no one misses out on the joy of a beautifully crafted pie.
author: Vegor
ingredients: 1 cup raw cashews, soaked overnight and drained
steps: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C).
In a blender, combine soaked cashews, maple syrup, melted coconut oil, vanilla extract, and salt. Blend until completely smooth.
Pour the filling into the prepared pie crust.
Bake for 45 minutes or until the filling is set and the crust is golden brown.
Allow to cool completely before slicing and serving.

title: Vegan BeaverTails
description: Indulge in the quintessential Canadian delight with a vegan twist – Classic Vegan BeaverTails. This beloved pastry, known for its deliciously unique oblong shape, crispy exterior, and soft, chewy interior, is reimagined here without any animal products, making it a perfect treat for anyone following a plant-based diet. Our rendition maintains the cherished characteristics of traditional BeaverTails, including the signature topping of cinnamon and sugar, which caramelizes beautifully upon frying, to offer an irresistible combination of textures and flavors. Crafted with simple, vegan-friendly ingredients, this recipe is designed to replicate the iconic taste and experience of the original, ensuring that vegans and non-vegans alike can enjoy this iconic Canadian pastry any day.
author: Vegor
steps: In a small bowl, mix warm water, yeast, and 1 teaspoon sugar. Let it sit for 10 minutes until frothy.
In a large bowl, mix flour, 1/4 cup granulated sugar, baking powder, salt, and 1 teaspoon cinnamon. Add the yeast mixture and melted vegan butter. Stir until a dough forms.
Knead the dough on a floured surface until smooth, about 5 minutes.
Place in a greased bowl, cover, and let it rise for 1 hour.
Divide the dough into 8 pieces. Roll each piece into an oval shape.
Fry each piece until golden brown, about 1-2 minutes per side.
Mix light brown sugar and 2 teaspoons of cinnamon. Coat the fried pastries in the sugar mixture.
Serve warm.

title: Vegan Bannock
description: The Classic Vegan Bannock brings a delightful twist on a traditional Indigenous bread, ingeniously adapted to meet a plant-based diet without compromising its heartiness or taste. Originating from the Indigenous peoples of North America, bannock has been a staple food for centuries, known for its simplicity, versatility, and satisfying nature. This vegan version maintains these cherished qualities while ensuring it is accessible to those following a vegan lifestyle. Incorporating basic ingredients and an uncomplicated preparation method, it promises a delectably soft and spongy interior encased in a lightly golden crust. Perfect for pairing with a variety of spreads, soups, or enjoying on its own, this recipe is designed to be a comforting and nourishing addition to any meal.
author: Vegor
steps: In a large bowl, mix together the flour, baking powder, and salt.
Add the vegetable oil and mix until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
Gradually add the water and stir until the dough comes together; if it’s too sticky, add a bit more flour.
Knead the dough on a floured surface until smooth.
Form into a round shape and place on a greased baking sheet.
Serve warm.

title: Vegan Maple Glazed Donuts
description: Indulging in a sweet treat doesn’t have to mean compromising on your ethical choices, and our Vegan Maple Glazed Donuts are the perfect testament to this. Crafted with precision and care, these donuts offer a deliciously soft and spongy texture, complemented by a rich and luscious maple glaze that brings a harmony of sweetness with every bite. Designed for vegans and non-vegans alike, this recipe uses plant-based ingredients, ensuring no compromise on flavor or texture. Whether it’s a lazy weekend morning or a special occasion, these donuts are sure to impress, providing a guilt-free indulgence that’s as kind to your palate as it is to the planet.
author: Vegor
steps: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C) and grease a donut pan.
In a large bowl, whisk together the flour, baking powder, and salt.
In another bowl, mix the almond milk, melted coconut oil, maple syrup, apple cider vinegar, and vanilla extract.
Combine wet and dry ingredients until just mixed.
Fill the donut pans evenly with the batter.
For the glaze, whisk together powdered sugar, maple syrup, and almond milk until smooth.
Once donuts are cool, dip each into the glaze and set aside to set.
Serve and enjoy!

title: Vegan Split Pea Soup
description: Embraced for its comforting warmth and wholesome ingredients, Classic Vegan Split Pea Soup is a testament to simplicity and healthful eating, marrying the principles of veganism with the timeless allure of homemade soup. This recipe, rooted in the tradition of hearty winter dishes, uses split peas as its foundation, supplemented by a mélange of aromatic vegetables and spices that gently simmer together, culminating in a rich, creamy texture without the use of any animal products. Its preparation is a journey of culinary alchemy, transforming humble ingredients into a nourishing meal that soothes the soul and satisfies the appetite. Ideal for any season, but particularly comforting during the cooler months, it’s a versatile dish that celebrates the beauty of plant-based cuisine.
author: Vegor
steps: Rinse the split peas under cold water.
In a large pot, heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add the onion, carrots, celery, and garlic. Sauté until the vegetables are soft, about 5 minutes.
Add the rinsed split peas, vegetable broth, bay leaf, and thyme to the pot. Bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 1 hour, or until the peas are soft.
Remove the bay leaf. Use an immersion blender to purée the soup to your desired consistency. Season with salt and pepper to taste.

title: Vegan Tourtière
description: A beloved holiday classic, this Vegan Tourtière recipe captures the essence of a traditional French-Canadian pie with a cruelty-free twist. A tender, flaky crust envelopes a rich, savory filling made from a blend of mushrooms, lentils, and walnuts, seasoned carefully with traditional spices such as clove, cinnamon, and nutmeg. This dish is not only a nod to Canadian heritage but also a testament to how vegan cuisine can beautifully adapt cherished recipes. Perfect for the holidays, or whenever you crave something hearty and warming, this Tourtière will become a staple in your festive culinary repertoire.
author: Vegor
ingredients: 1 tablespoon cornstarch mixed with 2 tablespoons water
steps: Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C).
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium heat. Add onion and garlic, cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add mushrooms, cook until they release their moisture and become brown.
Mix in the lentils and walnuts, then add cinnamon, nutmeg, cloves, salt, and pepper.
Stir in soy sauce and vegetable broth, bring to a simmer.
Pour the filling into the pie crust, then lay the second crust on top. Seal edges and make slits on top.
Bake for 45 minutes, or until the crust is golden brown. Let cool before serving.

title: Vegan Nanaimo Bars
description: A beloved Canadian dessert, the Nanaimo Bar, gets a sumptuous vegan makeover that promises to delight with its irresistible three-layer structure. Beginning with a crumbly, nutty base followed by a smooth, creamy custard-flavored middle, and crowned by a glossy layer of rich, dark chocolate, this no-bake recipe offers a complexity of textures and flavors that is bound to satisfy any palate. Despite its luxurious appearance, it is surprisingly straightforward to prepare, requiring simple pantry staples, making it an excellent option for when you crave something sweet without much fuss. This dessert not only pays homage to its Canadian roots but also invites vegans and non-vegans alike to indulge in its delightful composition.
author: Vegor
steps: Mix melted butter, sugar, and cocoa powder. Stir in graham cracker crumbs, walnuts, and coconut flakes. Press into the bottom of an 8×8 inch pan.
Beat room temp butter, custard powder, powdered sugar, soy milk, and vanilla until light. Spread over the base layer.
Melt chocolate with coconut oil and pour over the custard layer. Refrigerate until set, about 2 hours. Cut into bars and serve.

title: Vegan Butter Tarts
description: The rendition of the classic Canadian butter tart turns a traditionally non-vegan dessert into a delightful vegan treat. This classic vegan butter tart recipe embodies the essence of a cherished dessert without any animal products. By combining a flaky, tender crust with a smoothly caramelized filling, this dessert offers the perfect balance of texture and sweetness, accented by a hint of vanilla and the richness of brown sugar. Suitable for both dedicated vegans and those looking to explore dairy-free alternatives, these butter tarts don’t compromise on flavor. Whether it’s for a special occasion or a quiet moment of indulgence, these tarts are sure to impress.
author: Vegor
steps: 1. Preheat the oven to 375°F (190°C).
2. In a large bowl, combine flour and margarine until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs. Gradually add ice water until dough forms. Chill for 30 minutes.
3. Roll out dough on a floured surface and cut circles big enough to fit into a muffin tin. Press dough into tin forming small tart shells.
4. In a medium saucepan, combine brown sugar, maple syrup, cornstarch, vanilla, and plant-based milk over medium heat until thickened. Stir in raisins.
5. Fill each tart shell with the filling mixture.
7. Let cool before serving.

title: Vegan Poutine
description: This Classic Vegan Poutine is a delightful plant-based spin on a beloved Canadian dish. Traditionally, poutine consists of fries, cheese curds, and gravy, but this vegan version uses creamy, melt-in-your-mouth dairy-free cheese and a rich, savory mushroom gravy that beautifully complements the crispy homemade fries. Each component of the dish is crafted with attention to detail, ensuring that it delivers both on the classic textures and flavors poutine is known for, while being entirely plant-based. This recipe promises a comforting and indulgent experience, making it perfect for those looking to explore vegan versions of international comfort foods.
author: Vegor
ingredients: 4 large russet potatoes, cut into fries
steps: Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C). Toss cut potatoes with olive oil and sea salt, and spread on a baking sheet. Bake for 20-25 minutes until crispy and golden.

While fries are baking, prepare the gravy by sautéing mushrooms in a pan over medium heat until soft. Stir in flour, and gradually add vegetable broth and soy sauce, stirring constantly until the mixture thickens. Season with garlic and onion powder.
Assemble poutine by placing a generous portion of fries in a dish, sprinkle vegan cheese curds over fries, and ladle hot mushroom gravy on top. Serve immediately.

title: Vegan Upma
description: Classic Vegan Upma is a beloved South Indian breakfast dish known for its nourishing and comforting qualities. This version is meticulously crafted to cater to a vegan diet without compromising the dish’s authentic texture and flavors. Starting with a base of semolina or cream of wheat, it’s enriched with a tempered mix of mustard seeds, green chilies, ginger, and fresh curry leaves, providing a perfect balance of heat, zest, and aroma. The addition of vegetables such as carrots, peas, and beans elevates the dish, making it a wholesome meal rich in fiber and nutrients. The upma is finished with a sprinkle of lemon juice and a handful of chopped coriander for a fresh, tangy twist, ensuring each bite is as satisfying as it is delicious. This vegan upma recipe offers a delightful way to start the day with its blend of traditional flavors and healthful ingredients.
author: Vegor
ingredients: 1/2 cup mixed vegetables (carrot, peas, beans), chopped
steps: Heat oil in a pan. Add mustard seeds and let them splutter.
Add cumin seeds, green chilies, ginger, and curry leaves. Sauté for a few seconds until aromatic.
Add onions and sauté until they become translucent.
Add the mixed vegetables and sauté for 2-3 minutes.
Add semolina and roast on medium heat until light golden.
Add lemon juice and coriander. Mix well.
Serve hot garnished with more coriander if desired.

title: Vegan Undhiyu
description: This Vegan Undhiyu recipe is a delightful rendition of the traditional Gujarati winter delicacy, tailored to meet the dietary preferences of vegans without compromising on the authentic taste. Undhiyu, a mixed vegetable casserole, is deeply rooted in the heart of Gujarati cuisine and celebrated for its intricate flavors and delightful variety of textures. Our recipe meticulously substitutes ghee with vegetable oil and skips any animal-derived ingredients to ensure it aligns with a vegan diet. The result is an aromatic, hearty dish comprised of seasonal vegetables, flavorful herbs, and a rich blend of spices that harmonize to bring out the essence of Gujarat. It is an exquisite embodiment of Indian culinary artistry, offering a warming, nutritious meal perfect for any gathering or a cozy night in.
author: Vegor
steps: Prepare the fenugreek dumplings and set aside.
Heat oil in a heavy-bottomed pan, add mustard seeds, cumin seeds, and asafoetida.
Once the seeds start to crackle, add ginger-chili paste and sauté.
Add all the vegetables, powdered spices, and salt. Mix well.
Cover and cook on a low flame till the vegetables are tender.
Garnish with fresh coriander and serve hot.

title: Bharwa Baingan
description: Bharwa Baingan, a quintessential dish revered in the myriad cuisines of India, offers an exquisite symphony of flavors, elegantly wrapped in the lushness of eggplants. This vegan rendition carefully selects tender baby eggplants, slicing them just enough to create pockets that are artfully stuffed with a meticulously roasted blend of spices, peanuts, and coconut, offering a tapestry of textures and flavors. Each eggplant is then pan-fried to perfection, ensuring the spices meld harmoniously within, resulting in a dish that is as nutritious as it is flavorful. Accompanied by a rich, tangy, and slightly sweet tomato-based sauce, this Bharwa Baingan recipe transcends the ordinary, delivering a vegan masterpiece that promises to delight the palate.
author: Vegor
steps: Wash and slit the baby eggplants into four, keeping the base intact.
Dry roast peanuts, coconut, cumin seeds, and coriander seeds. Grind into a fine paste with garam masala and turmeric.
Stuff the eggplants with the spice blend.
Heat oil in a pan and fry the stuffed eggplants until partially cooked.
Set aside the eggplants. In the same pan, add onions, garlic, ginger, and green chilies. Sauté until golden.
Gently place the eggplants back into the pan, cover, and cook on low heat till done.
Serve hot with flatbread or rice.

title: Vegan Aloo Tikki
description: A timeless favorite from the heart of Indian street food cuisine, this Classic Vegan Aloo Tikki recipe offers a delectable combination of spicy, tangy potatoes crafted into crisp patties. Perfectly seasoned with traditional spices and herbs, these patties are pan-fried to golden perfection, resulting in a dish that’s both comforting and bursting with flavor. Ideal as an appetizer, snack, or even as part of a main meal, this versatile recipe is designed to satisfy vegans and non-vegans alike. A fusion of simple ingredients and rich textures, our Aloo Tikki ensures a culinary experience that pays homage to its Indian roots while delivering on wellness and taste.
author: Vegor
steps: Mix mashed potatoes and peas with cumin seeds, garam masala, amchur, turmeric, coriander, green chilies, and salt.
Form the mixture into small patties. Dust each patty with chickpea flour to give a crispy coating.
Heat oil in a pan and fry the patties on medium heat until both sides are golden brown.
Serve hot with chutney or ketchup.

title: Vegan Lauki Kofta
description: This Vegan Lauki Kofta recipe offers a delightful twist on a traditional Indian dish, making it accessible for those following a dairy-free and vegan lifestyle. The koftas, or dumplings, are crafted from grated bottle gourd (lauki), mixed with besan (chickpea flour) and a melange of spices, then baked to perfection instead of the conventional frying method. The magic unfolds as these succulent koftas are then immersed in a rich, creamy tomato-based sauce, exquisitely seasoned to tantalize your taste buds. This dish is not just a testament to the versatility of vegan cuisine but also pays homage to the rich culinary tradition of India, where spices play a pivotal role in crafting flavors that resonate with the soul. Perfect for dinner parties or a comforting family meal, it promises to be a memorable experience.
author: Vegor
steps: Preheat the oven to 180°C (350°F) and line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
In a bowl, combine grated lauki, besan, garam masala, turmeric, coriander powder, and salt. Mix well to form a dough.
Form the dough into small balls and place on the prepared baking sheet. Bake for 20 minutes, or until golden brown.
For the sauce, heat oil in a pan over medium heat. Add cumin seeds, and once they start to splutter, add chopped onions. Sauté until translucent.
Add ginger-garlic paste and cook for another minute. Then, add pureed tomatoes, red chili powder, turmeric, and salt. Cook until oil separates from the masala.
Garnish with chopped cilantro and serve hot.

title: Vegan Sev Tameta
description: Vegan Sev Tameta, a delightful concoction from the heart of Gujarat, is an epitome of comfort food. This traditional recipe transforms simple ingredients into a culinary masterpiece, harmoniously blending the tanginess of tomatoes with the crunchy texture of sev. The dish is a vivid celebration of flavors, meticulously prepared to appeal to both the eye and the palate. With its rich, gravy-like base infused with a medley of spices, it offers an authentic taste of Indian cuisine while ensuring inclusivity for those following a vegan lifestyle. Each portion of this delectable curry promises a journey through the vibrant streets of Gujarat, making it a perfect choice for any meal.
author: Vegor
steps: Heat oil in a pan over medium heat.
Add mustard seeds and cumin seeds, letting them sizzle.
Stir in the chopped tomatoes, turmeric powder, coriander powder, garam masala, and salt. Cook until the tomatoes become soft and mushy.
Add water and bring the mixture to a boil. Reduce heat and simmer for 10 minutes.
Garnish with sev and cilantro just before serving. Serve hot.

title: Vegan Dhokla
description: This Classic Vegan Dhokla recipe offers a delightful way to enjoy a traditional Indian snack without any animal products. Made from fermented batter composed of rice and chickpeas, this light and spongy cake is steamed to perfection, offering a healthy yet satisfying option. Infused with a blend of spices and served with a tangy green chutney, each bite is a burst of flavors that pays homage to its Gujarati roots. Ideal for those seeking to explore plant-based versions of beloved Indian classics, this dhokla is perfect as an appetizer, snack, or even a light meal. Its fermentation process not only contributes to its unique texture but also enhances its nutritional profile, making it a wholesome choice for any time of day.
author: Vegor
ingredients: For the tempering: mustard seeds, curry leaves, sliced green chilies, and coriander leaves
steps: Blend the soaked rice with water to make a smooth paste.
Mix the rice paste with chickpea flour, semolina, lemon juice, baking soda, salt, chilies, and ginger to form a batter. Let it ferment overnight.
Pour the batter into a greased steaming tray. Steam for 20 minutes or until a toothpick comes out clean.
Prepare the tempering by heating oil and adding mustard seeds, curry leaves, and green chilies. Pour over the steamed dhokla.
Garnish with coriander leaves and serve with green chutney.

title: Vegan Besan Chilla
description: Besan Chilla, often termed as vegan omelettes, is a delightful and nutritious staple originating from the heart of Indian cuisine. Fully embracing the vegan philosophy, this recipe employs a batter made from besan (chickpea flour), which is enriched with a vibrant mix of finely chopped vegetables, savory spices, and herbs. Each chilla is pan-fried to perfection, offering a golden and crispy exterior with a tender, flavorful interior. It’s a versatile dish that expertly marries simplicity with taste, serving as an ideal choice for breakfast, brunch, or a light dinner. The rich in protein chickpea flour makes it not only a crowd-pleaser but also a nutritional powerhouse suitable for a wholesome diet. This recipe ensures a delicious way to start your day or a comforting meal to wind down with, carrying the essence of traditional Indian cooking into vegan households.
author: Vegor
steps: In a mixing bowl, combine chickpea flour, turmeric, salt, and water. Whisk to a smooth batter.
Mix in the onions, carrots, tomatoes, coriander, and green chilies (if using) to the batter.
Add cumin seeds and give the batter a final stir.
Heat a non-stick pan over medium heat and grease lightly with oil.
Pour a ladleful of the batter onto the pan and spread it evenly to form a thin circle.
Flip and cook the other side for another 1-2 minutes.
Serve hot with your choice of chutney or sauce.

title: Vegan Sabudana Khichdi
description: This Classic Vegan Sabudana Khichdi recipe brings a delightful twist to the traditional Indian dish, making it accessible for everyone while maintaining its authenticity. Sabudana, also known as tapioca pearls, is soaked to perfection and then sautéed with a blend of finely chopped tomatoes, green chilies, and potatoes, providing a hearty base. The addition of freshly roasted peanuts adds a wonderful crunch, while a squeeze of lemon juice introduces a zesty freshness that elevates the dish. Spiced with cumin seeds and curry leaves, this khichdi is a harmonious blend of flavors and textures. Its simplicity, combined with the richness of spices, makes it an ideal choice for a vegan meal that doesn’t compromise on taste or satisfaction.
author: Vegor
ingredients: 1/2 cup peanuts, roasted and coarsely ground
steps: Soak sabudana in water for 6-8 hours or overnight.
Drain the sabudana and set aside.
Heat oil in a pan and add cumin seeds. Once they splutter, add curry leaves and diced potatoes. Cook until potatoes are soft.
Add the chopped green chili and soaked sabudana. Mix well.
Cook on a low flame for 5-7 minutes, or until the sabudana pearls become translucent.
Serve hot garnished with fresh coriander.

title: Vegan Paneer Tikka
description: This Vegan Paneer Tikka brings the sizzling flavors of traditional Indian cuisine to your table without any animal products, making it a perfect treat for vegans and non-vegans alike. Crafted with homemade tofu paneer that marries the robust tastes of spices and herbs, this dish encapsulates the essence of a culinary journey through India. The tofu paneer is marinated in a tantalizing mix of yogurt made from cashews, imbued with spices, then skewered and grilled to perfection. Served with a squeeze of lemon and a sprinkle of chaat masala, this Vegan Paneer Tikka invites you to a festive and flavorful fiesta that pays homage to its Indian roots while embracing a plant-based ethos.
author: Vegor
steps: Press tofu for 30 minutes and cut into cubes.
Blend cashews, lemon juice, spices, and water to make vegan yogurt.
Marinate tofu cubes in the yogurt mix for at least 30 minutes.
Thread tofu, diced bell peppers, and onions onto skewers.
Brush skewers with oil and grill until charred.

title: Vegan Pav Bhaji
description: Experience the authentic taste of Mumbai street food with our Classic Vegan Pav Bhaji. This delightful blend of mashed vegetables cooked with aromatic spices and served with buttered pav (bread rolls) is a treat for the senses. Our vegan version ensures everyone can enjoy this beloved comfort food without compromising on flavor. The process involves simmering a variety of vegetables until tender, then mashing them together with a special pav bhaji masala to create a rich, savory mixture. Served hot with a squeeze of fresh lemon and chopped onions, this dish is not just food; it’s a cultural experience. Dive into the heart of Indian cuisine with this simple yet irresistible recipe.
author: Vegor
steps: Boil potatoes, carrots, cauliflower, and green peas until soft. Mash them together and set aside.
In a large pan, heat vegan butter. Add onions, bell pepper, garlic, and ginger. Sauté until onions are translucent.
Add tomatoes, tomato paste, pav bhaji masala, turmeric, red chili powder, and salt. Cook until oil separates.
Add mashed vegetables to the pan. Mix well and cook for 10 minutes. Add water if the mixture is too thick.
Stir in lemon juice and garnish with cilantro. Serve hot with buttered pav.

title: Vegan Gobi Manchurian
description: This Classic Vegan Gobi Manchurian is a delectable fusion of Indo-Chinese cuisine that brings together the best of both worlds. It features crispy cauliflower florets doused in a lusciously thick, sweet, and spicy sauce, making it an irresistible dish. The recipe meticulously balances flavors to create a dish that’s not only a treat to your taste buds but also aligns with a vegan lifestyle. Drawing inspiration from the traditional Gobi Manchurian, this vegan version ensures no compromise on taste while adhering to plant-based ingredients. Perfect for dinners, appetizers, or even as a snack, this dish promises a gastronomic journey through its rich textures and flavors.
author: Vegor
steps: In a bowl, mix all-purpose flour, cornflour, ginger-garlic paste, water, and salt to create a batter.
Dip cauliflower florets in the batter and deep fry until golden brown. Set aside on paper towels.
In a pan, heat 2 tablespoons of oil and saute bell peppers and green onions for a minute.
Add soy sauce, tomato ketchup, chili sauce, and vinegar to the pan and cook for another minute.
Add fried cauliflower to the sauce and toss until the florets are well coated.

title: Vegan Vada Pav
description: The classic Vegan Vada Pav is a delectable blend of spicy potato filling encased in a gram flour batter, fried to golden perfection, and tucked between soft pavlovas accompanied by zesty chutneys. This iconic Indian street food, originating from the bustling streets of Maharashtra, offers a culinary experience that is both humble yet profoundly flavorful. Crafting this vegan version ensures all the traditional tastes are savored, minus the dairy elements, to cater to a broader audience. The fusion of spices, textures, and the homemade condiments elevate the simple ingredients into a beloved snack or meal that transcends cultural and dietary barriers. With careful attention to detail, this recipe promises to deliver an authentic taste of India’s beloved street food culture, making it an irresistible treat for vegans and non-vegans alike.
author: Vegor
ingredients: 1 tablespoon oil, plus more for frying
steps: Prepare the potato filling: Heat 1 tablespoon oil in a pan. Add mustard seeds and let them splutter. Add turmeric powder, ginger-garlic paste, green chilies, and sauté for a minute. Mix in the mashed potatoes and salt. Cook for 2-3 minutes, then set aside to cool.
Make the besan batter: In a bowl, mix the chickpea flour, salt, and water to form a smooth, thick batter.
Divide the potato mixture into equal-sized balls.
Heat oil in a deep frying pan. Dip each potato ball into the besan batter, ensuring it’s well coated, and deep fry until golden brown. Drain on paper towels.
Split the pav buns halfway through and spread green, tamarind, and garlic chutney on each side. Place a fried potato ball between the buns.

title: Vegan Masala Dosa
description: The Classic Vegan Masala Dosa is a savory, crisp crepe made from fermented rice and lentil batter, filled with a spiced potato filling. This iconic South Indian dish is beloved for its complex flavors and satisfying texture. The fermentation of the batter lends a slight tanginess to the dosa, perfectly complementing the warmth of the spices within the potato masala. This recipe embraces the traditional preparation methods, while ensuring all ingredients are plant-based, making it a delightful option for vegans. The process, though time-consuming, is deeply rewarding, offering a taste of Indian culinary tradition that’s both healthful and aromatic. Perfect for a wholesome breakfast or a hearty dinner, each bite of this vegan masala dosa takes you on a flavorful journey through the spices and regions of India.
author: Vegor
steps: Rinse and soak rice, urad dal, and fenugreek seeds in water for 6-8 hours.
Drain the soaking water and grind the mixture into a smooth batter, adding water as necessary. Allow to ferment overnight in a warm place.
For the filling, boil potatoes until tender, peel and mash. Set aside.
Heat oil in a pan, add mustard seeds, and let them splutter. Add sliced onions, green chilies, and curry leaves, sautéing until onions are golden.
Mix in turmeric powder, mashed potatoes, and salt, combining well. Cook for an additional 2-3 minutes then set aside.
Place a portion of the filling inside the dosa and fold. Serve hot with coconut chutney and sambar.

title: Traditional Tomato Rasam
description: Rasam is a South Indian soup traditionally prepared using tamarind juice as a base, with the addition of tomato, chili, pepper, cumin, and other spices as seasonings. It is a vital part of meals across South India, known for its ease of digestion and health benefits, particularly in improving digestion and preventing constipation. This vegan version of Tomato Rasam captures the essence of this beloved dish while adhering to plant-based dietary preferences. The recipe is designed to be both nourishing and flavorful, combining the tanginess of tamarind and tomatoes with the heat of black pepper and the aromatic presence of mustard seeds, curry leaves, and cilantro, creating a symphony of flavors that is both invigorating and comforting.
author: Vegor
steps: 1. In a pot, combine the chopped tomatoes, tamarind paste, turmeric powder, black pepper powder, and water. Bring to a boil.
2. Reduce the heat and simmer for 10 minutes.
3. While the tomato mixture is simmering, heat oil in a separate pan. Add mustard seeds, cumin seeds, coriander seeds, curry leaves, and dry red chilies. Sauté until they start to sputter.
4. Add the seasoning to the simmering tomato mixture. Stir well.
5. Add chopped cilantro and salt to taste. Simmer for an additional 5 minutes.

title: Vegan Aloo Paratha
description: This timeless Vegan Aloo Paratha recipe brings the heart of Indian cuisine right to your table, offering a delectable and flavorful experience for vegans and those looking for dairy-free alternatives. Each paratha is stuffed with a savory mixture of mashed potatoes seasoned with spices like garam masala, cumin, and amchur (dry mango powder), delivering a burst of flavor with every bite. The dough is rolled out, filled, and then cooked to golden perfection on a tawa or skillet, achieving that enticing crispy exterior and soft, spicy interior that makes Aloo Paratha an all-time favorite. Serve hot with a dollop of vegan butter or yogurt and a side of pickles or chutney for an irresistible meal.
author: Vegor
steps: In a large bowl, combine flour and a pinch of salt. Add water gradually to form a soft dough. Knead well and let it rest.
Mix mashed potatoes with coriander, cumin, garam masala, amchur, and salt.
Divide the dough into balls. Roll each ball into a flat round, place some potato mixture in the center, and seal it.
Roll the stuffed balls gently into flat rounds.
Heat a tawa or skillet and cook each paratha with a little oil until golden brown on both sides.

title: Vegan Bhel Puri
description: This Classic Vegan Bhel Puri recipe offers a delightful excursion into the heart of Indian street food culture, entirely devoid of animal products, making it a perfect choice for vegans and anyone looking to indulge in authentic, savory flavors. The dish combines puffed rice, crunchy vegetables, and a melange of spices, drizzled with tangy tamarind sauce and green chutney to create a textural symphony in your mouth. Each bite delivers a burst of flavors, from the sweetness of the tamarind to the heat of the green chilies, making it an irresistible snack. This recipe preserves the essence of traditional Bhel Puri while ensuring it’s entirely plant-based, making it not only a treat to the taste buds but also a heart-healthy option.
author: Vegor
ingredients: Handful of sev (gram flour vermicelli) for garnish
steps: In a large bowl, mix puffed rice, tomatoes, onions, potatoes, and coriander leaves.
Drizzle green and tamarind chutneys over the mixture. Sprinkle chaat masala, red chili powder, and salt. Mix gently but thoroughly.
Serve immediately garnished with sev to retain the crunchiness of the puffed rice.

title: Vegan Methi Malai Matar
description: Vegan Methi Malai Matar is a luxurious dish that transforms the traditional North Indian curry by substituting dairy ingredients with plant-based alternatives. This recipe perfectly combines the robust flavors of fenugreek leaves (methi), peas (matar), and a creamy coconut milk sauce to deliver a sumptuous meal that caters to a vegan lifestyle without compromising on the authentic taste. The rich, creamy sauce is seasoned with a delicate blend of spices that warmly envelops the fresh green peas and aromatic fenugreek leaves, offering a symphony of flavors in every bite. It’s a culinary delight that showcases how traditional recipes can be thoughtfully adapted for a modern, plant-based diet.
author: Vegor
steps: Heat oil in a pan and add cumin seeds. Once they begin to splutter, add the chopped onions and sauté until translucent.
Add the ginger-garlic paste and sauté for another minute.
Incorporate the tomato puree and cook until the oil separates.
Add the chopped fenugreek leaves and peas, cook for 5-7 minutes.
Pour in the coconut milk, add turmeric, garam masala, and salt. Simmer on low heat for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally.

title: Quinoa Khichdi
description: Quinoa Khichdi is a nutritious, vegan twist on the traditional Indian dish that typically combines rice and lentils. This version uses quinoa for a higher protein content and a unique, nutty flavor. It’s simmered with a variety of aromatic spices such as turmeric, cumin, and mustard seeds, along with a bounty of vegetables for a wholesome, balanced meal. Perfect for those seeking a comforting, yet healthy dish, Quinoa Khichdi is easy to digest and is packed with fiber, making it an excellent choice for any mealtime.
author: Vegor
steps: Rinse and soak quinoa and lentils for 30 minutes.
Heat oil in a pot. Add mustard seeds and cumin seeds until they start to pop.
Add diced vegetables and sauté for 2-3 minutes.
Add quinoa, lentils, turmeric, salt, and water. Bring to a boil, then simmer for 20 minutes.
Garnish with cilantro before serving.

title: Vegan Gajar Halwa
description: This Classic Vegan Gajar Halwa brings a beloved Indian dessert into the plant-based realm without sacrificing any of the traditional flavors and textures. By substituting dairy with almond milk and using plant-based ghee, this recipe carefully respects the essence of the authentic dish while ensuring it’s accessible to vegan diets. Gajar Halwa, traditionally prepared during festive seasons in India, involves slow-cooking grated carrots in milk until it reaches a dense, pudding-like consistency, then flavored with cardamom, sugar, and nuts. This vegan version captures the heartwarming indulgence of the original recipe, making it a delightful treat that can be enjoyed by everyone. Perfect for celebrations or as a comforting dessert after a family dinner, this Gajar Halwa is a testament to the versatility of vegan cooking, showcasing how traditional dishes can be adapted to fit modern dietary preferences without losing their cultural significance.
author: Vegor
ingredients: A handful of chopped cashews and almonds
steps: 1. In a large pan, heat the plant-based ghee over medium heat.
2. Add the grated carrots and sauté for about 10 minutes, or until they start to soften.
3. Pour in the almond milk and add the saffron if using. Bring the mixture to a low simmer.
4. Cover the pan and allow it to cook for 25-30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the milk is mostly absorbed by the carrots.
5. Add the sugar and cardamom powder, mixing well. Cook for another 5-10 minutes, or until the halwa thickens.

title: Ultimate Vegan Dal Makhani
description: This Ultimate Vegan Dal Makhani recipe brings the traditional North Indian delicacy right to your dining table with a vegan twist. Dal Makhani, a beloved dish known for its creamy texture and aromatic spices, has been reimagined here without any dairy products, ensuring a delectable experience for everyone, including those following a vegan lifestyle. By meticulously selecting plant-based ingredients, this recipe retains the original’s essence and rich flavors, made from whole urad dal (black lentils) and kidney beans, simmered in a lusciously thick tomato-based sauce. The addition of authentic spices, along with vegan butter and cream replacements, enhances its aroma and taste, promising a savory, heartwarming meal that pays homage to its roots while embracing modern dietary preferences.
author: Vegor
steps: Rinse the lentils and kidney beans, then soak them in water for at least 8 hours or overnight.
Drain and transfer the lentils and beans to a pressure cooker, add water, and cook until they are soft.
Heat vegan butter in a pan over medium heat. Add the chopped onions and sauté until golden brown.
Add ginger-garlic paste and sauté for another minute.
Stir in the tomato puree, garam masala, cumin, and turmeric, cooking until the oil starts to separate from the mixture.
Stir in vegan cream and season with salt. Cook for another 5 minutes.
Garnish with fresh cilantro and serve hot.

title: Vegan Palak Tofu
description: This Classic Vegan Palak Tofu recipe is a delightful rendition of the traditional Indian dish known for its smooth, rich, and creamy texture, combined with the marvelous flavors of spices and herbs. The dish skillfully replaces paneer with tofu, making it a perfect vegan delight without compromising on the authentic taste and richness. Fresh spinach leaves are pureed and then simmered with meticulously marinated tofu cubes, ensuring that each bite is packed with flavor and nutrients. The inclusion of aromatic spices like garam masala, cumin, and turmeric not only imparts a signature aroma but also boosts the health benefits. Ideal for those seeking to indulge in the culinary marvels of Indian cuisine while adhering to a vegan diet, this version of Palak Tofu becomes a testament to the versatility of traditional dishes in accommodating modern dietary preferences.
author: Vegor
ingredients: 1 block firm tofu, pressed and cubed
2 cups fresh spinach, blanched and pureed
steps: Press and cube the tofu, then marinate it with a pinch of salt and turmeric powder.
Heat oil in a pan. Add cumin seeds, and once they start to splutter, add chopped onions, garlic, and ginger. Sauté until golden brown.
Add the spinach puree to the pan along with garam masala, turmeric, red chili powder, and salt. Cook for 5-7 minutes.
Add marinated tofu cubes and water. Let it simmer for 15-20 minutes on low heat, allowing the tofu to absorb the flavors.
Garnish with fresh cilantro and serve hot with naan or rice.

title: Jeera Aloo
description: Jeera Aloo, a staple in the Indian culinary world, presents a harmonious blend of potatoes spiced with cumin and other aromatic spices. This quintessentially Indian dish is known for its simplicity, yet it packs a flavorsome punch that can elevate any meal from mundane to extraordinary. The method involves sautéing boiled potatoes with cumin seeds (jeera), turmeric, and other spices, resulting in a dish that’s both comforting and capable of awakening your taste buds. Ideal for those seeking a quick yet satisfying meal, Jeera Aloo aspires to deliver not just nourishment, but a burst of traditional flavors that are deeply embedded in Indian cuisine.
author: Vegor
steps: Heat oil in a pan and add cumin seeds.
Once the seeds splutter, add the boiled cubed potatoes and stir.
Add turmeric, coriander, chili powder, and salt. Cook for 5-7 minutes on medium heat until the potatoes are golden brown.
Garnish with fresh cilantro and serve hot.

title: Vegan Kadhi Pakora
description: Vegan Kadhi Pakora is a sumptuous, dairy-free adaptation of the traditional North Indian dish. This recipe replaces yogurt with a tangy blend of plant-based yogurt and chickpea flour to achieve the Kadhi’s characteristic sour taste and thick consistency. The Pakoras are made from a spiced mixture of gram flour and vegetables, deep-fried to golden perfection, and then immersed in the simmering Kadhi. The dish is seasoned with a tantalizing tadka of mustard seeds, cumin, dried red chillies, and curry leaves, releasing a fragrant aroma that promises a feast for the senses. Whether savored as a hearty dinner or a comforting meal on a rainy day, this Vegan Kadhi Pakora embodies the essence of Indian cuisine, offering a delightful experience of flavors and textures while being entirely plant-based.
author: Vegor
ingredients: For pakoras: 1 cup chickpea flour, 1 cup water, 1/2 cup sliced onion, 1/2 cup grated potato, 1 green chili (finely chopped), 1 tsp carom seeds, Salt to taste
For tadka: 2 tbsp oil, 1/2 tsp mustard seeds, 1/2 tsp cumin seeds, 2 dried red chilies, A pinch of asafoetida, 10-12 curry leaves
steps: Whisk together plant-based yogurt, chickpea flour, turmeric, salt, and water in a bowl until smooth and set aside.
Prepare pakora batter by mixing chickpea flour, water, onion, potato, green chili, carom seeds, and salt. Heat oil and drop spoonfuls of batter to fry until golden brown.
Heat oil in a large pan for tadka. Add mustard and cumin seeds, dried red chilies, asafoetida, and curry leaves. Pour the yogurt mixture and bring to a boil. Simmer for 15-20 minutes.
Add pakoras to the Kadhi, simmer for another 5 minutes, and serve hot.

title: Vegan Dum Aloo
description: This Classic Vegan Dum Aloo recipe captures the essence of traditional Indian cuisine while being completely plant-based. By leveraging the robust flavors of tomatoes, onions, ginger, and a blend of aromatic spices, this dish offers a tantalizing experience that rivals its non-vegan counterparts. The potatoes are first boiled to perfection, then gently sautéed with the spices, and finally simmered in a rich, creamy tomato-based sauce. This recipe is designed to delight the taste buds of vegans and non-vegans alike, making it a perfect addition to any meal. Ideal for those seeking to explore vegan Indian cuisine without sacrificing flavor, this Dum Aloo is a testament to the versatility of vegan cooking.
author: Vegor
steps: Boil the potatoes until they are tender and set aside.
Heat oil in a pan over medium heat. Add onions, ginger, garlic, and green chilies. Sauté until the onions are golden.
Add the tomato puree and cook until the oil separates.
Add turmeric, cumin, coriander, and garam masala. Cook for another 2 minutes.
Add the boiled potatoes and mix well to ensure they are coated with the spices.
Garnish with fresh cilantro before serving.

title: Vegan Cacık
description: This Vegan Cacık recipe is a refreshing and flavorful twist on the traditional Turkish cucumber and yogurt dish. By substituting dairy yogurt with a plant-based alternative, this recipe remains true to the spirit of cacık while being completely vegan. The combination of cool cucumber, tangy vegan yogurt, fragrant mint, and a hint of garlic creates a delightful appetizer or side dish that’s perfect for hot summer days or as a palate cleanser during any meal. Serving it chilled enhances its refreshing qualities, making Vegan Cacık a beloved dish for vegans and anyone looking to enjoy a dairy-free version of this Mediterranean classic.
author: Vegor
ingredients: Fresh dill and mint leaves for garnish
steps: In a large mixing bowl, combine the diced cucumbers, plant-based yogurt, olive oil, chopped dill, mint, and minced garlic.
Mix well until all ingredients are thoroughly combined.
Season with salt to taste.
Chill in the refrigerator for at least 1 hour before serving.
Garnish with fresh dill and mint leaves just before serving.

title: Vegan Patatesli Gözleme
description: This Vegan Patatesli Gözleme recipe offers a delightful twist on the traditional Turkish flatbread, substituting the typical dairy ingredients with plant-based alternatives to cater to a vegan lifestyle. The soft, unleavened dough encases a savory, spicy potato filling, seasoned with a blend of herbs and spices that bring warmth and depth of flavor. Each gözleme is pan-fried to golden perfection, creating a crispy exterior with a tender, flavorful interior. Perfect for a nourishing meal or as a snack, this dish celebrates the rich culinary traditions of Turkey while embracing modern dietary preferences. Serve hot with a squeeze of lemon for an extra zing, alongside a fresh salad for a complete meal.
author: Vegor
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
steps: In a large bowl, mix flour and salt. Gradually add water and knead until smooth dough forms. Cover and let rest for 30 minutes.

Mash the boiled potatoes. In a pan, heat olive oil over medium heat and sauté onions until translucent. Add mashed potatoes, smoked paprika, cumin, red pepper flakes, salt, and pepper. Cook for 5 minutes. Set aside.
Divide the dough into 8 equal balls. On a floured surface, roll out each ball into a thin circle.
Spread the potato mixture over half of each circle, leaving a small border around the edge. Sprinkle with chopped parsley. Fold the dough over to cover the filling and press edges to seal.
Heat a non-stick pan over medium heat. Cook each gözleme until golden brown on both sides, approximately 3-4 minutes per side. Serve hot with lemon wedges.

title: Vegan Sebzeli Güveç
description: Vegan Sebzeli Güveç is a delightful Turkish vegetable stew that exemplifies the rich tapestry of flavors found in Mediterranean cuisine. This hearty dish is prepared with an assortment of vegetables, including eggplants, zucchinis, bell peppers, onions, and tomatoes, that are slow-cooked to perfection. The inclusion of aromatic herbs like bay leaves, thyme, and rosemary, along with a hint of smokey paprika, infuses the stew with a mesmerizing scent and a deeply satisfying taste. The slow-cooking process allows the vegetables to become tender, absorbing the medley of spices and creating a dish that is both comforting and nourishing. Perfect for any season, Vegan Sebzeli Güveç is an invitation to explore the robust flavors of vegan Turkish cuisine.
author: Vegor
steps: Preheat the oven to 375°F (190°C).
In a large mixing bowl, combine the eggplant, zucchinis, bell pepper, onion, and tomatoes. Toss with olive oil, smoked paprika, salt, and pepper.
Transfer the vegetable mixture to a clay pot or a deep baking dish. Sprinkle with minced garlic, bay leaf, thyme, and rosemary.
Cover with a lid or aluminum foil and bake in the preheated oven for 60 minutes, or until vegetables are tender and flavorful. Stir halfway through cooking.
Remove the bay leaf before serving. Serve hot as a main dish or a side.

title: Tahinli Piyaz
description: This Tahinli Piyaz recipe is a celebration of simplicity and wholesomeness, embodying the essence of Mediterranean cuisine. Its foundation is a hearty mix of beans, offering a high-protein base, which is then elevated by the creamy richness of tahini, providing not only depth of flavor but also a velvety texture that beautifully coats each component of the salad. The inclusion of vibrant vegetables adds a crunch and freshness that balances the nuttiness of the tahini, with a hint of garlic and lemon bringing a zesty brightness that cuts through the richness. This dish is not only a testament to the harmony of flavors but also to the nutritional powerhouse it represents, making it a perfect choice for a light yet satisfying meal. Its vegan nature ensures it’s accessible to a wide audience, promoting inclusivity in healthy dining without sacrificing taste.
author: Vegor
steps: In a large bowl, combine the cooked beans, tahini, olive oil, lemon juice, and minced garlic. Mix well to coat the beans evenly with the tahini mixture.
Add the chopped parsley and sliced red onion to the bowl. Season with salt and pepper to taste, and gently toss to combine.
Transfer the salad to a serving dish and sprinkle with paprika for garnish. Serve immediately or chill in the refrigerator before serving.

title: Turkish Patates Salatası
description: This Classic Turkish Patates Salatası (Potato Salad) offers a delectable blend of flavors that are certain to appeal to vegans and non-vegans alike. It’s a simple, yet profoundly flavorful dish that showcases the beauty of Turkish cuisine through its use of fresh herbs, tangy lemon dressing, and the earthy base of potatoes.
