Heat oil in a deep fryer or large pot to 170°C (340°F). Fry the doughnuts until golden brown, then transfer to a plate lined with paper towels to drain excess oil.
Dust with icing sugar before serving.

title: Rustic Vegan Garlic Beer Soup
description: A rustic and flavorful soup that combines the aromatic presence of garlic with the depth of beer, creating a unique and satisfying vegan dish.
author: Vegor
steps: In a large pot, heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add the minced garlic and cook until fragrant but not browned.
Pour in the beer and vegetable broth, then season with oregano, salt, and pepper. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 20 minutes.
Stir in the lemon juice just before serving.
Garnish with parsley and serve hot.

title: Smoky Vegan Beer Cheese Soup
description: Elevate your soup game with this smoky vegan beer cheese soup. A hearty and satisfying blend that perfectly combines the richness of cheese with the distinct flavor of beer.
author: Vegor
ingredients: 1 cup raw cashews, soaked for 4 hours and drained
steps: Heat the olive oil in a large pot over medium heat. Add carrot, celery, onion, and garlic, cooking until softened.
Stir in the flour, nutritional yeast, smoked paprika, and mustard powder until the vegetables are well-coated.
Slowly add the beer and vegetable broth, stirring continuously to combine. Bring to a simmer.
In a blender, combine the soaked cashews with 1 cup of the hot soup mixture. Blend until smooth and creamy.
Pour the cashew mixture back into the pot. Season with salt and pepper. Cook until the soup thickens and is heated through.

title: Vegan Beer Soup
description: A cozy and comforting classic German beer soup made vegan. Perfect for warming up during the colder months and bringing a touch of tradition to your table.
author: Vegor
ingredients: 2 tbsp cornstarch mixed with 2 tbsp water
steps: Heat the olive oil in a large pot over medium heat. Add the onion and garlic and sauté until soft.
Pour in the beer and vegetable broth, then add the thyme and sage. Season with salt and pepper. Bring to a simmer.
Mix the almond milk and cornstarch mixture together then pour into the soup. Stir well until the soup thickens slightly.
Serve hot with croutons on top.

title: Vegan Lentil Soup (Linsensuppe)
description: A hearty and comforting lentil soup, perfect for cold evenings. Packed with nutritious vegetables and spices, this vegan version of the traditional German Linsensuppe is sure to satisfy.
author: Vegor
steps: Heat the olive oil in a large pot over medium heat.
Add the onion and garlic, and sauté until translucent.
Add the carrots and celery, and cook for about 5 minutes, until they start to soften.
Stir in the lentils, vegetable broth, thyme, and bay leaf. Bring to a boil.
Reduce heat and simmer for about 35-40 minutes, or until lentils are tender.
Remove the bay leaf, and serve hot, garnished with parsley.

title: Vegan Semolina Pudding (Grießbrei)
description: A creamy and delightful vegan semolina pudding, infused with vanilla and served with fresh berries or fruit compote. An easy and comforting sweet treat that’s perfect for any season.
author: Vegor
ingredients: Fresh berries or fruit compote, for serving
steps: In a medium saucepan, bring the almond milk to a boil.
Slowly whisk in the semolina and sugar, reducing the heat to low.
Continue to whisk until the mixture thickens, about 5-10 minutes.
Remove from heat and stir in the vanilla extract.
Pour the pudding into bowls and allow to cool slightly. It will continue to thicken as it cools.

title: Vegan Potato Pancakes (Kartoffelpuffer)
description: Crispy on the outside, tender on the inside, these Vegan Potato Pancakes are a delightful treat. Grated potatoes are mixed with flour and spices, then fried to golden perfection. Serve with apple sauce or vegan sour cream for the ultimate experience.
author: Vegor
steps: Place the grated potatoes and onion in a clean cloth and squeeze out as much liquid as possible.
Transfer the mixture to a bowl. Add the flour, baking powder, salt, and pepper. Mix well to combine.
Heat a thin layer of oil in a pan over medium heat.
Scoop a heaping tablespoon of the potato mixture into the pan, flattemand cook until the edges are crispy and golden, about 4-5 minutes. Flip and cook the other side.
Repeat with the remaining mixture, adding more oil as needed.

title: Vegan Goulash
description: A hearty and comforting stew, this Vegan Goulash combines tender chunks of potatoes and carrots in a rich, smoky tomato sauce with sweet paprika. It’s a perfect warming dish for cold evenings.
author: Vegor
steps: Heat the olive oil in a large pot over medium heat. Add the onion and garlic, cooking until softened.
Add the carrots, potatoes, and bell pepper, cooking for an additional 5 minutes.
Stir in the sweet paprika, smoked paprika, and cayenne pepper, cooking for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the diced tomatoes and vegetable broth. Bring to a boil, then reduce the heat and simmer, covered, for about 50 minutes or until the vegetables are tender.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.

title: Vegan Rouladen
description: These vegan Rouladen are a plant-based twist on the classic German dish, featuring thin slices of seasoned seitan wrapped around a flavorful filling of pickles, onions, and mustard, all simmered in a rich and savory broth.
author: Vegor
ingredients: 1 tbsp cornstarch mixed with 2 tbsp water
steps: Pound seitan slices to about 1/4 inch thickness.
Spread mustard evenly over each seitan slice.
Top with a layer of onions and pickle slices, then roll up tightly and secure with toothpicks.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium heat and brown the rouladen on all sides.
Add vegetable broth to the skillet, bring to a simmer, cover, and cook for about 45 minutes, until the seitan is tender.
Stir cornstarch mixture into the broth to thicken it into a gravy.
Serve the rouladen with the gravy spooned over the top.

title: Vegan Spaetzle
description: Tender and delicious vegan spaetzle, perfect as a side dish or a base for a hearty sauce. This egg-free version captures all the comfort of the traditional German dish.
author: Vegor
steps: In a large bowl, mix the flour and salt.
Add the water, plant milk, and olive oil, and stir until a smooth batter is formed.
Let the batter rest for 10 minutes.
Bring a large pot of salt naar water to a boil.
Press the batter through a spaetzle maker or a large holed sieve or colander into the boiling water.
Toss the spaetzia with a bit of olive oil or vegan butter and serve warm.

title: Vegan Black Forest Cake (Schwarzwälder Kirschtorte)
description: A sumptuous, moist chocolate cake layered with rich cherry filling and topped with whipped coconut cream and dark chocolate shavings, making for a perfect vegan adaptation of the classic Black Forest Cake.
author: Vegor
ingredients: 2 cups fresh cherries, pitted and halved
steps: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C) and grease two 9-inch cake pans.
In a large bowl, whisk together flour, sugar, cocoa powder, baking soda, and salt.
Add non-dairy milk, vegetable oil, apple cider vinegar, and vanilla extract, and mix until smooth.
Divide batter evenly between prepared pans and bake for 25-30 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean.
Allow cakes to cool completely before assembling.
Place second cake layer on top and cover with remaining whipped coconut cream.
Decorate with fresh cherries and dark chocolate shavings.
Chill for at least an hour before serving.

title: Vegan Bratwurst
description: A delicious and hearty vegan alternative to traditional bratwurst, perfect for grilling season or any festive gathering.
author: Vegor
steps: In a bowl, mix the vital wheat gluten, nutritional yeast, smoked paprika, garlic powder, black pepper, and nutmeg.
Add the vegetable broth, soy sauce, and mustard to the dry ingredients. Stir until a dough forms.
Divide the dough into 4 equal parts and shape each into a sausage.
Wrap each sausage in foil tightly.
Steam the sausages for 40 minutes.
Serve with your favorite side or in a bun with condiments.

title: Vegan Red Cabbage (Rotkohl)
description: A hearty and flavorful side dish, this vegan red cabbage is slowly cooked with apples, vinegar, and spices, creating a perfect blend of sweet and tart that complements any festive meal.
author: Vegor
steps: In a large pot, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
Add the onions and sauté until translucent.
Add the shredded red cabbage and apples, stirring to combine.
Stir in the apple cider vinegar, brown sugar, water, bay leaves, and cloves.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Remove the bay leaves and cloves before serving.

title: Vegan Apple Strudel (Apfelstrudel)
description: This Vegan Apple Strudel is a delightful twist on the classic Austrian dessert. Featuring tender, spiced apples wrapped in a flaky, golden crust, it’s the perfect sweet treat for any celebration or cozy night in.
author: Vegor
ingredients: 4 large tart apples, peeled, cored, and thinly sliced
steps: Preheat the oven to 375°F (190°C) and line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
In a large bowl, combine the sliced apples, raisins, sugar, and cinnamon.
Roll out the puff pastry on a lightly floured surface into a large rectangle.
Sprinkle breadcrumbs evenly over the pastry, leaving a small border around the edges.
Spread the apple mixture evenly over the breadcrumbs.
Transfer the strudel to the prepared baking sheet, seam side down.
Brush the top of the strudel with melted vegan butter.
Bake in the preheated oven for 45 minutes, or until golden brown.
Let the strudel cool slightly before dusting with powdered sugar.

title: Vegan Potato Salad (Kartoffelsalat)
description: A refreshing vegan take on the classic German potato salad, tossing tender potatoes with a vibrant mustard dressing and crisp vegetables for a perfect summer side dish.
author: Vegor
ingredients: 2 pounds small potatoes, boiled until tender and sliced
steps: In a large bowl, whisk together apple cider vinegar, Dijon mustard, olive oil, salt, and pepper to create the dressing.
Add the boiled potatoes, red onion, and cucumber to the bowl. Gently toss to coat evenly with the dressing.
Refrigerate for at least 1 hour to allow flavors to meld.
Garnish with fresh parsley before serving.

title: Crispy Vegan Schnitzel
description: This crispy vegan schnitzel is a delightful plant-based take on the Austrian classic. Made with tenderized seitan and breaded with seasoned breadcrumbs, fried to golden perfection. Serve with lemon wedges and your choice of vegan side dishes for a comforting meal.
author: Vegor
steps: Pound the seitan cutlets to about 1/4 inch thickness.
Set up three shallow dishes: one with flour, one with milk mixed with mustard, and one with breadcrumbs mixed with paprika, salt, and pepper.
Dip each cutlet into the flour, then the milk mixture, and finally coat evenly with the breadcrumb mixture.
Heat oil in a frying pan over medium-high heat. Fry each cutlet for about 2-3 minutes on each side, until golden brown and crispy.
Serve hot with lemon wedges on the side.

title: Vegan Sauerkraut
description: This classic vegan sauerkraut is crisply fermented, offering a tangy addition to any meal. It’s a simple yet delicious way to add some probiotics to your diet.
author: Vegor
steps: In a large bowl, combine sliced cabbage and salt. Massage the salt into the cabbage until it starts to release liquid.
Add caraway seeds if using and mix thoroughly.
Pack the mixture into a clean, airtight jar, pressing down firmly so that the liquid covers the cabbage.
Seal the jar and let it sit at room temperature for at least 2 weeks, checking occasionally to ensure cabbage is submerged.
Once fermented, store in the refrigerator.

title: Vegan Pretzels
description: Soft, chewy, and purely vegan, these classic pretzels will satisfy your snack cravings with a perfect twist. Enjoy them warm with your favorite mustard or vegan cheese dip for a traditional German treat anytime.
author: Vegor
steps: Combine warm water, yeast, and sugar in a bowl and let sit for 5 minutes.
Add flour and salt to the yeast mixture and knead until smooth.
Cover the dough and let it rise for 1 hour.
Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C).
Bring 10 cups of water to a boil and add baking the soda.
Boil each pretzel for 30 seconds, then place on a baking sheet.
Sprinkle with coarse salt and bake for 12-15 mins until golden brown.

title: Vegan Thai Spicy Noodle Soup
description: Warm up with this fiery Vegan Thai Spicy Noodle Soup, packed full of flavors and textures. A comforting bowl filled with spicy broth, tender noodles, and loaded with vegetables, it’s a hearty meal that soothes and satisfies.
author: Vegor
steps: Cook the rice noodles according to package instructions, then drain and set aside.
Heat the oil in a large pot over medium heat. Add the onion, garlic, and ginger. Sauté for 3-4 minutes until onion is translucent.
Add the carrots and bell pepper, sautéing for another 5 minutes.
Pour in the vegetable broth, coconut milk, soy sauce, and sriracha. Bring to a simmer.
Add the mushrooms and simmer for 10 minutes.
Serve hot, garnished with cilantro and green onions.

title: Vegan Thai Jackfruit Curry
description: Aromatic Thai curry made with young jackfruit, rich coconut milk, and a blend of Thai herbs and spices, offering a delightful vegan twist to the traditional curry experience.
author: Vegor
ingredients: 2 cans young green jackfruit, drained and shredded
steps: Heat the vegetable oil in a large pan over medium heat. Add the onion and sauté until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the garlic and ginger to the pan and sauté for another 2 minutes until fragrant.
Add the shredded jackfruit to the pan along with the red and yellow bell peppers. Cook for about 5 minutes, until the vegetables start to soften.
Stir in the coconut milk, Thai green curry paste, soy sauce, and brown sugar. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat and simmer for 25 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld together.
Season with salt to taste. Add the lime juice and stir well.

title: Vegan Thai Pumpkin Soup
description: This creamy Vegan Thai Pumpkin Soup is a comforting blend of flavors and spices, perfect for warming up on chilly evenings. Infused with coconut milk and aromatic Thai spices, it’s a healthy and satisfying meal.
author: Vegor
steps: In a large pot, heat the coconut oil over medium heat.
Add the onion, garlic, and ginger. Cook until the onion is transparent.
Stir in the red curry paste and cook for 2 minutes.
Add the pumpkin puree, vegetable broth, coconut milk, and maple syrup. Bring to a simmer.
Reduce heat and cook for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Season with salt to taste, garnish with pumpkin seeds, and serve hot.

title: Vegan Thai Cashew Stir-Fry
description: A tantalizing mix of crunchy cashews, colorful vegetables, and savory sauces, this Vegan Thai Cashew Stir-Fry brings a splash of Southeast Asian flavors to your dinner table. Perfect for a quick, nutritious meal that satisfies.
author: Vegor
steps: Heat the oil in a large pan over medium heat.
Add the garlic and sauté for 1 minute.
Add the bell pepper, carrot, and broccoli. Stir-fry for 5 minutes.
Add the cashews, soy sauce, maple syrup, and chili sauce. Stir well to combine.
Cook for another 3-4 minutes until the vegetables are tender but still crisp.

title: Vegan Thai Lettuce Wraps
description: These refreshing vegan Thai lettuce wraps are packed with flavors and textures. Made with tofu and crunchy veggies, wrapped in crisp lettuce leaves and served with a spicy-sweet peanut sauce, they are perfect for a healthy and light meal.
author: Vegor
ingredients: 1 block of firm tofu, drained and crumbled
steps: Heat the olive oil in a pan over medium heat.
Add the onion and garlic, sautéing until soft.
Stir in the crumbled tofu, carrot, and bell pepper. Cook for 5 minutes.
Add the soy sauce, maple syrup, lime juice, and chili flakes. Cook for another 2 minutes.
Remove from heat, and stir in the chopped peanuts.

title: Vegan Thai Sweet and Sour Stir-Fry
description: This vibrant stir-fry combines crisp vegetables and tofu in a deliciously tangy sauce, served over fluffy rice.
author: Vegor
ingredients: 1 block firm tofu, pressed and cut into cubes
steps: Heat the vegetable oil in a large pan over medium-high heat. Add the tofu cubes and cook until golden brown on all sides. Remove from the pan and set aside.
In the same pan, add the bell peppers, onion, and garlic. Cook for 3-4 minutes, until the vegetables start to soften.
Add the pineapple chunks to the pan and cook for another minute.
In a small bowl, mix together the sweet and sour sauce, soy app, cornstarch, and water until smooth. Pour over the vegetables in the pan, add the tofu back to the pan, and stir well to combine.
Cook for another 2-3 minutes, until the sauce has thickened and everything is heated through.

title: Vegan Thai Green Papaya Salad
description: A refreshing and spicy salad, featuring shredded green papaya, carrots, cherry tomatoes, and a tangy lime dressing.
author: Vegor
ingredients: 1 medium green papaya, peeled and shredded
steps: In a large bowl, combine the shredded green papaya, carrot, and cherry tomatoes.
In a small bowl, whisk together the lime juice, soy sauce, maple syrup, and minced garlic.
Pour the dressing over the salad and toss well to combine.
Add the sliced chilies and crushed peanuts, and toss again.
Garnish with fresh cilantro before serving.

title: Vegan Thai Peanut Noodles
description: Delicious and satisfying noodles tossed with a flavorful peanut sauce and crunchy vegetables, perfect for a quick and easy meal.
author: Vegor
steps: Cook the rice noodles according to package instructions, then rinse under cold water and drain.
In a small bowl, whisk together peanut butter, soy sauce, maple syrup, lime juice, garlic, and ginger until smooth. If the sauce is too thick, add a little water to reach desired consistency.
In a large bowl, combine the cooked noodles, bell pepper, carrot, edamame, and green onions.
Pour the peanut sauce over the noodle mixture and toss well to coat.
Serve garnished with chopped peanuts, cilantro, and a drizzle of sriracha or chili sauce if desired.

title: Vegan Larb Salad
description: This refreshing and zesty Vegan Larb Salad features finely chopped vegetables and tofu, seasoned with lime juice, chili flakes, and fresh herbs. A perfect light and healthy meal for any occasion.
author: Vegor
ingredients: Cucumber slices and lime wedges for garnish
steps: In a bowl, whisk together soy sauce, lime juice, brown sugar, and chili flakes.
Heat a non-stick skillet over medium heat, add the crumbled tofu, and cook until slightly browned.
Toss the tofu in the sauce mixture and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Remove from heat and allow to cool slightly.
In a large bowl, combine tofu, shallots, mint, cilantro, green onions, roasted rice powder, and lemongrass if using.

title: Vegan Thai Pineapple Fried Rice
description: A vibrant, sweet, and spicy Thai pineapple fried rice that brings a delightful vegan twist to a classic dish. Loaded with veggies and bursting with flavors, it’s perfect for any night.
author: Vegor
ingredients: 2 cups cooked and chilled jasmine rice
Green onions and lime wedges for garnish
steps: Heat coconut oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
Add the onion, garlic, and ginger, and sauté until the onions are translucent.
Stir in the carrots and bell peppers, cook for 5 minutes.
Add the pineapple and tofu cubes, stir well.
Mix in the chilled rice, breaking any large clumps.
Stir in the cashews and cilantro, cook for an additional minute.
Serve hot, garnished with green onions and lime wedges.

title: Vegan Zucchini Fritters
description: Crispy on the outside, tender on the inside, these vegan zucchini fritters are a perfect light appetizer or snack for any occasion, especially delightful in summer.
author: Vegor
steps: Place grated zucchini in a colander, sprinkle with salt, and let sit for 10 minutes. Squeeze out excess moisture.
In a bowl, mix zucchini, onion, flour, nutritional yeast, baking powder, salt, and pepper until well combined.
Heat oil in a pan over medium heat.
Form the mixture into patties and fry until golden brown on both sides, about 5 minutes per side.
Drain on paper towels and serve hot.

title: Vegan Tiramisu
description: An indulgent and wholly plant-based version of the classic Italian dessert, featuring layers of espresso-soaked ladyfingers and a creamy, cashew-based mascarpone substitute that will satisfy vegans and non-vegans alike.
author: Vegor
steps: Blend the soaked cashews, maple syrup, vanilla extract, and almond milk until smooth to make the mascarpone substitute.
Quickly dip each ladyfinger in the espresso and layer them in a dish.
Spread half the mascarpone substitute over the ladyfingers.
Add another layer of espresso-dipped ladyfingers and top with the remaining mascarpone mixture.
Dust with cocoa powder and refrigerate for at least 4 hours, preferably overnight.

title: Tomato Basil Bruschetta
description: An easy-to-make Tomato Basil Bruschetta, featuring ripe tomatoes, fragrant basil, and a hint of garlic, all piled high on toasted bread slices. A fresh and vibrant appetizer for any occasion.
author: Vegor
steps: In a large bowl, combine tomatoes, basil, garlic, balsamic vinegar, olive oil, salt, and pepper.
Let the mixture sit for 10 minutes to marinate.
Spoon the tomato mixture generously onto toasted baguette slices.
Serve immediately and enjoy your Tomato Basil Bruschetta.

title: Vegan Risotto
description: A creamy, comforting classic Vegan Risotto made with Arborio rice and a mixture of flavorful mushrooms and peas. A perfect blend of simplicity and taste.
author: Vegor
steps: Heat olive oil in a large pan over medium heat. Add onions and garlic, sauté until soft.
Stir in the Arborio rice and cook for 2 minutes until the edges become slightly transparent.
Add 1 cup of warm vegetable broth to the rice, stirring constantly until the liquid is absorbed. Repeat with remaining broth, one cup at a time.
Once the rice is creamy and al dente, stir in the mushrooms and peas. Cook for another 5 minutes.
Remove from heat and stir in vegan parmesan, salt, and pepper.

title: Vegan Gnocchi
description: Delicious homemade vegan gnocchi made with potatoes and flour, topped with a flavorful tomato sauce and fresh herbs. A comforting and satisfying meal that’s perfect for any family gathering or a special Sunday dinner.
author: Vegor
steps: Boil the potatoes until tender, then mash them.
Mix mashed potatoes with flour, salt, and pepper to form a dough.
Roll the dough and cut into small pieces, then boil until gnocchi float to the surface.
Heat olive oil in a pan, add tomato sauce, and cooked gnocchi.
Serve garnished with fresh basil.

title: Vegan Caprese Salad
description: A fresh and colorful salad featuring juicy tomatoes, creamy vegan mozzarella, and fragrant basil, finished with a drizzle of balsamic glaze.
author: Vegor
steps: Arrange slices of tomato and vegan mozzarella alternately on a plate, slightly overlapping.
Scatter fresh basil leaves over the top.
Drizzle with balsamic glaze and olive oil.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve immediately.

title: Vegan Lasagna
description: A hearty and satisfying vegan version of the classic Italian dish, layered with rich tomato sauce, creamy béchamel, and a flavorful tofu ricotta.
author: Vegor
steps: Preheat the oven to 375°F (190°C).
Cook the lasagna noodles according to the package instructions.
Prepare the vegan ricotta and béchamel sauce.
In a baking dish, spread a layer of marinara sauce.
Add a layer of noodles, followed by spinach, ricotta, and béchamel.
Cover with foil and bake for 40 minutes. Remove foil and bake for another 20 minutes, or until the top is golden and crispy.
Let cool for 10 minutes before serving.

title: Vegan Carbonara
description: A creamy, comforting vegan carbonara that doesn’t compromise on flavor. Made with silken tofu for the carbonara sauce and crispy smoked mushrooms as a bacon substitute, this dish is perfect for a cozy night in.
author: Vegor
steps: Cook the spaghetti according to package instructions.
Blend silken tofu, nutritional yeast, turmeric, salt, and pepper until smooth to make the sauce.
Fry the smoked mushrooms and garlic until crispy.
Toss the cooked spaghetti with the sauce and smoked mushrooms.
Garnish with parsley and serve immediately.

title: Vegan Minestrone Soup
description: A warming and nutritious blend of vegetables, beans, and pasta, simmered to perfection in a rich tomato broth, making it an ideal comfort food for any occasion.
author: Vegor
ingredients: 1 cup green beans, trimmed and cut into 1/2 inch pieces
1 can (15 oz) kidney beans, drained and rinsed
1 can (15 oz) cannellini beans, drained and rinsed
steps: Heat the olive oil in a large pot over medium heat.
Add the onion, carrots, and celery, and cook until the vegetables are soft, about 5 minutes.
Stir in the garlic and cook for 1 more minute.
Add the zucchini and green beans, cooking for another 5 minutes.
Pour in the diced tomatoes and vegetable broth, then bring to a boil.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve hot, garnished with fresh parsley.

title: Vegan Pesto Pasta
description: A light and fresh homemade pesto sauce tossed with your favorite pasta for a quick and delicious meal.
author: Vegor
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
steps: Bring a large pot of salted water to boil. Cook the pasta according to package instructions. Drain and set aside.
In a food processor, combine the basil leaves, pine nuts, and garlic. Process until finely chopped.
With the processor running, slowly add the olive oil until the mixture is smooth.
Transfer the mixture to a bowl. Stir in the nutritional yeast, salt, and pepper.
Toss the pesto sauce with the cooked pasta until evenly coated.

title: Ultimate Vegan Lasagna
description: Layers of rich tomato sauce, creamy vegan béchamel, tender pasta, and a hearty veggie mixture make this lasagna the ultimate comfort food. It’s perfect for any celebration or family dinner.
author: Vegor
steps: Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C).
Cook lasagna noodles according to package instructions, then set aside.
In a large pan, heat olive oil over medium heat. Add onion and garlic, cooking until soft.
Add zucchini, eggplant, bell pepper, and cook until they begin to soften. Add spinach and cook until wilted. Remove from heat.
In a large baking dish, spread a thin layer of marinara sauce. Layer with 4 lasagna noodles, followed by half of the vegetable mix, dollops of vegan ricotta, and a layer of cashew béchamel.
Cover with foil and bake for 40 minutes. Remove foil and bake for an additional 20 minutes, until the top is golden and bubbly.
Allow to cool for 10 minutes before serving.

title: Vegan Pasta Primavera
description: A vibrant, nutritious pasta dish packed with fresh vegetables and tossed in a light, creamy sauce. Perfect for welcoming the spring or enjoying a light summer meal.
author: Vegor
steps: Cook pasta according to package instructions.
While pasta is cooking, heat olive oil in a large pan over medium heat. Add zucchini, yellow squash, carrot, bell pepper, and broccoli. Cook until vegetables are tender, about 5-7 minutes.
Add cherry tomatoes to the pan and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
In a small bowl, whisk together cashew cream, lemon juice, nutritional yeast, garlic, salt, and pepper.
Drain pasta and return to the pot. Add cooked vegetables and cashew cream sauce. Toss until everything is well combined.

title: Soft and Light Vegan Crêpes
description: These vegan crêpes are unbelievably soft and light, perfect for filling with your favorite fruits, chocolate spread, or simply sprinkled with sugar and lemon juice.
author: Vegor
ingredients: 2 tbsp melted vegan butter or oil
Vegan butter or oil for cooking
steps: In a mixing bowl, whisk together the flour, plant-based milk, melted vegan butter/oil, sugar (if using), and salt until smooth.
Heat a non-stick pan over medium heat and lightly coat with vegan butter or oil.
Pour a small amount of batter into the center of the pan, swirling to spread evenly.
Cook for 1-2 minutes, or until the edges lift from the pan; flip and cook for another minute.
Repeat with remaining batter, adding more vegan butter or oil to the pan as needed.

title: Vegan Croissants
description: Delightfully flaky and buttery croissants made entirely without dairy or animal products. Perfect for an indulgent breakfast or a treat on special occasions.
author: Vegor
ingredients: 1 cup vegan butter, cold and cubed
Soy milk and agave syrup, for glazing
steps: Combine flour, sugar, salt, and yeast in a large bowl.
Add the lukewarm plant-based milk and mix to form a dough.
Knead the dough for 5-10 minutes until elastic.
Roll out the dough into a rectangle, and distribute the cubed vegan butter across 2/3 of the surface. Fold and roll several times to incorporate layers.
Chill the dough for at least 1 hour.
Place the croissants on a baking sheet, cover, and let rise until doubled in size.
Preheat the oven to 375°F (190°C).
Brush the croissants with a mixture of soy milk and agave syrup.
Bake for 25 minutes or until golden brown.

title: Hearty Vegan French Onion Soup
description: A comforting and robust vegan twist on the classic French Onion Soup. Caramelized onions simmered in a rich and flavorful broth, topped with vegan cheese and a slice of toasted crusty bread. Perfect for warming up during the chilly seasons.
author: Vegor
steps: In a large pot, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
Add the onions and a pinch of salt, cook until caramelized, about 25-30 minutes, stirring frequently.
Add garlic and cook for another minute.
Pour in the white wine and deglaze the pot, scraping up any browned bits.
Add the vegetable broth, bay leaf, and thyme. Bring to a simmer and cook for 15 minutes.
Preheat the oven broiler.
Ladle the soup into oven-safe bowls. Place a slice of bread on top of each, then sprinkle with vegan cheese.
Broil until the cheese is melted and bubbly.
Serve hot.

title: Vegan Cauliflower Tacos
description: These vibrant and delicious tacos feature roasted cauliflower as the star ingredient. Paired with a zesty lime slaw and avocado cream, they’re a perfect meal for any day of the week.
author: Vegor
steps: Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C).
Toss cauliflower florets with olive oil, chili powder, garlic powder, and cumin. Spread on a baking sheet and roast for 20-25 minutes.
Combine cabbage with half of the lime juice, and a pinch of salt. Let it marinate.
Mash the avocado and mix with coconut yogurt and the remaining lime juice. Season with salt and pepper.
Warm tortillas according to package instructions.

title: Vegan Ratatouille
description: Savor the essence of summer with this delightful blend of zucchini, eggplant, bell peppers, and tomatoes, all simmered to perfection in a herbed tomato sauce. A heart-healthy choice that brings the vibrant flavors of the French countryside to your table.
author: Vegor
steps: Heat olive oil in a large pan over medium heat.
Add garlic and onion, sauté until soft.
Add bell peppers, zucchini, and eggplant, cook for about 10 minutes.
Stir in tomatoes, tomato paste, thyme, rosemary, salt, and pepper.
Cover and simmer on low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally.

title: Spicy Black Bean Soup
description: A hearty and flavorful vegan black bean soup that’s perfect for warming up during the colder months. Loaded with spices and vegetables, it’s a delicious meal on its own or can be served with your favorite vegan bread.
author: Vegor
ingredients: 3 cans black beans, drained and rinsed
steps: Heat olive oil in a large pot over medium heat.
Add onion, garlic, and red bell pepper. Cook until vegetables are softened, about 5 minutes.
Stir in ground cumin and chili powder, cooking for another 1 minute.
Add black beans and vegetable broth. Bring to a simmer and cook for 25 minutes.
Use an immersion blender to puree the soup to your desired consistency.
Serve hot, garnished with fresh cilantro.

title: Vegan Japanese Curry
description: A heartwarming and spicy Vegan Japanese Curry that’s perfect for a cozy night in or for celebrating special events. Packed with vegetables and an aromatic curry sauce.
author: Vegor
ingredients: 2 carrots, peeled and cut into chunks
2 potatoes, peeled and cut into chunks
steps: Heat the oil in a large pot over medium heat. Add the onion and cook until soft and translucent.
Add the carrots, potatoes, ginger, and garlic. Cook for 5 minutes.
Stir in the curry powder and cook for another minute.
Pour in the vegetable broth, soy sauce, and add the bay leaf. Bring to a boil.
Reduce to a simmer, cover, and cook for 20-25 minutes, or until the vegetables are tender.
Serve hot with cooked rice.

title: Japanese Tofu and Vegetable Curry
description: Deliciously comforting, this Tofu and Vegetable Curry combines a rich, thick sauce with the delicate flavors of Japanese cuisine. A perfect vegan dish for family celebrations.
author: Vegor
ingredients: 1 block firm tofu, pressed and cubed
steps: Heat 1 tablespoon of oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add the tofu cubes and fry until golden on all sides. Remove from the skillet and set aside.
In the same skillet, heat the remaining oil. Add the onion, carrots, and bell pepper. Cook for about 3 minutes.
Add the broccoli and cook for an additional 5 minutes, or until the vegetables are just tender.
Sprinkle the vegetables with curry powder and cornstarch. Stir to coat evenly.
Slowly stir in the vegetable broth, ensuring there are no lumps. Bring to a simmer.
Serve warm with rice.

title: Mushroom Cauliflower Vegan Steak
description: A delicious and hearty mushroom and cauliflower steak that’s perfect for a festive holiday meal. Low in calories but rich in flavor, it’s sure to be a hit.
author: Vegor
steps: Preheat the oven to 400°F (200°C).
Slice the cauliflower into 1-inch thick steaks.
In a pan, sauté mushrooms and garlic in olive oil until soft. Season with thyme, salt, and pepper.
Place cauliflower steaks on a baking sheet. Top with the mushroom mixture.
Drizzle with balsamic vinegar.
Serve warm.

title: Seitan Steak
description: Juicy and flavorful seitan steak packed with protein. Perfect for BBQs and Father’s Day celebrations. A delightful alternative that even meat lovers will enjoy.
author: Vegor
steps: In a large bowl, mix the vital wheat gluten, nutritional yeast, garlic powder, and onion powder.
In a separate bowl, whisk together vegetable broth, soy sauce, olive oil, tomato paste, and smoked paprika.
Combine the wet and dry ingredients until a dough forms. Knead for 5 minutes.
Divide the dough into 4 equal parts and shape into steaks.
Cook in a preheated skillet on medium heat for 5-6 minutes per side or until browned.

title: Vegan Vanilla Cupcakes
description: Light, fluffy, and bursting with vanilla flavor, these vegan vanilla cupcakes are the perfect treat for any celebration.
author: Vegor
steps: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C).
In a small bowl, mix together almond milk and apple cider vinegar. Set aside for 5 minutes to curdle.
In a large bowl, cream together vegan butter and sugar until light and fluffy.
Add the curdled almond milk, vanilla extract, and mix well.
Sift in the flour, baking powder, baking soda, and salt. Mix until just combined.
Bake for 20 minutes or until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean.
Allow to cool before topping with your favorite vegan frosting.

title: Vegan Chocolate Chip Cupcakes
description: Deliciously moist and perfectly sweet vegan chocolate chip cupcakes, topped with a smooth chocolate frosting. Perfect for any occasion!
author: Vegor
steps: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C).
In a large bowl, mix together the flour, sugar, baking soda, and salt.
Add the oil, vanilla extract, water, and apple cider vinegar to the dry ingredients and mix until well combined.
Fold in the vegan chocolate chips.
Fill cupcake liners about 2/3 full with batter.
Allow to cool before serving.

title: Vegan Chocolate Avocado Mousse
description: A decadent and creamy chocolate mousse made with avocados. A healthy and delicious treat that’s easy to make.
author: Vegor
steps: Blend avocados, cocoa powder, maple syrup, vanilla extract, and salt in a food processor until smooth.
Refrigerate the mousse for at least 1 hour before serving.
Serve chilled with fresh berries on top.

title: Vegan Chickpea Salad Sandwich
description: A refreshing and easy chickpea salad, perfect as a filling for sandwiches or wraps. Loaded with flavors and healthy ingredients.
author: Vegor
ingredients: Bread slices or lettuce leaves for serving
steps: In a bowl, mash the chickpeas using a fork or potato masher.
Add vegan mayonnaise, dijon mustard, red onions, celery, and pickles to the mashed chickpeas. Mix well.
Season with salt and pepper according to taste.
Serve the chickpea salad on bread slices or in lettuce leaves as a wrap.

title: Creamy Vegan Asparagus Soup
description: A lusciously creamy asparagus soup, blending the delightful flavors of fresh asparagus with coconut milk, to create a perfectly smooth and comforting soup suitable for any spring gathering.
author: Vegor

title: Lemon Pepper Asparagus
description: A light and zesty side dish that brings out the best in fresh asparagus. Seasoned with lemon and pepper, this simple recipe is a perfect complement to any meal, particularly in the spring.
author: Vegor
ingredients: The zest and juice of 1 lemon
steps: Preheat your oven to 200°C (400°F).
Toss the asparagus in olive oil, lemon zest, lemon juice, cracked black pepper, and salt.
Spread the asparagus in a single layer on a baking sheet.
Roast for 15 minutes, or until tender and slightly crispy.
Serve warm as a delightful side dish.

title: Velvety Vegan Chocolate Cheesecake
description: Indulge in a rich and creamy chocolate cheesecake that’s completely vegan. A decadent dessert that marries the luxuriousness of chocolate with the creamy texture of cheesecake, all without any animal products.
author: Vegor
steps: Crush the digestive biscuits and mix with the melted vegan butter. Press into the bottom of a springform pan and refrigerate.
Blend silk tofu, vegan cream cheese, coconut cream, sugar, cocoa powder, and vanilla extract until smooth.
Fold in the melted dark chocolate.
Pour filling over the crust and smooth the top with a spatula.
Bake at 160°C for 60 minutes or until set.

title: Vegan Vanilla Cheesecake
description: A creamy and delightful no-bake vegan vanilla cheesecake that requires minimal effort. Featuring a silky smooth filling atop a crunchy crust, it’s a guilt-free indulgence for all occasions.
author: Vegor
steps: Process dates and oats in a food processor until a crumbly mixture forms. Press firmly into the bottom of a springform pan.
Blend soaked cashews, coconut oil, lemon juice, vanilla extract, maple syrup, and salt in a blender until smooth and creamy.
Pour the filling over the crust and smooth the top. Freeze for 4 hours or until set.
Before serving, remove from freezer to thaw for 10-15 minutes. Top with fresh berries.

title: Spicy Vegan Potato Curry
description: A hearty and spicy vegan potato curry that is sure to please all curry lovers. Packed with bold flavors and nutrient-rich ingredients, it’s a perfect comfort dish for any time of the year.
author: Vegor
ingredients: 1/4 cup chopped fresh cilantro for garnish
steps: Heat the oil in a large pot over medium heat. Add onion and garlic, and cook until softened.
Stir in curry powder, cumin, and turmeric for about 2 minutes.
Add the potatoes, coconut milk, diced tomatoes with their juice, and salt. Bring to a boil, reduce heat, and simmer for 30 minutes.
Stir in peas and cook for an additional 10 minutes.
Garnish with cilantro before serving.

title: Vegan Pizza
description: A delightful classic vegan pizza topped with homemade tomato sauce, melty vegan cheese, and your choice of veggies. Perfect for any gathering or a cozy night in!
author: Vegor
steps: Preheat your oven to 475°F (245°C).
Roll out the pizza dough on a baking sheet.
Spread the tomato sauce evenly over the dough.
Top with vegan cheese, bell peppers, mushrooms, and red onions.
Drizzle olive oil over the top and sprinkle with oregano and basil.
Allow to cool for a few minutes before slicing and serving.

title: Roasted Cauliflower Steak
description: Thick slices of cauliflower roasted to perfection, making a delicious and satisfying vegan steak alternative.
author: Vegor
steps: Preheat the oven to 400°F (200°C).
Slice the cauliflower into 1-inch thick steaks, ensuring the core keeps the slices intact.
Brush each side of the cauliflower steaks with olive oil and season with salt, pepper, garlic powder, and onion powder.
Place the cauliflower steaks on a baking sheet and roast for 15 minutes. Flip, then roast for another 15 minutes or until tender and edges are golden brown.
Garnish with fresh herbs before serving.

title: Spicy Roasted Cauliflower
description: A zesty and spicy roasted cauliflower that’s perfect for any occasion. Easy to prepare and packed with flavors.
author: Vegor
steps: Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C).
Chop the cauliflower into bite-sized floret sand place them in a large bowl.
Add olive oil, cumin, papara, chili.combat powder, salt, and pepper. Toss until the cauliflower is well coated.
Spread the cauliflower on a baking sheet in a single layer.
Roast in the preheated oven for 25 minutes, or until tender and golden brown, mixing halfway through.

title: Creamy Irish Potato and Leek Soup
description: A creamy blend of potatoes, leeks, and carrots, made completely vegan, that warms you up from the inside out.
author: Vegor
ingredients: 3 leeks, white and light green parts only, cleaned and sliced
steps: In a large pot, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
Add the leeks and garlic, cooking until leeks are soft, about 5 minutes.
Add the potatoes and vegetable broth, bring to a boil, then cover, reduce heat, and simmer until potatoes are tender, about 25-30 minutes.
Using an immersion blender, blend the soup until smooth.
Stir in the coconut milk and season with salt and pepper to taste. Heat through.

title: Irish Vegetable Soup
description: A hearty and comforting traditional Irish soup made with a blend of root vegetables in a savory broth, perfect for cold evenings.
author: Vegor
steps: Heat the olive oil in a large pot over medium heat.
Add the onion, carrots, leeks, and celery. Cook for about 5 minutes until softened, stirring occasionally.
Add the diced potatoes and cook for another 2 minutes.
Pour in the vegetable broth and bring to a boil. Reduce heat to a simmer, cover, and cook for about 20 minutes until the vegetables are tender.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.

title: Vegan Dublin Coddle
description: This vegan twist on the traditional Dublin Coddle makes use of hearty plant-based ingredients for a comforting, savory meal that warms the soul, especially during the chillier seasons.
author: Vegor
ingredients: 2 tablespoons chopped fresh parsley for garnish
steps: Heat olive oil in a large pot over medium heat.
Add onions and garlic and cook until soft.
Add vegan sausages to the pot and brown slightly.
Add carrots, potatoes, vegetable broth, and thyme to the pot. Season with salt and pepper.
Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low and simmer covered for about 1 hour, or until vegetables are tender.
Serve hot, garnished with fresh parsley.

title: Roasted Cabbage Steaks
description: Crispy edges, tender in the middle, these roasted cabbage steaks are a simple and quick vegan treat.
author: Vegor
ingredients: 1 large head of cabbage, sliced into 4 thick steaks
steps: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C).
Brush both sides of each cabbage steak with olive oil and season with salt, pepper, garlic powder, and onion coverage.
Place on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Roast in the preheated oven for 25 minutes, until edges are crispy and center is tender.
Garnish with fresh herbs before serving, if desired.

title: Vegan Barmbrack
description: A festive vegan version of the traditional Irish fruit bread, perfect for any celebration.
author: Vegor
steps: Soak dried fruit in black tea overnight.
Preheat oven to 325°F (165°C).
Mix flour, sugar, baking powder, mixed spice, and salt in a large bowl.
Stir in soaked fruit along with any leftover tea and applesauce.
Pour into a greased loaf pan.
Let cool before slicing.

title: Vegan Leek and Potato Soup
description: A creamy and comforting soup, perfect for cold weather. This vegan rendition of the classic leek and potato soup is rich in flavor, nourishing, and incredibly satisfying.
author: Vegor
steps: Heat olive oil in a large pot over medium heat.
Add leeks and onion, cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add potatoes and vegetable broth, bring to a boil.
Reduce heat, cover, and simmer for 20 minutes.
Puree the soup using an immersion blender until smooth.
Serve hot.

title: Champ (Irish Mashed Potatoes)
description: A traditional Irish dish of creamy mashed potatoes blended with spring onions for a simple yet satisfying side. Often served with a pat of vegan butter melting in the center.
author: Vegor
steps: Boil the potatoes in salted water until tender, about 20 minutes. Drain and mash with almond milk and vegan butter until smooth.
Stir in the green onions and season with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve warm with an extra dollop of vegan butter on top, if desired.

title: Irish Potato Cakes
description: Delightful and crispy Irish potato cakes, perfect for a vegan breakfast or as a tasty side dish for any meal.
author: Vegor
ingredients: 1 cup all-purpose flour (plus extra for dusting)
steps: Boil the potatoes in salted water until tender. Drain and mash with vegan butter and almond milk until smooth.
Let the potato mixture cool, then stir in the flour, baking powder, salt, and pepper. Mix until a dough forms.
On a floured surface, roll out the dough to about 1/2 inch thickness and cut into rounds using a biscuit cutter.
Heat oil in a frying pan over medium heat. Fry the potato cakes until golden brown on each side, about 4 minutes per side.
Drain on paper towels before serving hot.

title: Vegan Shepherd’s Pie
description: A comforting, hearty dish with a flavorful lentil and vegetable filling, topped with creamy mashed potatoes. Perfect for chilly evenings and sharing with family and friends.
author: Vegor
steps: Preheat the oven to 400°F (200°C).
In a large pan, heat olive oil over medium heat. Add the onion and garlic, and sauté until soft.
Add the carrots, celery, and cook for 5 minutes. Stir in the lentils, vegetable broth, thyme, rosemary, and tomato paste.
Bring to a boil, then reduce the heat and simmer for 25 minutes, or until the lentils are soft.
Stir in the frozen peas and cook for another 5 minutes. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Add the vegan butter and almond milk to the potatoes and mash until smooth. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Spread the lentil mixture in an oven-proof dish. Top with the mashed potatoes and smooth with a spoon.
Bake for 25 minutes, or until the top is golden. Let it stand for 5 minutes before serving.

title: Vegan Boxty (Irish Potato Pancakes)
description: These vegan Boxty pancakes offer a delightful and hearty start to your day or can be a wonderful side dish. Made with a mix of grated and mashed potatoes and cooked to golden perfection, they’re sure to impress.
author: Vegor
ingredients: 1 1/4 cups almond milk or any plant-based milk
steps: Wrap grated potatoes in a clean cloth and squeeze to remove excess moisture.
In a large bowl, mix together the squeezed grated potato, mashed potato, flour, baking powder, and salt.
Gradually add almond milk to form a thick batter.
Heat a non-stick skillet or frying pan over medium heat and melt a small amount of vegan butter to coat the pan.
Spoon portions of the batter onto the hot pan, flattening them down to form pancakes.
Transfer to a serving plate and garnish with chives if desired. Serve warm.

title: Vegan Irish Stew
description: A hearty and traditional Irish stew filled with savory flavors, perfect for warming up on a cold day. This vegan version features tender mushrooms and root vegetables in a rich, herby broth.
author: Vegor
steps: Heat olive oil in a large pot over medium heat. Add onion and garlic, cooking until soft.
Add carrots, parsnips, mushrooms, and potatoes to the pot, stirring to combine.
Pour in vegetable broth, stout beer, tomato paste, and soy sauce. Stir in thyme and rosemary.
Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for about 45 minutes to an hour, until vegetables are tender.
Season with salt and pepper to taste. Serve hot.

title: Vegan Irish Soda Bread
description: A traditional, dense Irish bread made vegan-friendly. Perfect alongside your favorite stew or just to enjoy with a dab of vegan butter. No kneading required and remarkably simple to make.
author: Vegor
ingredients: 1 3/4 cups vegan buttermilk (mix 1¾ cups of soy or almond milk with 1 tablespoon of apple cider vinegar)
1 tablespoon maple syrup (optional for a slight sweetness)
steps: Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C).
In a large bowl, whisk together the flour, baking soda, and salt.
Make a well in the center of the flour mixture and pour in the vegan buttermilk and maple syrup if using. Stir until just combined.
On a lightly floured surface, gently knead the dough into a round loaf, then place on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Cut a cross on the top of the loaf to help it expand while baking.
Let cool on a wire rack before slicing.

title: Vegan Xiao Long Bao (Soup Dumplings)
description: These vegan Xiao Long Bao, or Chinese soup dumplings, are an authentic experience you won’t forget. The secret is in the savory mushroom broth that turns gelatinous when cooled and liquid when steamed.
author: Vegor
steps: In a saucepan, combine vegetable broth and agar-agar. Bring to a boil, then simmer until agar-agar is fully dissolved.
Add mushrooms, soy sauce, sesame oil, and green onion to the broth. Remove from heat and let cool until mixture becomes gelatinous.
Cut the gelatinous mixture into small cubes.
Place a cube of the gelatinous broth mixture and a slice of mushroom in the center of a dumpling wrapper.
Wet the edges of the wrapper with water, gather the edges above the filling, and twist to seal.
Serve hot with ginger slices and soy sauce.

title: Edamame Dumplings
description: Delicious and easy-to-make, these vegan edamame dumplings are perfect for any occasion. Filled with a tasty mixture of edamame beans, garlic, and ginger, they’re sure to impress.
author: Vegor
steps: Cook edamame according to package instructions. Drain and let cool.
In a food processor, blend edamame, garlic, ginger, soy sauce, and sesame oil until smooth.
Place a small spoonful of the filling in the center of a dumpling wrapper.
Wet the edges of the wrapper with water, fold over the filling, and press to seal.
Cook dumplings in boiling water for about 3-4 minutes or until they float to the top.

title: Vegan Chinese Chive Pockets
description: Crispy on the outside, juicy on the inside, these vegan Chinese chive pockets are filled with fragrant chives, glass noodles, and an assortment of vegetables.
author: Vegor
ingredients: 1 cup soaked and chopped glass noodles
steps: Prepare the dough by mixing flour and salt, then slowly adding boiling water until a soft dough forms. Let it rest for 30 minutes.
Mix chives, glass noodles, carrot, tofu, soy sauce, sesame oil, and salt in a bowl.
Divide the dough into small balls and roll each out into a thin circle.
Place a spoonful of the filling into the center of each circle, fold, and seal the edges.
Heat a pan with a thin layer of oil. Place the chive pockets in the pan and cook until golden brown on both sides.

title: Sweet Red Bean Paste Buns
description: Soft and fluffy buns filled with sweet red bean paste, perfect for an addition to dinner, or a snack be it in the middle of the day or late at night!
author: Vegor
steps: Dissolve yeast in warm water and set aside for 5 minutes.
In a large bowl, mix flour, sugar, and vegetable oil. Add yeast mixture and knead until smooth.
Cover dough with a damp cloth and let rise in a warm place for 1 hour.
Divide dough into small pieces and flatten each piece. Fill with a spoonful of red bean paste and shape into buns.
Place buns on a baking sheet, cover, and let rise for another 30 minutes.

title: Crystal Dumplings
description: These vegan Crystal Dumplings are beautifully translucent and filled with a flavorful mix of vegetables and tofu, making them a perfect treat for any celebration or as an appetizing starter.
author: Vegor
ingredients: 1 pack of dumpling wrappers (ensure vegan friendly)
steps: In a large bowl, mix together cabbage, carrot, mushrooms, scallions, and tofu.
Season the mixture with soy sauce, sesame oil, and ginger, then stir well to combine.
Take a dumpling wrapper, place a spoonful of the filling in the center, moisten the edges with water, fold in half, and press to seal, ensuring no air is trapped inside.
Repeat with the remaining wrappers and filling.
Steam the dumplings for about 10 minutes or until translucent.
Serve hot with a side of soy sauce for dipping.

title: Garlic Spinach Dumplings
description: Packed with the goodness of spinach and the aromatic flavor of garlic, these vegan Garlic Spinach Dumplings are an easy and delicious finger food perfect for any occasion.
author: Vegor
steps: Heat olive oil in a pan over medium heat and sauté garlic until fragrant.
Add the chopped spinach and cook until wilted. Combine the tofu, salt, and pepper into the mixture and cook for another 2 minutes.
Let the filling cool before wrapping.
Place a small portion of the filling in the center of each dumpling wrapper, moisten the edges of the wrapper with water, fold and press to seal securely.
Steam the dumplings for about 15 minutes or until the wrappers become translucent.

title: Vegan Steamed Bao Buns with Mushroom Filling
description: Fluffy and soft steamed bao buns filled with a delicious and hearty mushroom mixture, perfect for any vegan feast.
author: Vegor
steps: Prepare the dough for buns by mixing flour, sugar, yeast, and baking powder. Gradually add warm water to form a dough. Knead until smooth, then cover and let it rise.
For the filling, sauté mushrooms, soy sauce, hoisin sauce, sesame oil, green onions, and garlic until cooked.
Punch down the dough and divide into 6 equal parts. Roll each part into a ball, then flatten and fill with the mushroom mixture.
Steam the bao buns for about 20 minutes until they have expanded and are fluffy.
Serve immediately while hot.

title: Crispy Taro Croquettes
description: These crispy taro croquettes make a unique and tasty vegan treat. Made with a creamy taro filling and coated in a golden crispy layer, they’re perfect for any special occasion or as a delightful snack any time.
author: Vegor
steps: Boil the taro cubes until soft, then mash until smooth.
Season the mashed taro with salt and pepper, then let it cool.
Form the taro mixture into small balls, then freeze for 15 minutes to firm up.
Prepare three bowls for breading: one with flour, one with non-dairy milk, and one with panko breadcrumbs mixed with cornstarch.
Dip each taro ball firstly in the flour, then the non-dairy milk, and finally in the panko mixture.
Drain on paper towels and serve hot.

title: Vegan Stuffed Shiitake Mushrooms
description: Succulent shiitake mushrooms stuffed with a magical blend of walnuts, garlic, and green onions, baked to perfection. A delectable vegan delight that’s sure to impress at any gathering.
author: Vegor
steps: Preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C).
Remove stems from mushrooms and finely chop them, setting the caps aside.
In a bowl, mix together the chopped stems, walnuts, green onions, garlic, and soy sauce.
Fill each mushroom cap with the walnut mixture.
Place stuffed mushrooms on a baking sheet, drizzle with olive but oil, and season with salt and pepper.
Serve warm and enjoy!

title: Vegan Spring Rolls
description: Fresh and colorful vegan spring rolls, packed with crispy vegetables and sweet mango, all wrapped in a delicate rice paper. Accompanied by a zesty lime dipping sauce for a refreshing bite.
author: Vegor
steps: Prepare vegetables and mango.
Fill a large bowl with warm water. Dip one rice paper wrapper into the water for about 15-20 seconds until just pliable.
Lay wrapper on a clean, wet cloth and place a handful of the veggies, mango, cilantro, and mint in the center.
Fold in the sides of the wrapper and then roll tightly.
Repeat with the remaining ingredients.
Serve spring rolls immediately with the dipping sauce.

title: Vegan Rice Noodle Rolls
description: Delightfully light and filled with fresh veggies, these vegan rice noodle rolls are perfect for a quick and healthy meal. Packed with flavors and dipped in a savory sauce, they’re sure to become a favorite!
author: Vegor
ingredients: For the sauce: 2 tablespoons soy sauce, 1 tablespoon rice vinegar, 1 teaspoon sesame oil, 1 teaspoon maple syrup, 1 clove garlic, minced
steps: Cook rice noodles according to package instructions then rinse under cold water and set aside.
Prepare the vegetables and set aside.
Mix all the ingredients for the sauce in a small bowl.
Soak a rice paper wrapper in warm water for about 20 seconds until it’s soft.
Lay out the wrapper and place a small amount of noodles, carrots, cucumber, cabbage, basil, and mint in the center.
Repeat with the remaining ingredients.
Serve with the dipping sauce.

title: Vegan Sesame Balls
description: Crispy on the outside, soft and chewy on the inside, these vegan sesame balls are a delectable sweet treat. Made with a sweet red bean paste filling and coated in sesame seeds, they’re an irresistible snack or dessert.
author: Vegor
steps: Mix the glutinous rice flour, sugar, and water in a bowl to form a dough.
Divide the dough into 20 equal portions.
Flatten each piece and place a teaspoon of red bean paste in the center. Enclose the filling with the dough and roll into a ball.
Dampen the balls slightly with water and roll them in sesame seeds until fully coated.
Heat the oil in a deep fryer or deep pan to 175°C (350°F).
Remove with a slotted spoon and drain on paper towels.
Serve warm or at room temperature.

title: Vegan Shumai
description: Delicate and savory, these vegan shumai dumplings are filled with a tasty mixture of vegetables and tofu, steamed to perfection. A delightful appetizer for any occasion.
author: Vegor
steps: Mix cabbage, carrot, mushrooms, tofu, soy sauce, sesame oil, and ginger in a bowl.
Place a spoonful of filling in the center of each wonton wrapper.
Moisten the edges of the wrapper with water, fold and seal.
Steam the shumai over boiling water for about 20 minutes.
Serve hot with soy sauce for dipping.

title: Savory Vegan Turnip Cake
description: This savory vegan turnip cake, laced with aromatic mushrooms and shallots, is a classic dim sum dish reimagined. Delightfully chewy and flavorful, it’s a unique addition to any festive table.
author: Vegor
steps: Sauté mushrooms and shallots with soy sauce and sesame oil until soft.
Combine grated turnip, rice flour, five-spice powder, and sautéed mix in a bowl.
Add water gradually until you get a thick batter.
Pour the batter into a greased baking dish.
Steam for about 45 minutes or until firm and cooked through.
Serve warm with a soy sauce dipping.

title: Bamboo Fungus Rolls with Spicy Tofu Filling
description: Savory bamboo fungus rolls packed with a tantalizing spicy tofu mixture. A perfect vegan delight that tantalizes the taste buds.
author: Vegor
ingredients: 8 dried bamboo fungus, soaked and cleaned
steps: Mix crumbled tofu with carrot, green onions, soy sauce, chili sauce, ginger, salt, and pepper.
Take one bamboo fungus and open it gently. Place a spoonful of the tofu mixture in the center.
Roll the fungus over the filling tightly.
Heat oil in a pan and fry the rolls until golden brown.
Serve hot with dipping sauce.

title: Vegan Steamed Lotus Leaf Wraps
description: Delicious and aromatic, these vegan steamed lotus leaf wraps are filled with fragrant sticky rice, mushrooms, and assorted vegetables, making them a perfect dish for any festive occasion.
author: Vegor
ingredients: 2 cups of glutinous rice, soaked overnight and drained
steps: Soak the lotus leaves in warm water for 1 hour until softened, then drain and set aside.
Heat vegetable oil in a pan over medium heat. Add garlic and sauté until fragrant.
Add the mushrooms, carrot, and bell pepper. Cook for 5-7 minutes until slightly soft.
Mix in the glutinous rice, soy sauce, sesame oil, sugar, salt, and pepper. Cook for another 5 minutes.
Divide the rice mixture among the lotus leaves, folding them to enclose the filling securely.
Serve warm.

title: Vegan Pumpkin Puffs
description: These delightful vegan pumpkin puffs are light, fluffy, and packed with the warm flavors of pumpkin and spices, making them a fantastic treat for fall gatherings and holiday celebrations.
author: Vegor
steps: Preheat your oven to 375°F (190°C) and lightly grease a muffin tin.
In a large bowl, whisk together the flour, baking powder, salt, and pumpkin pie spice.
In another bowl, mix the pumpkin puree, vegetable oil, almond milk, and maple syrup until well combined.
Fold the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients until just combined, being careful not to overmix.
Spoon the batter into the prepared muffin tin, filling each cup about 3/4 full.
Let cool in the tin for 5 minutes before transferring to a wire rack to cool completely.
Serve warm or at room temperature.

title: Vegan Stuffed Mushrooms
description: These smoky vegan stuffed mushrooms are the perfect appetizer for any celebration. Packed with a mouth-watering mixture of vegan cheese, spinach, and a hint of liquid smoke, they’re guaranteed to impress!
author: Vegor
steps: Preheat the oven to 375°F (190°C) and lightly grease a baking sheet.
Remove stems from mushrooms and finely chop them. Set the caps aside.
Heat olive oil in a pan over medium heat and sauté chopped stems and garlic until soft.
In a bowl, mix the sautéed stems and garlic with spinach, vegan cream cheese, walnuts, nutritional yeast, liquid smoke, salt, and pepper.
Stuff each mushroom cap with the mixture and place on the prepared baking sheet.
Serve warm and enjoy.

title: Vegan Macaroni salad
description: This creamy, dilly vegan macaroni salad is the perfect side dish for any summer gathering, picnic, or barbeque. Packed with fresh herbs, crunchy vegetables, and a tangy dressing, it’s a crowd-pleaser that will have everyone asking for seconds.
author: Vegor
ingredients: 8 oz macaroni, cooked according to package directions and cooled
steps: In a large bowl, combine the vegan mayonnaise, apple cider vinegar, Dijon mustard, sugar, and chopped dill. Whisk until smooth.
Add the cooked and cooled macaroni to the bowl, along with the diced cucumber, red bell pepper, and sliced green onions.
Toss well to ensure the macaroni and vegetables are evenly coated with the dressing.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Chill in the refrigerator for at least 1 hour before serving to allow the flavors to meld.

title: Taquitos with Cauliflower and Black Beans
description: These vegan air-fryer taquitos are filled with a flavorful mix of cauliflower and black beans, making them a perfect snack for game night or a festive New Year’s appetizer. Crispy on the outside and tender on the inside, they’re a crowd-pleaser for all ages.
author: Vegor
ingredients: 1 cup canned black beans, rinsed and drained
steps: In a bowl, combine cauliflower, black beans, chili powder, cumin, salt, and pepper.
Microwave tortillas for 30 seconds to make them pliable.
Place about 2 tablespoons of the filling on each tortilla, sprinkle with vegan cheese, and roll tightly.
Place taquitos seam side down in the air-fryer basket.
Air-fry at 400°F for 8-10 minutes until crispy.

title: Loaded Vegan Chalupas with Chipotle Queso
description: Crispy chalupas loaded with flavorful beans, fresh veggies, and a decadent chipotle queso that’s entirely vegan! Perfect for any party or a family dinner.
author: Vegor
steps: Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C) if using chalupa shells that need to be baked.
Spread vegan refried beans on each chalupa shell.
Top with avocado slices, lettuce, tomato, and diced onion.
Generously drizzle vegan chipotle queso over each chalupa.
Garnish with chopped cilantro and serve with lime wedges on the side.

title: Crispy Air-Fryer Onion Rings
description: Dive into a plate of these crispy, air-fried onion rings, a healthier version of your favorite snack. Perfectly seasoned and golden, they’re a hit for any game night or as a special treat for Mother’s and Father’s Day celebrations.
author: Vegor
steps: Make a buttermilk by mixing almond milk and apple cider vinegar. Let sit for 5 minutes.
Separate onion slices into rings.
In a bowl, mix flour, cornstarch, garlic powder, paprika, salt, and pepper.
Dip onion rings into the almond milk mixture, then into the flour mixture, back into the almond milk, and finally into the bread crumbs.
Place in a single layer in the air-fryer basket.
Serve immediately.

title: Black Bean and Sweet Potato Quesadillas
description: Hearty and flavorful quesadillas filled with creamy sweet potatoes and protein-rich black beans. These quesadillas are the perfect quick and satisfying meal for any time of the day.
author: Vegor
ingredients: 2 medium sweet potatoes, peeled and diced
1 can (15 oz) black beans, drained and rinsed
steps: Preheat the oven to 400°F (200°C). Toss the diced sweet potatoes with olive oil, cumin, smoked paprika, salt, and pepper on a baking sheet. Roast for 15-20 minutes, or until tender and slightly caramelized.
In a mixing bowl, combine the roasted sweet potatoes, black beans, and red onion. Mash lightly with a fork, leaving some texture.
Spread the sweet potato and black bean mixture on half of each tortilla. Sprinkle vegan cheese on top, then fold the tortillas over to enclose the filling.
Heat a large skillet over medium heat. Cook the quesadillas for 2-3 minutes on each side, or until browned and crispy.
Serve the quesadillas with sliced avocado, chopped cilantro, and lime wedges on the side. Enjoy!

title: Potato Waffle Sandwiches with Herbed Tofu Cream
description: Innovative and hearty vegan potato waffle sandwiches filled with a lush herbed tofu cream. A delightful twist on breakfast that’s both satisfying and fun!
author: Vegor
ingredients: 2 tablespoons fresh herbs (dill, parsley, chives), finely chopped
steps: Combine grated potatoes, flour, baking powder, salt, and pepper in a large bowl.
Heat your waffle iron and spray with cooking spray.
Spoon potato mixture into the waffle iron and cook according to the manufacturer’s instructions until crisp and golden.
For the herbed tofu cream, blend tofu, nutritional yeast, garlic powder, onion lapowder, chopped herbs, and plant milk until smooth.
Assemble the sandwiches by spreading a generous amount of herbed tofu cream between two potato waffles.

title: Vegan Cornbread
description: A perfect vegan cornbread that’s tender and flavorful, made without dairy or eggs. Ideal to serve with chili or as a sweet snack.
author: Vegor
steps: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C).
In a large bowl, mix together flour, cornmeal, sugar, baking powder, and salt.
Add almond milk, applesauce, and vegetable oil to the dry ingredients and stir until just combined.
Pour batter into a greased 8-inch square baking dish.
Bake for 20-25 minutes or until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean.

title: Sweet Potato and Shiitake Mushroom Dumplings
description: Delightful vegan dumplings filled with sweet potato and shiitake mushrooms, perfect for a comforting meal or a festive gathering.
author: Vegor
ingredients: 1 large sweet potato, peeled and diced
steps: Steam the sweet potato until tender, then mash it in a large bowl.
In a pan, heat the sesame oil and sauté the shiitake mushrooms with soy sauce and ginger until cooked.
Combine the mushrooms with mashed sweet potato, green onions, salt, and pepper in the bowl.
Take each dumpling wrapper and place a tablespoon of the filling in the center. Wet the edges of the wrapper with water, fold in half, and press to seal.
Heat vegetable oil in a pan over medium heat. Fry the dumplings in batches until golden brown, about 2-3 minutes on each side.

title: Vegan BLTA with Carrot Bacon
description: A delicious and innovative take on the classic BLTA, using smoky carrot bacon combined with fresh lettuce, tomato, avocado, and vegan mayo on your favorite bread.
author: Vegor
ingredients: 4 large carrots, peeled and thinly sliced lengthwise
8 slices of your favorite bread, toasted
steps: Preheat the oven to 350°F (180°C).
In a bowl, mix together the soy sauce, maple syrup, olive oil, liquid smoke, and smoked papagna.
Add the carrot slices to the bowl and toss until well coated.
Spread the carrots in a single layer on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Bake for 20-25 minutes, turning halfway through, until the carrots are tender and starting to crisp.

title: Loaded Potato Skins
description: These vegan loaded potato skins are packed with flavor and perfect for any occasion. Crispy skins filled with a creamy, savory mixture, topped with vegan cheese and green onions, they’re a comforting and satisfying crowd-pleaser.
author: Vegor
ingredients: 6 medium russet potatoes, washed and dried
1/2 cup black beans, rinsed and drained
steps: Preheat your oven to 400°F (204°C). Prick potatoes with a fork and bake for 50 minutes, or until tender.
Let the potatoes cool slightly, then cut in half lengthwise and scoop out the flesh, leaving a small border of potato on the skin.
Increase the oven temperature to 450°F (232°C). Brush both sides of the potato skins with olive oil and season with salt and pepper. Place skin-side up on a baking sheet and bake for 10 minutes.
Flip the potato skins and bake for an additional 10 minutes, or until crisp.
Fill each skin with vegan cheese, black beans, and corn. Return to the oven for 5 minutes, or until the cheese has melted.

title: Tuna-less Tuna Salad Sandwich
description: An amazingly close-to-the-real-thing vegan “no-tuna” salad sandwich made from chickpeas, perfectly spiced and served with crisp lettuce on your favorite bread.
author: Vegor
ingredients: 1 (15 oz) can chickpeas, drained and rinsed
1 teaspoon nori or kelp granules (optional for a sea-like flavor)
steps: In a bowl, mash the chickpeas with a fork or potato masher until flaky in texture.
Add the vegan mayonnaise, dijon mustard, chopped red onion, chopped celery, nori or kelp granules (if using), and mix until well combined.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Assemble the sandwiches with two slices of bread, a generous helping of the “no-tuna” salad, and a lettuce leaf. Serve immediately.

title: Vegan salad with Refried Beans
description: Delight in these homemade vegan sopes topped with savory refried beans, fresh lettuce, and salsa for a perfect Mexican-inspired meal. A delightful mix of textures and flavors in every bite!
author: Vegor
steps: In a mixing bowl, combine masa harina, water, and salt to form a dough.
Divide the dough into 8 equal portions and shape each into a ball.
Press each ball into a thick disk shape using your hands.
Heat oil in a pan over medium-high heat and cook each sope for about 2-3 minutes on each side, until golden brown.
Spread vegan refried beans on top of each sope, followed by lettuce, salsa, and vegan cheese if using.

title: NO-FRY FRIED RICE
description: A healthier twist on traditional fried rice, this no-fry version is packed with colorful vegetables and flavorful spices, making it a perfect vegan delight for any meal of the day.
author: Vegor
ingredients: 1 cup frozen peas and carrots, thawed
steps: Heat the sesame oil in a large pan over medium heat.
Add the garlic and sauté until fragrant.
Increase the heat to medium-high and add the peas and carrots, corn, and cooked rice. Stir well to combine.
Pour the soy sauce, ground ginger, and black pepper over the rice mixture. Stir well and cook for 5-7 minutes, or until the rice is heated through.
Remove from heat and stir in the green onions and cilantro.

title: Ultimate Vegan Gravy
description: This rich and savory vegan gravy is the perfect topping for your mashed potatoes and holiday roasts. Made with a blend of mushrooms, aromatic vegetables, and fresh herbs, it’s a flavor-packed addition to any meal.
author: Vegor
steps: Heat the olive oil in a saucepan over medium heat.
Add the onion and garlic, and sauté until softened.
Add the mushrooms and cook until they begin to release their juices.
Stir in the soy sauce and flour, cooking for a minute to get rid of the flour taste.
Gradually add the vegetable broth, whisking continuously to prevent lumps.
Adjust seasoning if necessary and serve hot with your favorite dishes.

title: Fancy Vegan Meatballs
description: These luxurious vegan meatballs are a blend of wild mushrooms, lentils, and walnuts, giving them a hearty and umami-rich flavor. Perfect for impressing guests or treating yourself to a high-end vegan experience.
author: Vegor
steps: Finely chop the mushrooms and garlic.
In a pan, sauté mushrooms and garlic in olive oil until tender.
In a food processor, combine sautéed mushrooms, lentils, walnuts, soy sauce, smoked paprika, salt, and pepper. Pulse until well combined but not pureed.
Transfer to a bowl, mix in breadcrumbs, and form into meatballs.
Heat olive oil in a pan and fry meatballs until golden on all sides.

title: Zucchini Fritters
description: Crispy on the outside and tender on the inside, these zucchini fritters are a delightful way to enjoy summer squash. Infused with fresh herbs and garlic, they’re perfect for a light meal or a savory side dish.
author: Vegor
steps: Squeeze grated zucchini in a clean kitchen towel to remove excess moisture.
In a bowl, combine zucchini, garlic, green onions, flour, baking powder, salt, and pepper.
Heat olive oil in a pan over medium heat.
Form small patties from the mixture and fry until golden brown on both sides.
Drain on paper towels and garnish with fresh herbs before serving.

title: Vegan Queso Sauce
description: A creamy and spicy vegan queso sauce that’s perfect for dipping or drizzling over your favorite Mexican dishes. Made with nutritious cashews and flavorful nutritional yeast, this queso is not only delicious but also guilt-free.
author: Vegor
ingredients: 1 cup raw cashews, soaked for 4 hours or overnight and drained
steps: In a blender, combine the soaked and drained cashews, nutritional yeast, garlic powder, onion powder, cumin, smoked paprika, chopped carrot, and water.
Blend on high speed until the mixture is completely smooth and creamy. Depending on the power of your blender, this may take a couple of minutes.
Add the lemon juice, pickled jalapeños, and salt to the blender. Blend again to incorporate.
Taste and adjust the seasonings, adding more salt or lemon juice if needed.
Serve the queso warm or at room temperature with your favorite tortilla chips, vegetables, or drizzle over nachos. Enjoy!

title: Simple Vegan Empanadas
description: Delight in the perfect blend of crispy, golden pastry filled with a savory, spiced vegetable mixture. These simple vegan empanadas are a testament to the joy of plant-based eating, offering a mouthwatering experience that’s both satisfying and wholesome. Ideal for any season, they’re a hit at gatherings or as a comforting meal at home. Enjoy the delightful crunch, the aromatic flavors, and the warmth of a dish that’s sure to become a favorite.
author: Vegor
ingredients: 1 cup canned black beans drained and rinsed
Salt and pepper to taste 2 tbsp tomato paste
steps: In a large bowl mix the flour and salt. Add the olive oil and mix until crumbly. Gradically add cold water and knead until a smooth dough forms. Cover and let rest for 30 minutes.

Meanwhile heat a pan over medium heat. Add a bit of olive oil then sauté onions and bell peppers until soft. Add corn black beans cumin paprika salt pepper and tomato paste. Cook for 5-7 minutes then set aside to cool.
Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C).
Divide the dough into 12 equal parts. Roll each part into a circle about 5 inches in diameter.

Place a spoonful of the vegetable mixture in the center of each dough circle. Add vegan cheese if using. Fold the dough over the filling to create a half-moon shape. Seal the edges by pressing down with a fork.
Place the empanadas on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper. Brush the tops with soy milk for a golden finish if desired.
Bake for 25 minutes or until the empanadas are golden and crispy.
Let them cool slightly before serving. Enjoy your simple vegan empanadas warm!

title: Vegan Pad Thai
description: This Vegan Pad Thai brings together tender rice noodles, crispy tofu, and crunchy vegetables, all tossed in a tangy tamarind sauce that’s sure to tantalize your taste buds. Garnished with crushed peanuts, fresh cilantro, and lime wedges, every bite is a balance of flavors and textures, making it a delightful vegan twist on the Thai classic.
author: Vegor
ingredients: 1 block (14 oz) firm tofu, pressed and cut into small cubes
3 tablespoons soy sauce (or tamari for gluten-free)
steps: Prepare Noodles: Cook the rice noodles according to package instructions, usually soaking in hot water for 5-10 minutes until tender. Drain and set aside.
Fry Tofu: Heat 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil in a large pan or wok over medium-high heat. Add the tofu cubes and fry until golden and crispy on all sides. Remove from the pan and set aside.

Sauté Vegetables: In the same pan, add the remaining tablespoon of oil. Sauté the carrot, bell pepper, and green onions for about 2 minutes. Add the garlic and bean sprouts, and cook for an additional minute until fragrant but still crisp.
Combine Sauce Ingredients: In a small bowl, whisk together the tamarind paste, soy sauce, maple syrup, rice vinegar, and chili flakes. Adjust the sauce to taste.
Mix Pad Thai: Add the cooked noodles and fried tofu to the pan with the vegetables. Pour the sauce over the top and toss everything together until well coated and heated through.

Serve: Transfer the Vegan Pad Thai to serving plates. Garnish with crushed peanuts, fresh cilantro, and lime wedges. Offer extra bean sprouts, peanuts, and lime wedges on the side for added texture and flavor.

title: Spinach-Artichoke Flatbread
description: This Vegan Spinach-Artichoke Flatbread is a creamy, savory, and utterly delicious take on the classic dip, transformed into a hearty main dish or appetizer. Featuring a golden-brown crust topped with a rich and flavorful spinach-artichoke blend, it’s a guaranteed crowd-pleaser for any occasion.
author: Vegor
ingredients: 1 cup canned artichoke hearts, drained and chopped
steps: Preheat oven according to pizza crust package instructions.
In a blender, combine soaked cashews, water, nutritional yeast, lemon juice, garlic clove, and salt. Blend until smooth to create the cashew cream.
In a mixing bowl, combine the cashew cream with chopped artichoke hearts and spinach. Mix until fully incorporated.
Spread the spinach-artichoke mixture evenly over the pizza crust, leaving a small border around the edges.
Top with thinly sliced red onion and black olives.
Let cool for a couple of minutes before slicing and serving.

title: Vegan Speculoos Cookies
